observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the BAC to take immediate action to address the noted deficiencies and ensure that all lacking documents identified in the auditorial review, as enumerated in Annex A, are submitted to enable the Audit Team to establish the validity, legality and propriety of the procurement process on the implementation of the foregoing projects.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat will submit the lacking documents enumerated in the Annex A of AOM 2024-019 to establish the validity and propriety of the transaction. The management will also require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the required supporting documents are complete before affixing signature in the DVs and effecting payments.
observations.reason: Lacking documents are submitted and validated as to propriety and completeness.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management consider preparing an action plan where different strategies could be applied based on the age and nature of the collectibles and realistically project collections under the circumstances. The following collection strategies may be considered:

	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to cause notices of delinquency to be posted at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall  and in publicly accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the LGU concerned pursuant to Section 254 of RA 7160;

Advise the Chief of RPTS to seek assistance and intervention of barangay officials in the collection of delinquent accounts; and

	Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to enforce administrative and judicial actions after the expiration of the five-year collection period to all taxpayers with delinquent accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Treasurer’s Office sent a notice of delinquency to the 
concerned/ delinquent taxpayers through the assistance of the Barangay officials. Barangay officials informed their constituents the importance of timely paying the RPT to the LGU.

The management continue its massive and extensive tax campaign in the ten (10) barangays of Santo Tomas, especially those barangays with high records of delinquency. Constituents were informed how important their role as partner of the LGU in delivering public service by paying the right amount of tax.
observations.reason:
List of Delinquent Taxpayers were already posted at the main entrance of the municipality.

Implemented
Request letters informing the barangay about the municipality's Tax Campaign were submitted to the auditor.

Not Implemented
A similar deficiency was observed in the municipality's 2024 transactions.

Revised in Part II of this 2024 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
Supply Officer be required that stock cards be maintained and keep up-to-date for each item particularly drugs and medicines as well as Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies;

Inventory Committee conducts physical count of inventory of supplies to properly matched with accounting records and property records; and

Municipal Accounting Office records all purchases to its corresponding inventory account classification and to adjust this account for whatever consumption on the basis of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), duly supported with the original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS).
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the Audit Recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a)	Assign an official or employee to conduct pre-inspection of the properties subject for repair and post-inspection of the work accomplished and submit the corresponding evaluation or inspection reports thereof, and

b) Ensure that all procurements for repairs and maintenance are supported with complete documentation to prove the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions. It is emphasized that completeness of documentation includes not only the submission of the required documents but also the completeness of the
 
details thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the Audit Recommendations

observations.recommendation:

a. Require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to provide and to take full responsibility  of the preparation of the required reports and strictly observe the timely submission of the same to the Audit Team;
b) Advice concerned officials to religiously adopt and comply with the full disclosure policy of the state on matters of public interest as clearly professed and embodied in the Constitution; and

observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the Audit Recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management cause the immediate submission of all purchase orders and perfected contracts with complete supporting documents which are already delayed/overdue to the Office of the Auditor for us to expedite the conduct of audit, contract review and request for inspection of completed projects.

(a) require the GAD Focal Point Person (GFPP) (i) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), work on the complete establishment of a GAD database and the maintenance thereof for a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; (ii) amend the GPB to match the pre-prepared GPB with the approved appropriation in the annual budget for the budget year, subject to the review and approval of the DILG; and (iii) closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects embodied in the approved GPB by the DILG to ensure full attainment of the GAD targets/objectives, intervene immediately if delays in the implementation are observed, and amend the GPB, if warranted; and
(b) require the Budget Officer, during the budget deliberation make sure that at least 5% of the total appropriations for the year is budgeted or attributed for the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to: prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Register/Taxpayer’s index card, showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and furnish the City Accountant the duly certified copy of the taxpayers, to be basis of the establishing/setting-up of the correct receivables at the beginning of the year in compliance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Submitted JEV Nos. 300-2024-03-00139 and 300-2024-03-00140 both dated March 5, 2024 to correct the balances of affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Disaster Risk Management Officer (CDRRMO) to: a. submit within 60 days from receipt thereof the City’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P76,559,693.75, consisting of 70 percent MF and 30 percent Quick Response Fund (QRF) of P53,804,693.75 and P22,755,000.00, respectively; and b. evaluate the feasibility of planned Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) including the total capital outlay amount of project out of disaster fund available and which portion of are Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) in nature as basis of the amount to be transferred as unspent balance of disaster fund to trust liability at year end in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The City’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRMF was submitted to the Audit Team. The CDRRMO evaluated the feasibility of planned PPAs including the total amount of disaster fund available and implemented accordingly. The City Accountant prepared in May 6, 2024 JEV No. 300-2024-02-00183 to recognize the Capital transfer of CO five percent Calamity Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Agriculturist to coordinate with the City Planning and Development Office and other concerned department for the identification of potential barangay beneficiaries of the fund for incorporation in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) as basis for its implementation; and b. require the City Social Welfare and Development Officer and the City Accountant to submit the duly signed copy of the MOA or Trust Agreement pertaining to funds received from PCSO as basis of the implementation. If none, exert efforts and coordinate with PCSO to facilitate the signing of MOA or Trust Agreement.
observations.managementAction: The City’s plan for the implementation was submitted. The City Social Works and Development Officer and the City Development Officer already inquired with the PCSO for the copy of MOA, however the PCSO stated that there was no MOA for the funds given to the City as it is their mandate to provide financial assistance through the Local Government Units (LGUs) to qualified beneficiaries of their programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Development Council, headed by the City Mayor assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects and include development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1.
observations.managementAction: Three projects funded out of the 20 percent DF were already completed, as follows: 1. Construction/Rehabilitation of Road with Drainage at San Bartolome - 04/05/24; 2. Rehabilitation/Improvement Climate-Resilient Road at San Fernando- San Luis - 02/12/24; 3. Rehabilitation/Improvement Climate- Resilient Road at San Antonio - 03/14/24. For the Construction of Ospital ng Lungsod ng Sto. Tomas at Barangay San Miguel the fund was re-aligned to other project because of the granted financial assistance from Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) that will be used for this priority project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City General Services Office (GSO) to require the present and accountable officers to identify the enumerated items not found during Technical Audit Specialist (TAS) inspection and request for the re-inspection, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The City GSO confirmed that the items will be re-inspected and re-evaluation will be requested from COA TAS. Reinspection and reevaluation is expected to be completed until the end of 2nd quarter of 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Acting City Treasurer to: a. issue separate Accountable Forms for each collector, to prevent transfer of Accountable Forms between AOs; and b. monitor the issuances of Official Receipts by each Collecting Officers and ensure that the Official Receipts used by Collecting Officers correspond to those issued by the Treasurer’s Office through review of Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) prepared by each collector.
observations.managementAction: Separate Accountable Forms are now being issued to each AO. Strict monitoring of issuance and issuance of Official Receipt and issuance of Accountable Forms is now in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Immediately after the conduct of complete physical count, reconcile inventory results with property and accounting records; b. Ensure that all counted items are properly specified and identified; c. Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and accounting records as well as the amount of PPE items, if any, without available supporting documents; and d. The Inventory Committee or a newly created Inventory Committee to study and implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 on one-time cleansing of PPE and consider its implementation to account for the non-existing PPEs and those PPEs that lost criteria to be recognized as PPE and to present reliable balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Inventory taking of PPE items were already performed. Inventory items were counted and reconciliation actions taken. The Inventory Committee, together with GSO and Accounting prepared and submitted the necessary documents. Reconciliation is being performed between the Accounting and GSO. The City is still in the process of reconciliation of RPCPPE with accounting records. The City is still in the process of reconciliation and one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Register/Taxpayer’s index card, showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and furnish the City Accountant the duly certified copy of the taxpayers, to be basis of establishing/setting-up of the correct receivables at the beginning of the year in compliance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: The Certified List of Taxpayers was prepared by the City Treasurer’s Account Office. Certified List of Taxpayers was already furnished by the City Treasurer’s Office to City Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The members of the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) update the CLUP ahead of the review in CY 2023; b. The members of the LDC chaired by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) facilitate the preparation and update of the CDP consistent with the guidelines in the preparation of the CDP to prevent wastage of funds and ensure that PPAs implemented under the 20 percent Development are truly the inputs from the CDP towards the realization of the City’s development targets.
observations.managementAction: Initial CDP was prepared by the City and the draft CLUP was submitted to the Provincial Land Use CDP Committee for Comments and deliberation. CDP shall be finalized after approval of final CLUP. The City submitted the draft CLUP and CDP. The LDC facilitated the implementation. The CLUP revisions were addressed; the City is waiting for the deliberation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CDRRMO to promptly comply the required annual preparation and updating of the LDRRMFIP with defined projects or activities, and strictly comply with the requirement before the release and use of the unexpended previous year’s DRRM balance accruing to the special trust fund.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO complied and submitted the required documentation and updated LDRRMFIP. The City implemented required compliance and revised the plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CDRRMO to judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund based on LDRRMFIP and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the City regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk management.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO implemented actions to maximize utilization of the LDRRMF and accelerated implementation to achieve disaster relief and risk reduction goals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. City engineer to ensure that repair, rectification and correction of the noted deficiencies will be completed by the contractor and submit to the Audit Team the status of compliance for submission to the COA TAS, otherwise, refund the noted deficiencies and defects totaling P603,155.20 plus liquidated damages, if warranted; and b. City Accountant to deduct from the claims of the concerned contractors the deficiencies noted if no action was undertaken pursuant to 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer already submitted additional re-inspection and re-evaluation documents and computations for the noted deficiencies and excessive contract cost to COA Technical Audit Division (TAD). The City Engineer submitted additional documents and re-evaluation to be forwarded to COA TAD. The City Accountant is awaiting results of re-evaluation to deduct from contractors' claims if warranted.
observations.reason: The observations will be subjected to additional re-inspection and re-evaluation by COA TAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City observe guidelines provided in: Section C.1 (5) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA to effectively plan and implement GAD PPAs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The City complied and implemented GAD programs. The GFPS implemented GAD PPAs and included measures to ensure compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. City Accountant and City GSO to evaluate causes of material discrepancy of P289,569,333.25 to ascertain fairness and reliability of PPE balances in the financial statements; b. City GSO together with the Inventory Committee to adopt COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of disposal and reconciliation; c. City GSO prepare Report on Local Road Networks, Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and their components and furnish concerned offices for reconciliation; d. require the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to initiate/facilitate the disposal of the subject unserviceable properties and to generate additional income from disposal.
observations.managementAction: The City initiated validation and reconciliation activities. The City GSO and Accounting are performing reconciliations and preparing required reports. The City GSO started the preparation of Report on Local Road Network and submitted the Road Map. The City reorganized Inventory and Disposal Committee and planned disposal actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to seek the assistance of the GSO to determine the applicable fair values of the unrecorded motor vehicles and record them whether serviceable or unserviceable, in compliance with IPSAS 17 and 23 for proper presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor clarified that there are only 129 parcels of land owned by the City. For the donation and motor vehicles, the City coordinated with GSO and Accounting to determine fair values. The City is processing recognition of the donated vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The City GSO to prepare the IIRUP for all identified unserviceable properties, and initiate the process of disposing the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386; b. strictly follow the prescribed rules and procedures in the divestment or disposal of property and other assets as provided under COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.
observations.managementAction: The Renewal of PAR, Acknowledgment Receipt Equipment, and control procedures were implemented during physical count. The City GSO prepared the IIRUP for the demolished properties and submitted photographs and Waste Material Report; the items were revised and approved by Regional Director on November 11, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. prepare the IIIRUP for all identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the same; b. strictly follow the prescribed procedures and renew PAR and regularly during the conduct of physical count.
observations.managementAction: The City prepared the IIRUP for identified unserviceable properties and initiated disposal. Renewal of PAR and inventory reconciliation were implemented and photos submitted. Items were revised and approved by Regional Director on November 11, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GSO and other concerned officials to prioritize the registration and transfer of titles of the 157 parcels of land, and undertake appropriate actions to secure Certificates of Title from Land Registration Authority (LRA).
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor disclosed status: of the 157 parcels only 129 parcels are City-owned; eight of the 129 parcels have title, 100 are not applicable to titling, five are in process of titling (three out of five are ongoing process of application) and 16 parcels are intended for donation to various schools. Required actions for titling were in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer prepare the certified list and the City Accountant make detailed evaluation and tracing of the noted unreconciled beginning balances to reflect the reliable balances of these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made based on data provided by the City Treasurer. The City Accountant made detailed evaluation and tracing of the noted unreconciled beginning balances to reflect reliable balances presented in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. City Accountant and City GSO to ascertain fairness and reliability of PPE balances; b. City GSO to conduct complete physical count, reconcile results with accounting records and submit to Audit Team; c. City Accountant to make detailed tracing of unreconciled beginning balances to reflect reliable balances; d. The City to ensure disclosure and reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee performed physical counts and submitted RPCPPE; reconciliation between Accounting and GSO being performed. Adjustments and reconciliations are in progress and some items were revised and approved by Regional Director on November 11, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor instruct City Assessor to facilitate assessment and identification for titling.
observations.managementAction: Of the 157 parcels only 129 confirmed City-owned; eight already titled, 100 not applicable to titling, five are in process, 16 intended for donation. Appropriate actions initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor instruct City Accountant to maintain and update SLC for each category and GSO to regularly maintain SCs and PCs and reconcile records.
observations.managementAction: Records and subsidiary ledgers (SLs) maintained; reconciliation performed. Both departments started maintaining required inventory records. Reconciliation is being performed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor instruct City Assessor to undertake appropriate action to secure Certificates of Title from Registry of Deeds.
observations.managementAction: Status disclosed: certificates for some lots with Registry of Deeds; others in process; documentation handled by Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City Mayor require City Agriculturist and other concerned City officials to monitor the City's dispersed animals and submit progress and reconciliation with Accounting records; and ensure supporting documents are submitted.
observations.managementAction: City Veterinary Office started monitoring dispersal program. Cattle dispersed to different farmers of each barangay. Supporting documents submitted to Accounting Office for verification of implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to initiate/facilitate immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties and not to expose these properties to further deterioration or loss.
observations.managementAction: The City prepared IIRUP and initiated disposal processes; items were revised and approved by Regional Director on November 11, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor assign payroll preparation to HRMO and require verification by Accounting Office to validate payrolls and ensure net take home pay will not be lower than P5,000.00 and monitor payroll deductions.
observations.managementAction: Payrolls are handled by HRMO; verification procedures implemented; HRMO will institute additional measures such as evaluation of net pay before approval of prospective loans.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor direct CDRRMO to utilize LDRRMF judiciously and accelerate implementation to achieve disaster relief, recovery and risk reduction targets.
observations.managementAction: The City complied, updated LDRRMFIP and implemented PPAs aiming disaster relief. Documentation updated and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor aim to reduce unnecessary delays in project implementation and submit complete monthly/quarterly statements on fund utilization to oversight bodies.
observations.managementAction: The City submitted monthly/quarterly statements and implemented funded projects. Documentation and reporting improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City GSO prepare Report on Local Road Networks; prepare Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards and reconcile with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: City GSO prepared Report on Local Road Networks; Road Map submitted; components provided to Assessor’s Office. Reconciliation in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City GSO prepare Report on Local Road Networks, Road Map and keep complete Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and components; City Assessor to facilitate assessment.
observations.managementAction: City GSO started preparation of Report on Local Road Network and Road Map which were submitted. Cost components provided to Assessor and Accounting for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Treasurer exert additional effort to trace and identify delinquent accounts and City Accountant maintain ledgers for RPT and SET Receivables to validate balances.
observations.managementAction: Collection efforts were exerted; City collected amounts and Treasurer committed further actions. City Accountant started maintaining ledgers and validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City make inventories, maintain SLCs and reconcile records.
observations.managementAction: Both Accounting and GSO started maintaining SLCs and PCs; reconciliation performed; adjustments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor instruct City Assessor to facilitate titling.
observations.managementAction: Titles processed: some titles secured; others in process; documents with Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended monitoring and submission of inventory and status reports; reconciliation with Accounting.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring of dispersal program is strictly performed by Barangay Animal Health Workers and City Veterinary Office. Supporting documents submitted to Accounting for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Chairman of Disposal Committee to initiate disposal per procedures to avoid further deterioration and loss.
observations.managementAction: City prepared IIRUP; Disposal Committee reorganized and scheduled disposals; process in progress and approved by Regional Director on November 11, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended assign payroll preparation to HRMO and require verification by Accounting; institute controls ensuring net take-home pay not less than P5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Payrolls handled by HRMO; verification to ensure payroll accuracy implemented; HRMO to institute additional measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Mayor direct CDRRMO to use LDRRMF properly and implement projects to meet disaster relief and reduction goals.
observations.managementAction: The City updated LDRRMFIP and implemented PPAs. Documentation improved and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended reduce delays, expedite implementation and submit complete monthly/quarterly statements on fund utilization and status.
observations.managementAction: The City submitted required statements and implemented projects; reporting to oversight bodies improved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Bids and Awards
Committee to be updated on
issuances in order to comply
with the regulations and avoid
sanctions;
b. Municipal Accountant in
collaboration with the BAC
Secretariat, to submit
immediately the lacking
documentary requirements,
and thereafter, ensure the
submission of complete
documentation for all
transactions; and
c. The officer-in-charge to
process immediately the
transfer of the OR-CR of the
dump truck.
observations.managementAction:
Already acquainted with the latest DBM Revised circular.
Lacking documentary requirements were already submitted to the Audit Team.
Transfer is in process; awaiting the release of documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant
together with the BAC to
facilitate the immediate
submission of the pertinent
documents for audit in
adherence to Section 4(6) of
PD 1445 and COA Circular
No. 2012-001 to substantiate
the legality, validity and
propriety of the said
transactions; and
b. Municipal Accountant to
exercise utmost prudence
and diligence in her duties
and responsibilities in the
verification of the
completeness of the
documentary requirements
for all transactions of the
Municipality before
payments are made.
observations.managementAction:
Pertinent and lacking documentary requirements were submitted to the Audit Team.
Results of the audit for CY2023 revealed that there was still processed/paid transactions despite incomplete documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the
MPDC and Local Development
Council to consider revisiting
the Annual Investment Plan
(AIP) of the Municipality for
the past years and determining
the barangays that least
benefited from funding for the
implementation of PPAs, and
thereafter, consider the
inclusion of the proposed
projects of such barangays in
the next budget year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the MPDC and LDC to prioritize barangays to be included in the PPA for the next budgeting year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor, in
coordination with the Human
Resource Office:
1. revisit and study the
organizational structure of
the municipality, particularly
the staffing pattern of all its
offices, and consider abolishing
items not considered vital,
specifically those that have
been left vacant for a long
period; and
2. fill up all vacant positions
that are still considered vital
by hiring new employees or
expediting the promotion of
deserving job-order absorbed by
the LGU in permanent status.
observations.managementAction:
Organizational structure was revisited and some vital positions were posted in the CSC web portal.
Deserving rank in file employees were promoted and some Job Orders were absorbed by the LGU in permanent status.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the:
e. Port Manager to direct his
staff to prepare the necessary
forms and reports in
compliance with Section 5 of
the Ordinance, and ask the
assistance of the Municipal
Accountant in the cost
estimation of its utility
expenses and preparation of
Statement of Financial
Performance of the Port.
observations.managementAction: All necessary forms were prepared by the designated Port Manager together with his staff.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. trace the remaining
variance of P1,274,526.22
between the balances of the
accounts Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other
Funds, and effect the
necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts through
the preparation of Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) duly
supported with documents,
explanations and/or
computations in order to
reflect the correct balances of
accounts in the financial
statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is already making every effort to locate various documents and necessary records to determine previous years’ records to reconcile the balances stated between Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds. Moreover, the previous Accountant has not turned-over records/files pertaining thereto.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
the Municipality revert/refund
the amount of ₱233,415.04
pertaining to the penalties
incurred due to late payment of
loan amortizations and will be
subject for re-appropriation to
finance development projects.
observations.managementAction: The penalties due to late payment of loan amortizations amounting to ₱233,415.04 was fully settled because this was deducted from the TLB of Mr. Ventenilla prior to his retirement.

observations.recommendation:
We also recommended that
the Municipal Mayor ensure that
the 20% Development Fund be
optimally utilized for the
attainment of the desirable
socio-economic development
and environmental outcomes
for the benefit of its
constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Planning and the Budget Office to reprogram the 20% Development Fund that were disallowed by the PGLU during the budget review to utilize the said funds for the benefit of all Tomasians.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to request authority to write off dormant receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016. Ensure that the necessary documents to support the said request are properly provided.
observations.managementAction: The management plans to coordinate and inquire with the previous accountants and treasurers of the municipality to provide the needed justifications and proof that all possible means have been exhausted to collect the stated amount.
observations.reason: The management will look for the earliest record available to satisfy the documents needed for write-off of the said dormant receivables

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the BAC to:

Discontinue immediately the practice of procuring supplies and/or items from non-PhilGEPS-registered suppliers.
Ensure that only qualified suppliers are requested to tender their price quotations and/or awarded contracts in compliance with Section 8.5.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and Section J of the Guidelines on Shopping and Small Value Procurement.
observations.managementAction:
The management communicated to the LGU’s usual supplier the need to register in PhilGEPS in order for them to continuously transact with our LGU. 

For future procurement of the municipality, the management will transact only with PhilGEPS Registered Suppliers to ensure that the contracts are awarded only to qualified suppliers.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that the Head of the Agency require the BAC Chairperson through the BAC Secretariat to:

Prepare all the documents required under each stage of the procurement on time to ensure that all documents are completed at the end of the procurement process.

Submit copies of perfected contracts, approved POs, and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period of five (5) working days pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 for a timely review and evaluation thereof. Administrative disciplinary action, as provided in Section 4 of the same circular against the persons responsible, be imposed upon unjustified failure to comply.
observations.managementAction: The management will assign personnel who will assist the concerned/accountable officer in performing the needed task. This will aid to lessen, if full compliance is not possible, the number of days delayed in submission of copies of perfected contracts, approved purchase orders and its supporting documents.
observations.reason: Problem can be traced to the shortage of manpower of the local government unit where the concerned personnel were assigned multiple designations that hinders him to perform and submit the necessary documents on the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to ensure that:

No disbursement voucher shall be paid unless properly documented and signed.

All financial transaction documents from the Treasury Office are promptly turned over to the Accounting Office for timely recording of transactions, which contributes to the timely submission of accounts to COA.
observations.managementAction: The management advise the Municipal Treasurer to devise a plan to expedite and quicken the release of checks without compromising its proper documentation.
observations.reason: The usual problem encountered by the Treasurer’s Office that hinders the management in the timely submission of accounts is the late collection of LGU suppliers/creditors of their check payment from the municipality. Despite of the constant follow up and notice from the Treasurer’s Office that their checks were already available for release, they still collected it on their most convenient time.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management consider preparing an action plan where different strategies could be applied based on the age and nature of the collectibles and realistically project collections under the circumstances. The following collection strategies may be considered:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to cause notices of delinquency to be posted at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in publicly accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the LGU concerned pursuant to Section 254 of RA 7160;

Advise the Chief of RPTS to seek assistance and intervention of barangay officials in the collection of delinquent accounts; and

Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to enforce administrative and judicial actions after the expiration of the five-year collection period to all taxpayers with delinquent accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Treasurer’s Office sent a notice of delinquency to the concerned/delinquent taxpayers through the assistance of the Barangay officials. Barangay officials informed their constituents the importance of timely paying the real property taxes to the LGU.

The management continue its massive and extensive tax campaign in the ten (10) barangays of Santo Tomas, especially those barangays with high records of delinquency. Constituents were informed how important their role as partner of the LGU in delivering public service by paying the right amount of tax.

Management ensured that all possible actions shall be exhausted before resorting to the enforcement of administrative and judicial actions to the taxpayers with delinquent accounts.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Engineering Office in determining the actual
completion dates of the LRNs recorded at historical cost,
amounting to ₱200,222,554.17; and
b. compute the depreciation expense, the accumulated depreciation,
and the net book value as of December 31, 2023, and reflect the
same in the financial statements.

2.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Municipal
Treasurer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit the Deeds of Donation evidencing the distribution of the 26
hand tractors and 2 water pumps;
b. use the RIS as a supporting document in the distribution of
supplies;
c. provide all the necessary details of the distribution;
d. prepare required documents and maintain updated inventory
records based on the receipt, acceptance, and issuance of inventory
items as prescribed by the Manual;
e. strictly follow the procedures set forth in the Manual; and
f. conduct semestral (or oftener depending on the discretion of
management) physical count of inventory items to be reported in
the RPCI which should be submitted to the Audit Team’s Office.

2.14 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to record the following adjusting journal
entries:
Debit Credit
Agricultural and Marine Supplies for 322,600.00
Distribution
Donations 182,600.00
Property and Equipment for Distribution 140,000.00

3.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to
stop the practice of withholding business taxes from payments to
suppliers since these two are different and distinct from one
another;
b. The BPLO to send Notices of Assessment to the business address of
the contractors; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to:
   i. record the advance collection of business tax as credit to Other
   Deferred Credits account; and
   ii. make the following adjusting entry:
   Business Tax ₱246,136.86
   Other Deferred Credits ₱246,136.86

We have recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to effect immediately the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts amounting to ₱16,578.25 for a fair presentation of balance of the cash in bank and the liability accounts in the financial statements.
Below are the suggested pro-forma adjusting entries:
For Check cancellation:
Cash in Bank-LCCA			xx
Accounts Payable			xx
For Replacement:
Accounts Payable			xx
Cash in Bank-LCCA			xx
Direct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the list of outstanding checks in the preparation of the monthly bank reconciliation statements to determine immediately all checks that become stale to ensure a timely adjustment in the books of accounts.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to be guided by Section 59, Volume 1 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs) relative to the treatment of spoiled and stale checks.

We have recommended that management undertake the following courses of action:
Require the department heads who are part of the Inventory Committee to designate capable alternates to conduct the physical inventory of road networks.
Require the Municipal Engineer to spearhead the physical inventory of the road networks because of his technical knowledge and skills in engineering.
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Require the General Services Officer or its equivalent to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network using the prescribed format as mentioned in the above circular and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system.

We have recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer (Acting GSO) to prepare a duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to report all identified unserviceable properties of the LGU.
Direct the Disposal/Appraisal Committee headed by the Municipal Treasurer to conduct and manage the disposal process which includes the preparation of appraisal reports and other required documents to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth under Section 379 of RA No. 7160, Section 167 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to establish property, plant, and equipment balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to request authority to write off dormant receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016. Ensure that the necessary documents to support the said request are properly provided.

We have recommended that Management:
Verify the validity of payables aged two years and more, send confirmation letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors, and remunerate all valid claims. Otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund;
Exert effort to retrieve the documents of unidentified Other Payables or those without details in the subsidiary ledgers to properly classify the amounts recorded; and
Prepare the Aging of Payables to keep track of any outstanding accounts to suppliers and facilitate their payments.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the following:
A written explanation from the collecting officers for the noted delays in the remittance and the retention of collections within an unreasonable period; and
The Revenue Collecting Officer is to remit their collections and accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) at the close of each business day pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Vol. 1 and see to it that no accumulation of cash from collections and mingling of funds shall be made by any collector to avoid loss, misapplication and/or unauthorized use.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the following courses of action:
To ensure that all collections are deposited intact and daily, or on the next banking day pursuant to the provisions of Section 32 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUS), Volume I;
To instruct the Municipal Treasurer to check the actual amounts of collections remitted by the collecting officer and to regularly update the cashbooks on the amount of collections and deposits made for the day; and
Shall there be any cash overage incurred, demand a written explanation from the Accountable Officer concerned. If no satisfactory explanation was provided, issue OR for the amount of overage in favor of the government. In case of cash shortage, demand immediately the payment of the amount of shortage.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure that vital information required to substantiate the validity and propriety of the claims is complete and properly accomplished before affixing their signatures on the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payment in compliance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001.

We reiterated the following recommendations that Management requires:
The BAC to post the procurement opportunity on the PhilGEPS website for at least three (3) calendar days pursuant to Sections 54.2 and 54.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
The BAC to submit a BAC Resolution stating the mode of procurement adopted by the Management.
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments of goods or services procured by management are duly supported with BAC Resolution and printout copy of the posting on the PhilGEPS website.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the General Service Officer or its equivalent to require each department to prepare the APP-CSE for each year to be consolidated into one APP-CSE where the ordinary or regular supplies shall be procured on a quarterly basis at the DBM-PS as the first consideration. When unavailable, the items shall be procured through public bidding when the amount exceeds the threshold for shopping or small-value procurement.  Ensure that the conditions set forth by the law are strictly observed before resorting to alternative modes of procurement.
To minimize the recurrence of reimbursement as a practice of procuring common-use supplies, we have recommended that Management:
Ensure that all procurements of the LGU are meticulously and judiciously planned and within the approved budget of the Procuring Entity.
Procure ordinary and regular office supplies on a quarterly basis.
Require check payments to suppliers except for expenses that are petty in nature, non-recurring, and urgently needed, which shall be charged from the Petty Cash Fund.

We have recommended that Management require the BAC to:
Discontinue immediately the practice of procuring supplies and/or items from non-PhilGEPS-registered suppliers.
Ensure that only qualified suppliers are requested to tender their price quotations and/or awarded contracts in compliance with Section 8.5.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and Section J of the Guidelines on Shopping and Small Value Procurement.

We have recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to take immediate action to address the noted deficiencies and ensure that all lacking documents identified in the auditorial review, as enumerated in Annex A, are submitted to enable the Audit Team to establish the validity, legality and propriety of the procurement process on the implementation of the foregoing projects.

Considering the limited manpower of the LGU, especially in the Accounting, Treasury, and Assessor’s Office, we have recommended the consideration and adoption of electronic or computerized systems for accounting, assessment, and collection processes. This would lead to timely and cost-efficient transaction processing and facilitate the preparation of reports, enabling management to comply and submit on time. Given the benefits it would provide to management and employees, we highly encourage the implementation of this recommendation.

We have recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the ATM or digitized payroll system in the disbursement of payroll for salaries, wages and other emoluments of elected officials, regular and casual employees of the LGU. The adoption of a digitized payroll system is highly encouraged because it strengthens internal control on cash with improved delivery service to employees at reduced costs.

We have recommended that the Head of the Agency require the BAC Chairperson through the BAC Secretariat to:
Prepare all the documents required under each stage of the procurement on time to ensure that all documents are completed at the end of the procurement process.
Submit copies of perfected contracts, approved POs, and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period of five (5) working days pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 for a timely review and evaluation thereof. Administrative disciplinary action, as provided in Section 4 of the same circular against the persons responsible, be imposed upon unjustified failure to comply.

We have recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to ensure that:
No disbursement voucher shall be paid unless properly documented and signed.
All financial transaction documents from the Treasury Office are promptly turned over to the Accounting Office for timely recording of transactions, which contributes to the timely submission of accounts to COA.

We have recommended that management consider preparing an action plan where different strategies could be applied based on the age and nature of the collectibles and realistically project collections under the circumstances. The following collection strategies may be considered:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to cause notices of delinquency to be posted at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall  and in publicly accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the LGU concerned pursuant to Section 254 of RA 7160;
Advise the Chief of RPTS to seek assistance and intervention of barangay officials in the collection of delinquent accounts; and
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to enforce administrative and judicial actions after the expiration of the five-year collection period to all taxpayers with delinquent accounts.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all purchases of supplies and materials are recorded under the proper inventory account; and (ii) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as called for in the above-quoted regulation;
direct the BAC and Acting-Municipal Accountant to examine causes of   noted deviations in Item 2.18 to avoid similar lapses in future  transactions and adhere closely to the requirements of the law before payments thereof.
instruct the GSO to (i) conduct periodic physical count inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulations; and (ii) make an effort to procure directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations and made additions thereon and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the:
LDRRMO together with the LDRRMC, to (i)prepare and submit annually the LDRRMFIP in the prescribed format with details such as allotment class, in reference to COA Circular 2012-002, inclusive of the plan relative to the prior years’ unexpended balance of the fund; (ii) include in its plan the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and (iii) prepare and submit the liquidation report for the financial assistance received from the City of Davao; and
Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) include in its Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure in COA Circulars 2012-002 and 2014-002; and (ii) cause the preparation and submission of Report on Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations in a monthly basis within the reglementary period; and
Budget Officer to prepare Registry of Cash Donations and Utilization to for donations received per donor and purpose as per COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014;
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to consider the creation of a GSO position to be in charge of the records and reports for donations and other properties and inventories received and issued by the LGU and cause the preparation of the report on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations and submit the same within the prescribed period as mentioned above.

We reiterated and supplemented our prior year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) see to it that funds held in trust are duly supported with MOA or equivalent document and that the disbursements are in accordance therewith; (ii) effect the necessary entry in the GF to recognize the assets derecognized in the TF; (iii) exert additional efforts to trace the unreconciled trust liabilities and cause the return of the unutilized balances to their source agencies, if warranted; and (iv) in coordination with the BAC Chairman,  submit lacking documents on contracts discussed in item 6.16 of this Report; and
instruct the Local Finance Committee and the implementing departments to (i) review and submit the noted deficiencies in item 6.18.; and (ii) in case of future similar undertaking, strictly adhere to the agreement or guidelines relative to the specific fund held in trust.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the GAD Focal Point Person (GFPP) (i) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), work on the complete establishment of a GAD database and the maintenance thereof for a more gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; (ii) amend the GPB to match the pre-prepared GPB with the approved appropriation in the annual budget for the budget year, subject to the review and approval of the DILG; and (iii) closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects embodied in the approved GPB by the DILG to ensure full attainment of the GAD targets/objectives, intervene immediately if delays in the implementation are observed, and amend the GPB, if warranted; and
require the Budget Officer, during the budget deliberation make sure that at least 5% of the total appropriations for the year is budgeted or attributed for the ensuing year.

We reiterated and supplemented our prior years’ audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Require close coordination between the Treasurers’ Office and  the  Accounting Department, to (i) make sure that the same amount withheld is remitted within the prescribed period; (ii) look into the breakdown of the variances from the withheld amount and remittances starting off with the current year and subsequently the previous years and make necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (iii) on noted incorrect accounting entries, incoordination  with previous OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to the misstatements identified above, to reflect accurate balances of the accounts as mentioned in item 10.14 of this Report;
as part of the internal control system, designate/appoint a person who will check all deductions and remittances made to make sure all are accounted for; and
provide the Accounting Office with additional personnel, if possible, to aid in the preparation of the Index of Payment and immediate detection of errors in remittances.

We recommended that the City Mayor together with the Inventory Committee and the other concerned offices to complete the disposition and derecognition procedures for missing PPEs and for unidentified PPE per accounting records as stipulated in Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish accurate PPE balances.

We also recommended that the City Mayor require the Inventory Committee to validate the existence and condition of the 21 service vehicles and provide for the valuation of such PPEs for recording by the Accounting Office, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor direct the Chairman of the
Committee on Disposal to initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of the subject
properties, in accordance with the afore-cited provisions and with the Revised Manual on
the Disposal of Government Properties and provide the necessary adjusting entries in the
books of accounts.

In view thereof, we recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City
Accountant to adjust and withhold taxes from Accounts Payable still
outstanding as of date, representing procured goods and services, and apply
this practice prospectively in succeeding accounting periods pursuant to BIR
Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare
the necessary accounting entries/adjustments to correct the balances of the
affected accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CDRRM Officer to:
a. submit within 60 days from receipt thereof the City’s plan for the
implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to
P76,559,693.75, consisting of 70 percent MF and 30 percent QRF of
P53,804,693.75 and P22,755,000.00, respectively;
b. evaluate the feasibility of planned PPAs including the total amount of
disaster fund available and conduct strict monitoring to ensure its optimal
implementation within the plan, and identify projects which will no longer be
implemented for possible reversion and reprogramming to other disaster-
related PPAs.

We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to
identify which disaster fund projects are CO and which are MOOE in nature as
basis of the amount to be transferred as unspent balance of disaster fund to
trust liability at year end in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. require the City Agriculturist to coordinate with the City Planning and
Development Office and other concerned department for the identification of
potential barangay beneficiaries of the PCA fund for incorporation in the
Annual Investment Plan (AIP) as basis for its immediate implementation; and
b. require the City Social Welfare and Development Officer and the City
Accountant to submit the duly signed copy of the MOA or Trust Agreement
pertaining to funds received from PCSO as basis of implementation. If none,
exert efforts and coordinate with PCSO to facilitate the signing of MOA or
Trust Agreement.

We recommended that the City Development Council, headed by the City Mayor
assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current
needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects and include
development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2020-01.

We recommended that the City Mayor to require the GFPS to:
a. prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with the mandate of 4.C.2 of PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and Sections 1 and 3 of the PCW
Memorandum Circular 2018-04;
b. enhance their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related
laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender
analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related
tools among others; and
c. monitor the implementation of the GAD Agenda according to the budget
allotted for the year to avoid over/under utilization of the funds intended for
each specific programs and activities.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GSO to require the contractor
and the accountable officers to present and identify the enumerated items not
found during TAS inspection and request for the re-inspection, if warranted.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Acting City Treasurer to:
a. issue separate Accountable Forms for each collector, to prevent transfer of
Accountable Forms between AOs; and
b. monitor the issuances of Official Receipts by each Collecting Officers and
ensure that the Official Receipts used by Collecting Officers correspond to
those issued by the Treasurer’s Office through review of RAAF prepared by
each collector.
