a. seek the assistance of the GSO to determine the applicable fair values of the
unrecorded motor vehicles and record them whether serviceable or
unserviceable, in compliance with IPSAS 17 and 23 for proper presentation in
the financial statements; and

b. review all the items included in the PPE accounts and reclassify the identified
misclassified motor vehicles with net book value P19,663,319.46 to their
proper Construction and Heavy Equipment account.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. direct the City Engineer to submit justification on the circumstances
surrounding the demolition of the Buildings, Other Structures and Markets,
and why the Office of the Auditor was not timely informed of the said
demolition;

b. instruct the City GSO to submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Properties for the demolished properties, photographs as well
as the Waste Material Report;

c. require the City Accountant to:
➢ reconcile with the records of the GSO as regards the demolished
properties;
➢ prepare the appropriate adjusting entries to recognize the derecognition
after thorough assessment of the transactions, and copy furnished the
Office of the Auditor for evaluation and reference; and
➢ make proper coordination with the responsible departments for proper
documentation and recording of demolished properties;

d. in coordination with the responsible departments, ensure strict compliance
with the guidelines on the divestment or disposal of property and other
assets as provided under COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to ensure that
DRRM funds received from National Government and from other LGUs be treated
as special trust liability recorded under Trust Liabilities – DRRMF in the Trust
Fund books, instead of Due to Other NGAs account. Prepare the necessary
accounting entry to recognize the adjustment/transfer of the P4,889,330.00 from
Due to Other NGAs to Trust Liabilities – DRRMF.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in coordination with the responsible
departments, to:

a. aim to reduce unnecessary delays in project implementation and strictly effect
the necessary processes to expedite the completion of the not-yet started and
ongoing projects, to allow the early enjoyment of benefits by the general
public and the full utilization of the funds received from various NGAs;

b. consider preparing a request for extension/retention of the two financial
assistance chargeable against the FY 2020 Calamity Fund rather than the
immediate return of the unutilized funds; and

c. prepare the required monthly and other quarterly Report on Fund Utilization
and Status of Program/Project/Activity Implementation, and submit the same
to the NDRRMC and other oversight bodies, copy furnished the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare the
adjusting Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the unutilized balance of ER1-
94 Funds received by the City, as follows:

Trust Fund Book:
Other Payables P1,562,639.12
Cash in Bank - LCCA P1,562,639.12
General Fund Book:
Cash in Bank - LCCA P1,562,639.12
Prior Year Adjustment P1,562,639.12

In addition, we recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to
record all subsequent shares of Funds under the Share from National Wealth
account in the General Fund book of the City, pursuant to IPSAS 1 and 23, COA
Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 and BLGF Memorandum Circular
No. 03-01-2017.

B. Due to misclassification of the ER 1-94 Funds, the funds totaling P25.956
million were utilized without appropriation approved by the Sangguniang
Panlungsod, thus non-observance of Section 294 of Republic Act (RA) No.
7160, The Local Government Code of 1991. Relative thereto, the separate
account in the General Fund was not maintained for the Share from National
Wealth account, contrary to Section 105, Chapter 6 of the Manual on New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume I, hence, the
proper procedure on the appropriation, disbursements, recording and
monitoring of the funds received was not strictly observed.

Section 294 of RA No. 7160 provides that the proceeds from the share of local
government units pursuant to this chapter shall be appropriated by their respective
Sanggunian to finance local development and livelihood projects: Provided, however,
that at least 80 percent of the proceeds derived from the development and utilization
of hydrothermal, geothermal, and other sources of energy shall be applied solely to
lower the cost of electricity in the local government unit where such a source of
energy is located.

Meanwhile, Section 105, Chapter 6 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I states the
following:

“Sec. 105. Special Accounts in the General Fund. – Local government units shall
maintain special accounts in the General Fund for public utilities and other
economic enterprises, loans, interests, bond issues, and other contributions for
specific purposes; and development projects funded from the share of the local
government concerned from the internal revenue collections and development of
national wealth and such other special accounts which may be created by law or
ordinance.”

The manner of the appropriation and preparation of reports and its monitoring should
be similar with the 20 percent Development Fund and five percent Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund, wherein the appropriations need to be
approved by the Sangguniang Panlungsod and separate Registries shall be
maintained to monitor its utilizations.

Verification of the utilization of ER 1-94 Funds amounting to P24,393,490.00
disclosed that there was no approved appropriation by the Sangguniang Panlungsod
(SP) for the programs and projects to be charged out of the Fund. Relative thereto, a
Separate Account in the books of the General Fund and Registries was not prepared
and maintained by the City. As discussed earlier, the amount of receipt and
utilization of the funds was recorded in the books of the Trust Fund. Hence, the non-
observance of Section 294 of RA No. 7160, and contrary to Section 105, Chapter 6
of the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I.

However, it is worthy to mention that despite the absence of the ordinance, the City
passed the following resolutions and was presented to the Audit Team, to wit:
• SP Resolution No. 2019-016 authorizing the City Mayor and the City Treasurer to
open a Trust Account with the Landbank of the Philippines (LBP) Sto. Tomas
Branch for financial benefits provided under Energy Regulation No. 1-94 issued
by the Department of Energy (DOE) from the operation of Aboitiz Power (AP)
Renewables, Inc. power facility of the AP Renewables, Inc. located at Sto.
Tomas, Batangas;
• SP Resolution No. 2019-1635 authorizing the City Mayor and the City Treasurer
154
to open a trust account with the LBP Sto. Tomas Branch for the financial benefits
provided under the Energy Regulation No. 1-94 issued by the DOE from the
operations of the 32-Megawatt Maibarara Geothermal Power Facility of the
Maibarara Geothermal, Inc. located at the City of Sto. Tomas, Batangas;
• SP Resolution No. 2019-015 authorizing the City Mayor, Hon. Edna P. Sanchez,
to enter into a memorandum of agreement for and in behalf of the City of Sto.
Tomas, Batangas with the AP Renewables, Inc. for the establishment and
administration of trust accounts in accordance with DOE DC No. 2018-08-0021
of AP Renewables Power Facility at Sto. Tomas, Batangas;
• SP Resolution No. 2019-1634 authorizing the City Mayor, Hon. Edna P.
Sanchez, to enter into a memorandum of agreement for and in behalf of the City
of Sto. Tomas, Batangas with the Maibarara Geothermal, Inc. for the
establishment and administration of trust accounts in accordance with DOE DC
No. 2018-08-0021 of the 32 MW Maibarara Geothermal Power Facility of
Maibarara Geothermal, Inc. (MGI) at Sto. Tomas, Batangas.
• SP Resolution No. 2021-466 certifying that the Annual Work Program for 2021 of
AP Renewables, Inc. and Maibarara Geothermal, Incorporated is in accordance
with the City’s Annual Investment Plan (AIP) Program.

Misclassification of Management in taking up the ER 1-94 Funds as liability and
crediting the Due to NGAs and Other Payables accounts instead of a revenue and
crediting Share from National Wealth account resulted in the non-issuance of the
ordinance for the appropriation of the funds. Also, due to the non-maintenance of
Registries, the proper recording of the disbursements was not appropriately
monitored by the concerned City officials.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. direct the CDRRMO to judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of
the LDRRM Funds, and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve
the goal/target of the City regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk
reduction and management;

b. direct the CDRRMO to use or transfer only the fund under Special Trust Fund
for other purposes after five years, of which, the fund will be reverted back to
the unappropriated surplus of the GF;

c. require the City Accountant and the City Budget Officer to establish a
continuing appropriation in the ensuing year intended for the amount of the
rescue truck, which was already obligated/contracted, but no delivery yet.
Likewise, direct the City Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the nature of transaction in order to inform the users of the status
of the said fund; and

d. require the CDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged
against the unexpended appropriation of previous years or those funds under
the Trust Liability-DRRM, and submit the list of PPAs funded or to be funded
from the unexpended prior year’s DRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) strictly comply with the
provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, series of 2017, and prepare the annual
and supplemental budget as prescribed for a more transparent and accountable
reporting.

We also recommended that the LSB require the schools to prepare the 3-year SIP
for the identification of the priority programs/projects/activity to be included in the
SEF Budget.

Further, we recommended that the officer concerned cause the immediate
reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the balances of the projects already
implemented in the amount of P14,940,733.86 for reprogramming to another
PPAs for the benefits of the students.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. officials and employees concerned to refrain from resorting to procurement and
disbursement through reimbursement basis, and comply strictly with the
relevant provisions of RA No. 9184. Provide to the Office of the Auditor
substantial proof of the actual delivery of the cellular phones purchased;

b. Accounting Office, in coordination with the City Health Office, to locate and
submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly signed payrolls for the proper
liquidation of the cash advance for the financial assistance;

c. City Accountant to ensure the full implementation of the perpetual inventory
method in recording and reporting of all procured supplies and materials,
pursuant to the Section 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

d. concerned departments to submit to the Audit Team the proof of distribution/
utilization of the items/goods which lacked the appropriate distribution lists for
evaluation of the Audit Team; and

e. Bids and Awards Committee to ensure compliance with the posting
requirements of Request for Quotation, Notice of Award and Purchase Order
under the alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended that the City Solid Waste Management Board review and update
its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the current and succeeding years,
in compliance with RA No. 9003.

Review the initial plan of establishing a new sanitary landfill considering the
budgetary requirements and other factors which may affect its feasibility and
necessity of having a landfill.

Likewise, we recommended that the City Government ensure that all barangays have
established their own operational MRF, for efficiently and effectively discharged an
environmentally-sound practice in solid waste management.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC, thru its Secretariat, to
ensure compliance with:

a. Section 12.2 of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 39-2017 dated
December 21, 2017 in the preparation of PMR and APCPI Results; and

b. GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020 in the posting and
submission requirements of the APP, PMR, and APCI to promote
transparency in the government procurement process.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City GSO to adhere strictly to
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the prompt submission of the
copies of perfected Contracts/POs and their supporting documents within five
working days after execution/issuance thereof, for timely review/evaluation of
the legal and/or technical aspects of the contracts, and for Management to be
informed early of any deficiencies noted in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Office of the CGSO to submit
to COA the letter notifying the Audit Team on every delivery for post-audit
reference regardless of the modes of procurement used.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructed the GAD Focal Point System to strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanisms of the Municipality’s CBMS and GAD databases through preparation of master plan and indicating the timelines, process, budget and other necessary actions.



Thereafter, start the implementation in CY 2021 in order to ensure updated, operational and reliable information provided by said databases which will serve in attaining an evidenced-based planning, policy-making, and identification of beneficiaries for relief distribution and financial assistance in the future.

We recommended further that if necessary, the Management to augment the number of employees responsible for the maintenance and development of the databases, more specifically to update the data contained therein because data gathering and collating mechanism of the GAD Database and CBMS Database is certainly a tedious work.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality was able to update the GAD database, however, the CBMS will now be under the PSA.




The Municipality had planned to conduct annual survey pertaining to the data needed in planning, policy making and audit with the assistance of the 24 barangays.




The Municipality already hired a Planning Assistant and Job Orders who are responsible for the maintenance and development of the databases.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to revisit the existing plan for the procurement and distribution of relief goods;



and craft agreements with other suppliers that could timely cater the needs of the Municipalities in times of calamities.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality has 500 packs of relief goods stockpiled at the DRRM Operations Center to comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121 or the DRRM Act of 2010 for immediate distribute to families in emergency situation.  

The Municipality has an existing MOA with the owners of the grocery establishments and refilling stations to cater our needs in times of disaster or calamity.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

trace the remaining variance of P1,274,526.22 between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts through the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) duly supported with documents, explanations and/or computations in order to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements;

ensure that reciprocal accounts have the same balances all the time to facilitate transfer or reversion of cash to the debtor fund/s; and

call the attention of the Municipal Treasurer to transfer/revert the amount of P12,460,101.64 from Trust Fund to General Fund for errors made in previous years.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant explained that there were no available SL for the said balances which pertained prior to CY 2014.





As of December 2021, all reciprocal accounts have the same balances except for those arose prior to CY 2014.  

The Municipal Accountant had already transferred all previous years’ Due to Other Fund accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust best effort to locate all relevant records and documents to support the amounts booked from previous years, i.e. through available means such as confirmation and inquiry with former Accountants and other concerned officials.

Thereafter, if all remedies had been exhausted, the Municipal Mayor request from the Sangguniang Bayan to revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality.


We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify the liabilities in conformity with Paragraph 80 of IPSAS 1 for fair presentation in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
Confirmation letters were sent, and inquiry was made.





The Sanggunian Bayan had already issued a resolution for the said request.




The Municipal Accountant had classified the said payables under current liabilities.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection on completed projects after the one-year defects liability period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if there are no defects detected or after such were repaired or corrected.

Further, for the Management to oblige the contractor to post warranty security that will address the structural defects that may be discovered from the final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2.

Lastly, for the Management to furnish the Audit Team of copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed contracts to monitor the posting of the said warranty security by the contractors.
observations.managementAction:
The Inspectorate Team had issued Certificates of Final Acceptance for all the five completed projects in CY 2019.




The Management had obliged the contractors to post warranty security.




Copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance and warranty security were already submitted to the COA Office.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the lacking documentary requirements immediately to the Office of the Auditor; 



and to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that contracts and its supporting documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) had submitted all the documents for the six projects which include POW, Detailed estimates, Approved Design/Plan and other supporting documents.

The BAC ensures that the contracts and its supporting documents are submitted.

The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) had participated in the first semester Barangay Assembly to disseminate information.




The Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Ordinance served as guidelines in implementing the no segregation, no collection policy.  At present, the LGU has 75 operational MRFs in the 24 barangays.  



After the inauguration of the sanitary landfill on June 25, 2021, the LGU started collecting wastes and saturated all the 24 barangays.

The sanitary landfill was fully operational just after its inauguration on June 25, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enter into a contract with a reputable gasoline station for the procurement of fuel for the usage of the Municipality, upon authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan.

We also recommended that Management establish internal control guidelines on the purchase, use and documentation of fuel and motor vehicle-related transactions of the Municipality such that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished, travel and purchase of fuel are properly authorized.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality had entered into an agreement with the owner of the gasoline station for its regular consumption.



The Municipality ensured that starting January 2021, the Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWD Officer to review/re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures on AICS Program, and adopt a written policy on the determination as to the appropriateness, procedure and payment of financial assistance to individuals/families in crisis situation, considering the nature, eligibility of beneficiaries, amount of financial assistance and such other measures that would facilitate the effective and efficient implementation of the AICS Program of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO and MPDC were able to draft the guidelines which were already approved and adopted by the Municipality.

The Building Official had already incorporated the payment of occupancy permit in their building permit fee for CY 2021 to ensure its collection. Also, inspection of the newly built structures will follow as soon as its completion.

The Municipality enjoined the barangay officials to report any construction or completed structures.



The Municipality is closely monitoring the issuance of permits.



The Municipal Assessor is waiting for the implementation of the general revision of RPT as directed by the Provincial Assessor’s Office to saturate all structures within the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the wages of Job Order Workers under Other General Services and for the Municipal Accountant to charge the actual disbursements on the same transactions to the said account.

Also, we recommended that the Budget Officer ensure that accounts used in budgeting are reflective of the account’s function and intention of the Management.
observations.managementAction:
The wages of the Job Order were appropriated already to Other General Services and at the same time the Municipal Accountant charged the same to the said account.


The Budget Officer ensures that accounts used in budgeting are reflective of the account’s function.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following concerned officials:

Municipal Treasurer to maintain stock cards per stock number and prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued duly supported by Requisition and Issue Slips for submission to the Municipal Accountant;

Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventory items and thereafter, submit to the Auditor the Reports of the Physical Count of Inventories not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and

Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and to record the inventories using the asset method.  She should coordinate with the Provincial Accountant on the determination of the cost of medicines and medical supplies in order to facilitate the accurate amount to be adjusted in order to reflect fair financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
A stock card was prepared to track the donations bestowed by the DOH and PHO.




A physical inventory was conducted semestral by the Inventory Committee.




The Municipal Accountant already maintained a Ledger Cards for medicines and medical supplies present in the Municipal Health Office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management to strengthen the internal control as to the monitoring, coordination, planning and execution of Republic Act No. 7171 and Republic Act No. 8240.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensures the internal control

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management meticulously and adequately plan and identify the projects that are feasible and compliant with Section 3 of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1, including the accumulated savings amounting to P1,140,494.20.


We also recommended that the Management ensure that 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized for the attainment of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The disallowed PPAs of the SP were reprogrammed so as to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund and the unimplemented projects were implemented to attain its optimum benefits for the betterment of the locality.

The Municipality ensured that the fund be optimally utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to maintain updated database on basic/minimum information on vulnerable sectors to facilitate the LGU addressing appropriately and accurately the probable measures before, during and after calamities;



require the MDRRMO to include in the propose LDRRMFIP which is to be approved by the MDRRMC the procurement of forklift, jack hammer, shoring, electric drill, concrete cutter, hacksaw and chisel needed for disaster preparedness, response and recovery of the Municipality.  

Thereafter procure through the Bids and Awards Committee, the needed tools and equipment; and


capacitate the MDRRMO and his staff through seminars like preparation of Business Continuity Plan, Hazardous Materials Management and Management of the Dead and the Missing.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO had set up a database pertaining to the vulnerable sectors and its vulnerabilities per barangay for planning purposes which are vital to protect and save lives before and during the calamities.  At the same time, to fast track the implementation of the rehabilitation plan.

The Municipality had included in the LDRRMFIP the said tools and equipment.




The Municipality had already procured the aforementioned equipment needed by the MDRRMO in times of disasters.

The LDRRMO and his staff attended various seminars to equip them of what to undertake in times of unforeseen contingencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer prepare the Report of Disbursement as supporting document for liquidation for each specific cash advance to facilitate monitoring of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The supporting documents were prepared and attached to the liquidation report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to devise a control mechanism that will secure compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act No. 11260, thus, ensuring employee productivity and satisfying their families’ basic needs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensures that the employees shall only be allowed to avail loans with maximum monthly amortization amounting to the difference between their current net take home pay and mandated amount to maintain compliance with the above-cited provision.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanism of the Municipality GAD Database to ensure an updated, operational and reliable database, which serve the GAD Focal Point System in attaining an evidence-based GAD planning and policy-making.  The retroactive effects of the lack of data provided by the GAD Database may be theoretical in nature, but the prospective outcome of improving the database will most likely be positive, however, difficult to measure; and

comply with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the establishment of a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system through the GAD M&E Team’s performance of its functions to evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Planning Team assisted the GAD Focal Person in filling up the lacking data in the GAD database. Also, the Planning Office collated data from various offices and agencies to ensure compliance of the memorandum and speed up the M&E process.





The Municipality had complied with the mentioned provisions.

The Municipality had strictly imposed the conditions set forth in the Market Lease Agreement per stall owners/vendors in the market to raise more revenues for the betterment of the municipality.










The Market Supervisor provided an individual ledger for all stall owners and vendors to track their timely payment and remind them of their financial obligations every month.



The personnel in-charge was able to prepare schedules of the lease and the corresponding payments.

The Treasury Office was able to collect from some erring lessees.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Management agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality exercise its authority to update the existing Revenue Code of the Municipality in conformity with the provisions of the Local Government code of 1991, otherwise known as RA 7160 in order to generate additional income to finance programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Revenue Code of the Municipality was already updated and authorized by the Office of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan last March 9, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Sanggunian facilitate the identification of assigned/designated common parking area/s where collection of parking fee shall be imposed.  


Also, the Municipal Treasurer to strictly implement the provisions of the Regulatory Fees as per provision of the Revised Municipal Revenue Code of the Municipality in order to generate more income to finance programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.

Likewise, the regulation of the parking area could prevent vehicular accidents along the national highway of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Sangguniang Bayan have already assigned/
designated common parking area/s per TODA in the municipality.  

The Municipal Treasurer had implemented the provisions of the Regulatory Fees.




The Municipality prohibited illegal parking and road obstructions along the major thoroughfares per memorandum issued by the DILG on road clearing.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card or computer generated report to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for Real Property Taxes and Special Education Taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the LGU purchased a PC Server/ Workstation for the implementation of iTAX. The LGU installed the necessary software, through the help of the provincial office, in the procured PC Workstation. This enable the concerned office to prepare and furnish a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office based the amount of set-up for CY 2021 RPT and SET Receivable on a list of taxpayers and taxes due and collectibles submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended Management:


To instruct the Property Custodian to issue PAR to the end user and renew at least every three years or every time there is a change in accountability of the property.

To require the Inventory Committee to adopt a uniform property identification system for PPE wherein a unique Property Number shall be assigned for each item for proper identification of properties in conformity with Section 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. 
	
For the General Service Officer to prepare the Report of Physical Count in the prescribed format in accordance with Sections 69, 70, 71, 72 and 73 Volume II of GAM for LGUs and properly install a strong internal control in safeguarding the government properties.
observations.managementAction:
Management directed the property custodian to issue PAR to the end user of the purchased equipment.


The management required the Municipal Treasurer to adopt a uniform property identification system for PPE and to prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)


The management required Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of GSO, to use the prescribed format of Report of Physical Count in accordance with Sections 69, 70, 71, 72 and 73 Volume II of GAM for LGUs.
observations.reason:
Management complied with the recommendation.

Not Implemented

A uniform Property Identification System for PPE was not yet adopted by the LGU. 


Not Implemented

The Municipal Treasurer did not adopt the prescribed format of the Report of Physical Count.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure complete submission of necessary supporting documents such as the approved DTRs and application for leaves before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit.


That the HRMO regularly verify the DTRs and application for leaves and ensure that before the DTRs are forwarded to the Local Chief Executive for approval, the DTRs have been duly processed/ verified and initialled by the concerned Department Head.


That management may consider the usage of the biometric machine of the Municipality for the personnel to register actual and prescribed office or work hours in order to ensure correctness in the computation/payment of leave credits, salaries and wages.
observations.managementAction:
Management commented that to support the payment of Salaries and Wages for the year 2021, the management opt to use the manual DTR initialed by the Head of Office, verified by the HRMO and approved by the Local Chief Executive to ensure compliance to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 1 and 2, Rule XVII of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book XVII of Executive Order No. 292.

The management required the HRMO to regularly verify the DTRs and application for leaves and ensure that before the DTRs are forwarded to the Local Chief Executive for approval, the DTRs have been duly processed/ verified and initialed by the concerned Department Head.

For the year 2020, the management use manual attendance signed by the concerned employee and verified by HRMO. The management temporarily stop the use of biometric machine, as a measure to contain the spread of COVID19.
observations.reason:
Post audit of transactions for CY 2021 showed that the payment of salaries was already supported with manual DTR of LGU employees.

Fully Implemented

Management complied with the recommendation.

Not Implemented

Management still use the manual recording of the actual attendance of LGU personnel.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management instruct the Office of the HRMO and Sangguniang Secretary to issue and submit a certificate showing the number of workdays of actual service rendered in a month of the department heads and officials claiming RATA to serve as a basis in the computation of actual claims. The certification should be issued by the Office of the HRMO for department heads covered under Section 7.1 of LBC No. 103 and by the Sangguniang Secretary for those officials covered under Section 7.5 of the same Local Budget Circular.
observations.managementAction: HRMO and Sangguniang Bayan Secretary issued the necessary certification for the actual service rendered in a month of the department heads and officials, to support and serve as a basis in the computation of RATA. The management will ensure further compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that payment of BAC and TWG honoraria be supported with complete documentary requirements as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 also known as the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions to support validity and propriety of the claim.
observations.managementAction: The management attached and submitted the necessary documents to support the payment of honoraria of BAC and TWG. The management will ensure further compliance to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Treasury Office to prepare a duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Ticket to properly control the use of government motor vehicles. Further, monthly report of fuel consumption with PO slip attached be prepared for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed. 

For the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for fuel consumption be supported with complete documentation in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2020, the Municipal Treasury Office prepares PO slip to properly control the fuel consumption of the LGU and also institute the use of Driver’s Trip Ticket.
observations.reason:
Management complied with audit recommendation.


Fully Implemented

As per post audit of CY 2021, the management was able to support the payment of fuel with Driver’s Trip Ticket.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the concerned officials and employees submit immediately the documents being required for a holistic evaluation of the project by the Regional Technical Service of this Office and for the Audit Team to generate timely and relevant audit results. 

It is to be emphasized herein that non-compliance on the submission may result in the issuance of Notice of Suspension. 
In addition, deficiencies noted should be properly addressed to the advantage of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management directed the concerned officials and employees to submit the documents being required for a holistic evaluation of the project by the Regional Technical Service.
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to Management:

Require the concerned Municipal Office to prepare the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor; and


Require the Municipal Accountant to record any balances of donations received be costed and recorded in the books or in the registries.
observations.managementAction:
The LDRRMO prepared and submitted the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, and a copy will be furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

The management posted and disclosed the Summary/List of Donations in its agency website. Anonymous donations with undeclared value prevent the Municipal Accountant to record the cost/value of donation.
observations.reason:
A copy of the summary/list of donations received, distributed and balances was provided to the Office of the Auditor.


Fully Implemented

Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management exert more effort to collect these real property tax delinquencies especially those that have been long overdue in order for the municipality to augment its income that could be used for other projects beneficial to the constituents of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2020, the LGU significantly reduce the delinquencies on Real Property Tax. This emanates from the joint efforts of local officials and employees, especially through the constant communication and action of Local Chief Executive to the land owners. For the year 2021, the LGU’s remaining real property tax delinquencies only amounts to P937,367.76. This shows the effective joint efforts of local officials and employees in collecting real property taxes.
observations.reason: Considering the decrease of the delinquent RPT, the Audit Team encourage the management to exert more effort to collect the remaining RPT delinquents.

observations.recommendation: We commended the efforts of the Municipal Treasurer in continuing the practice of remitting amounts due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291.

We commended the efforts of Management for being compliant with the HDMF Law and we recommended that it sustain efforts in the correct deduction of premiums and loan amortizations as well as in the timely remittance thereof pursuant to the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of Management for being compliant with the National Insurance Act and we recommended that it sustain efforts in the correct deduction of premiums as well as in the timely remittance thereof pursuant to the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit financial reports and documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, and strictly adhere with the requirements on the timely submission of accounts and financial reports, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY- 2020 | The management hired additional permanent personnel and Job Order personnel in the Office of the Municipal Accountant to facilitate and take charge of the submission of the reports and pertinent documents needed by the Audit Team and to submit them on the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to present the PPAs of the 20% DF in details and group them in accordance with its prescribed components; 

(b) instruct the BAC Members, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to    properly plan and strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund to only eligible capital expenditures to ensure that the Fund is utilized for its intended purpose; and 

(c) continue to maximize implementation of PPAs on a timely fashion and in accordance with the plans and targets for the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY- 2020 | The concerned departments assured the Management that they will strictly follow the recommendations of the Audit Team with regards to the components and the Utilization of the 20% Development Plan. The management also mandated all the Department Heads to submit PPMP to be used as a basis in APP formulation.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the efforts of the Municipality in complying with some of minimum standards on disaster preparedness as required in RA 10121 and NDRRM standards. 

However, on other minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2020 | The MDRRM office will recommend in the yearly Planning and Budgeting to include and consider the purchase of equipment of their office base on the priority of their usage or importance

AAR CY- 2020 | The Management assigned personnel to take charge of the Reports or documents needed to comply with the COA recommendations. Issuance of Gas/Fuel PO is strictly monitored by the Office of the Mayor and non-issuance of Gas PO to department heads.
AAR CY 2020 | The Management complied with the Audit team recommendations as to 5% budget requirements and to strictly monitor and check that all activities and disbursements are GAD related.

AAR CY- 2020 | The Management issued certification signed by the Municipal Budget Officer and HRMO that the MSWD staff are qualified to receive Hazard Pay under DBM DSWD Joint Circular No. 1 Series of 2018 dated December 27, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the 

(a) return of the invalid/expired official receipts to MD One Trading and request for replacements or issuance of new and valid official receipts; and 

b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments made by the municipality are adequately supported with complete and valid documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY- 2020 | The Management required or instructed the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments made by the Municipality are adequately supported with complete and valid documents. The Management and the MD One trading have already discussed the issues and currently seeking for advice to settle the problem.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to submit the liquidation report for SAP-1; and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the cash received from the DSWD and the cash advances made by the Municipal Treasurer for the fair presentation of the financial statements of the Municipality in accordance with the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and other accounting standards, rules and procedures; 

(b) Municipal Treasurer or any issuing official to secure a copy of any issued certification or any evidence of the returned amount of money by the unqualified recipients of SAP or for the same type of assistance for the transparency of the Municipal Office; 

(c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the MSWDO on the unsubmitted documents as required by DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020 and the MOA including the additional payroll, accordingly, to this Office; and 

(d) MSWDO and other concerned officials to 

(i) conduct further investigation regarding the claim of a beneficiary who did not receive the SAP despite being in the DSWD and Municipality’s payroll where a signature was reflected; and 

(ii) coordinate with DSWD Field Office III on the unmatched list, synchronize the data based on the actual payout made to aid in the posting of accurate data on the DSWD website.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY- 2020 | The concerned departments had already prepared the SAP 1 Liquidation reports.  The Office of the Municipal Accountant had made the adjusting entries for the Cash Received and the Cash Advances made by the Treasurer.

	The staff of the MSWD and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer conducted an investigation as recommended by the Audit Team as to the claim of the beneficiary allegedly did not receive the SAP despite being in the Municipal Payroll with her signature.  Mrs. Cecilia D. Manaloto, the complainant/recipient acknowledged the receipt of “AYUDA” in the amount of Six Thousand Five Hundred (6,500.00), thinking that SAP and Ayuda are different and as to the returned SAP, they were replaced by qualified recipient indicated in one payroll.

	The List of qualified SAP recipient was recommended by the Barangay Officials and was subject for assessment of the MSWD Staff. The final list of recipients was submitted at the DSWD Field Office III for cross matching process.

	The preparation of official payrolls was based on the clean lists downloaded by the DSWD.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant commented that he made a suggestion to the GSO to use the records of the Accounting Office due to the fact that all the data they need for reconciliation could be found in the said records. The GSO was amenable to the idea and promised to start the reconciliation of their records.

The GSO commented that their office had submitted to the Audit Team the report of unserviceable properties, however, the said report was returned to them for compliance, as it lacked supporting documents. As of this writing, the report has not yet been returned to the Audit Team. The Chairman of the Committee on Disposal had not initiated/facilitated disposal of these properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
• City Treasurer to prepare the Schedule of Unreleased Checks every year-end for submission to the Accounting Office as basis of the City Accountant in the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the restoration of the cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and the recognition of the appropriate liability/payable account at year-end; and
• City Accountant to ensure that only those unreleased checks at year-end would be included in the adjusting entries for fairness in the presentation of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant is setting-up the amount of due and collectible RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers of the City Treasurer pursuant to Section 42, Volume 1 of the GAM for LGUs; and adjusting entries included only the unreleased checks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
• the Management to devise various strategies to collect the delinquent accounts; • follow up from the homeowner’s associations and private companies the payment for production assessment; and • initiate the necessary legal actions thru the City’s Legal Officer against defaulting consumers in order to enforce collection of long overdue receivables.
observations.managementAction: Communication with homeowners' associations and other private companies for the collection of such accounts. Proposed to hire 45 Job Order workers to help the Management in collecting the delinquent accounts.
observations.reason: Some actions were implemented; collection of long overdue delinquent accounts will take more time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
• the City Accountant to set-up the amount of due and collectible RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers submitted by the City Treasurer pursuant to Section 42, Volume 1 of the GAM for LGUs; and
• the official concerned to make or draw Journal Entry Voucher for the adjustments supported by schedules and explanations.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant started setting-up the amount of due and collectible RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers submitted by the City Treasurer. The City Accountant is amenable but no JEV was made by the Accounting Office yet.
observations.reason: No detailed evaluation and tracing was made by the City Accountant since the beginning balance still existed in the records; JEV not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to continuously reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs by identifying and analyzing the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for adjustment, if warranted, so as to achieve reconciled account balances.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant already proposed to hire 45 Job Order workers to help them with the massive and more efficient distribution of notice to delinquent taxpayers. Coordination with the Provincial Accountant was undertaken to reconcile the records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records is being conducted; records reconciliation not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to draw the necessary accounting entries to reclassify the discount on real property taxes paid in advance to its proper account for fairness in presentation of accounts in the City’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Accounting entry has already been made to reclassify the discount on real property taxes paid in advance to its proper account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor cause the proper planning and monitoring by the personnel/offices in charge of the programs/projects to ensure timely implementation and completion, thus the attainment of its purpose/s.
observations.managementAction: For subsequent programs/projects of the City, cause the proper planning and monitoring by the personnel/offices in charge; measures were being implemented to improve planning and monitoring.
observations.reason: Some planning and monitoring measures were implemented but not all distributions were completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned Department Heads to:
• submit all the lacking supporting documents for the various transactions within 10 days from receipt of this Report; • submit justification on the noted inconsistencies within 10 days from receipt of this Report to support BGCM transactions and ensure all documentary requirements for emergency purchases are complied with; and • ensure that payments to suppliers/creditors be made based on complete and adequate supporting documents to prevent lapses in procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that they will submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team; some documents were submitted, others pending. City Mayor also instructed departments to refrain from reimbursement basis procurements and to provide proof of actual delivery.
observations.reason: Not all documents were submitted; some justifications and supporting documents remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned: • City Accountant to make partial submission of lacking supporting documents within 10 days and substantiate the claims; and • Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), thru its Secretariat, to ensure that procedures under negotiated procurement (emergency cases) are undertaken and required documents prepared.
observations.managementAction: Management accepted recommendation and instructed concerned offices to submit supporting documents; BAC to ensure compliance.
observations.reason: Not all required documents were submitted for evaluation; some procedures for negotiated procurement were not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned to submit the necessary documents within 10 days from receipt of this Report to support the grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay to officials and employees and job order workers during the ECQ period. Otherwise, Notices of Suspension will be issued to enforce submission.
observations.managementAction: Some supporting documents were submitted; City Mayor to issue notice if required documents not submitted.
observations.reason: Not all required supporting documents were submitted; further verification pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, in compliance with Sections 2.0 and 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were to be implemented during the year were submitted.
observations.reason: Some PPAs were submitted; not all were reported at start of year as required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
• Local Development Council, City Engineering Office and City Planning and Development Office (CPDO), together with concerned offices, to immediately look into the unimplemented projects under the 20 percent Development Fund and take appropriate actions on causes noted for their non-implementation; formulate strategies/solutions and timetable for implementation; and
• CPDO ensure programs/projects properly planned before bidding to avoid implementation problems; City Engineer to perform in-depth planning and detailed site inspections; CPDO to enhance monitoring and submit regular status reports to the City Mayor.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the City Mayor instructed City Accountant and City Budget Officer to perform actions including immediate reversion of unexpended balances and presentation to LSB; CPDO enhanced monitoring; City Engineer conducting planning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: • City Accountant and City GSO to evaluate the causes of the material discrepancy of P79,685,766.09 to ascertain the fairness and reliability of the PPE balances in the financial statements; • City GSO together with the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory and reconcile results with accounting records and submit to the Auditor; and • City Accountant to make detailed evaluation and tracing of the noted unreconciled beginning balances to reflect reliable balances in the financial statements; and the City GSO to prepare the RPCPPE in conformity with prescribed format and contents and ensure submission to the Auditor on or before January 31 each year; completely fill up and include vital information on the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant commented and suggested reconciliation and the GSO was amenable; inventory committee started tracing; City Accountant to exert efforts to evaluate and trace unreconciled balances.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE prepared for CY 2021 was still not in conformity with the prescribed format; inventory reconciliation not completed for all properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
• Inventory Committee, in coordination with the City Engineering Office, to conduct physical inventory of all existing local road networks in the City and reconcile it with accounting records and furnish a copy to the Audit Team; • General Services Officer to periodically maintain a complete Report on Local Road Networks, prepare Road Map and keep Local Road Network Property Cards and furnish copies to concerned offices; • City Engineer to provide the City Accountant the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and • City Accountant to maintain subsidiary records and provide separate depreciation for the road and road components.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee started conducting physical count; City Engineer and City Assessor to provide supporting records; City Accountant started maintaining SLs for road networks.
observations.reason: Some actions started (physical count, record maintenance) but reconciliation and full compliance not yet achieved; assessment and road mapping ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and City Treasurer to reconcile the records of RPT and SET receivables; City Treasurer to exert additional effort to trace and identify delinquent taxpayers and to implement more effective tax campaign and intensified collection measures; propose to hire job order workers to help in massive distribution of notices and collection.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant started maintaining subsidiary ledgers; City Treasurer proposed to hire 45 Job Order workers; coordination and efforts to intensify collection undertaken.
observations.reason: Due to difficulty tracing delinquent taxpayers and voluminous records; reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
• Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory and prepare RPCI and reconcile with accounting records and furnish RPCI to Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 each year; • City Accountant and GSO to determine status of old/beginning balances and reconcile; and • maintain SLCs and Stock Cards and Property Cards for systematic monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee started tracing status of old/beginning balances; City Accountant started maintaining SLCs for each category of supplies; GSO to maintain SCs and PCs.
observations.reason: Inventory count not completed; updating of records ongoing; reconciliation not yet completed due to voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Agriculturist and concerned officials to monitor the City’s dispersal program and submit progress and reconciliation to Accounting Department and submit semi-annual inventory/status reports.
observations.managementAction: Inventory/dispersal monitoring started; City Agriculturist to prepare reports; coordination ongoing.
observations.reason: Reports and reconciliations not yet fully submitted; monitoring being implemented but documentation incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
• require the General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Consolidated PIF to the GSIS and the Audit Team on or before April 30 of each year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2018-002; • ensure properties to be acquired will be fully and adequately insured; and • instruct GSO to coordinate with GSIS for insurance of properties and ensure appropriations are made.
observations.managementAction: GSO sought assistance from GSIS for insurance; consolidated PIF for CY 2021 not submitted to Audit Team; some properties identified as insurable but premiums not included in Annual Investment Plan.
observations.reason: Consolidated PIF not submitted; some insurable properties identified but not all insured; premiums not budgeted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to constantly monitor and remind all accountable officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose has been served.
observations.managementAction: City Accountant to monitor and remind AOs; constant monitoring being implemented.
observations.reason: Some advances (e.g., Intelligence and Confidential fund) remained unliquidated; liquidation still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor assign the preparation of payrolls to the HRMO, while verification of payrolls to the Accounting Office which shall validate payrolls for accuracy before payment; require HRMO to institute effective controls for payroll processing and monitor payroll deductions to ensure net take home pay is not lower than P5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: HRMO and Accounting to coordinate; payroll procedures being reviewed; HRMO to implement measures; some payrolls adjustment and monitoring done.
observations.reason: Despite measures, some employees still had take-home pay below P5,000.00 though reduced compared to prior.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CDRRMO to judiciously plan to maximize utilization of LDRRMF and accelerate implementation of PPAs; direct CDRRMO to use/transfer funds under Special Trust Fund only after five years; require City Accountant and Budget Officer to establish continuing appropriation for contracted but undelivered items (rescue truck) and disclose in Notes; require CDRRMO include PPAs charged to unexpended prior years in LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: Management amenable; City Accountant to coordinate with Budget Officer to establish continuing appropriation for rescue truck and disclose transaction in Notes.
observations.reason: Judicious planning and full utilization not yet achieved; continuing appropriation to be established; inclusion in LDRRMFIP pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor, in coordination with the responsible departments, reduce unnecessary delays in project implementation and expedite completion of not-yet started and ongoing projects; consider request for extension/retention of CY 2020 calamity fund assistance rather than immediate return; prepare required monthly and quarterly report on fund utilization and submit to NDRRMC and other oversight bodies with copies to Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed concerned officials to maximize utilization of financial assistance/grants, to look into unimplemented projects, to formulate timetable and strategies, and prepare monitoring reports. A request letter for extension/retention of two financial assistance was prepared and required reports were prepared and copy furnished Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some funds/projects still unimplemented due to late receipts and need for project planning; reporting and requests for extension prepared.

observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed all the concerned departments to strictly comply with the recommendation of the Audit Team. The newly designated GSO and the OIC Municipal Accountant are still in the process of adjusting to their newly assigned work. They are exerting effort to study and adopt the proper procedure as to supply procurement and inventory. The management will assign clerk to assist the newly designated GSO for the documentation of supplies and report preparation.  The temporary stock room of the Municipality is small and could not accommodate bulk supplies so the other departments keep or store their supplies in their offices. The on-going construction of Municipal Building Extension has proposed a warehouse or storage room.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct end users in the next budgeting period to prepare Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the budget for the ensuing year; (b) require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the latter based on submitted PPMPs, as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP); and (c) cause the Acting General Services Office (GSO) Head to (i) furnish COA copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract and (ii) send notification of the time and date of scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the newly designated GSO to comply with the recommendation of the Audit Team to furnish or submit copies of the purchase order, contracts and other documents needed before the payments of the projects or supplies and send notification before the date of deliveries.  The concerned Departments also mandate to prepare PPMP as a basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP)
observations.reason: BAC officials and the GSO are not familiar with the preparation of the required Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and the furnishing of advance copies of purchase orders and contracts to COA for review and information or notice on deliveries in the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor observe due diligence in the exercise of its basic powers, duties and functions, specifically what is emphasized under Section 444,(b) (1)(x), Article One, Chapter 3, R.A. 7160 by directing Mrs. Rufina S. Bagunu, OIC-Municipal Accountant, Mr. Florante S. Galasinao, OIC- Municipal Treasurer, to collectively cause timely submission of the paid disbursement vouchers and payrolls including related reports of         disbursements,         used accountable forms and corresponding        reports        of collections            and        remittances, financial and other reports, and monthly/quarterly/yearly financial statements within the reglementary period. Otherwise, appropriate	charges		shall	be instituted   against	the officials who are primarily responsible for violating	the		requirements	of law, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted only vouchers that are paid thru cash under the GF.

observations.recommendation:
