It is further required that timetable/deadlines set by management be strictly followed and that required reports be submitted pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386. We likewise recommended for the Municipal Accountant to compute depreciation expense on depreciable assets every year pursuant to COA Circular No. 003-007 dated December 11,
2003.

Further recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute
depreciation	expense	on depreciable assets every year pursuant to COA Circular No. 2003-007 dated December 11,
2003.
observations.managementAction: The management did not conduct physical inventory	or properties during the year.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC Municipal Treasurer to apply the civil remedies provided for by law under Sections 174 (a) and (b) and Section 175 (a) of
R.A. No. 7160 to enforce the collection of tax. Concerned officials should be reminded to
be vigilant in the issuance or execution of levy within one (1) year from the time tax became delinquent.

Also, require the Municipal Assessor to update all information pertaining to taxable real property to have an accurate basis in the set-up of RPT/SET receivables.
observations.managementAction: The	Municipal Mayor instructed	the		officials concerned	to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We	reiterated		our recommendation for the timely submission of the monthly bank reconciliation	statements together    with   the    negotiated checks and the debit and credit | memos issued by the bank to the audit team for verification.
observations.managementAction:
No	Bank
Reconciliation submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We   recommended    that    the Municipal Mayor follow the terms and conditions of the restructuring of the loan to avoid payment of accrued interests, penalties and surcharges and that the amount should be included in the succeeding budget until the loan is fully settled.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was unable to pay loan amortizations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Planning	Development Coordinator (MPDC) timely furnish the audit team the list of projects in detail under the Development fund and the status so	as to conduct validation/inspection of projects implemented   during   the   year.

Furthermore, accounts pertaining to the disbursements should also be submitted for audit within the prescribed period in order for the audit team to conduct a timely audit.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer require the Revenue Collectors concerned to deposit all their collections to the authorized	government depository bank and to stop using collections from payment of various expenses pursuant to the provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section 69 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management promised to comply with the audit recommendation starting CY2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management meticulously and adequately plan and identify the projects that are feasible and compliant with Section 3 of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1, including the accumulated savings amounting to P1,140,494.20.  

We also recommended that the Management ensure that 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized for the attainment of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The disallowed PPAs under the 20% Development Fund were not automatically reprogrammed.  At the end of the budget year, said PPAs will be allocated to other beneficial projects of the LGU.

The Management will implement projects programmed under 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

1. direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to maintain updated database on basic/minimum information on vulnerable sectors to facilitate the LGU addressing appropriately and accurately the probable measures before, during and after calamities;



2. require the MDRRMO to include in the propose LDRRMFIP which is to be approved by the MDRRMC the procurement of forklift, jack hammer, shoring, electric drill, concrete cutter, hacksaw and chisel needed for disaster preparedness, response and recovery of the municipality.  Thereafter procure through the Bids and Awards Committee, the needed tools and equipment; and

3. capacitate the MDRRMO and his staff through seminars like preparation of  Business Continuity Plan, Hazardous Materials Management and Management of the Dead and the Missing.
observations.managementAction:
In coordination with our Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, she requested all barangays to submit their updated list of families and households. The lists were encoded in the municipal database which includes vital information on vulnerable sector like the age of children, persons with disability and senior citizens.

The aforementioned disaster equipment in the audit observation is lined up to be purchased in the 1st quarter of 2021. Sufficient funds are allocated to purchase some of this equipment.


Business continuity plan, hazardous materials management and management of the missing training were not materialized due to pandemic. These proposed trainings will be undertaken in 2021.

It is provided in the updated Environment Code the segregation of waste at source. Since the sole responsibility of the municipality is the collection of residual waste, we strictly implement the “No segregation, no collection policy” in our catchment barangays. Further, there is an open-ended MOA with the Municipality of Agoo until the construction of sanitary landfill is completed and ready for use. 


Expansion of collection in all barangays may materialized on the second semester of 2021 since the construction of sanitary landfill is ongoing and the completion is 2021. The goal of the municipal government is to attain the 100% collection of residual wastes in the 24 barangays since the tipping ordinance has been approved. Moreover, while waiting for the operation of the sanitary landfill, the Management encourage the Punong Barangays not covered by the regular collection to request in our office if they have piles of residual wastes to be    collected in their respective area. This scheme was discussed in the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan duly approved by the NSWMC.

New platforms had been considered such as the use of social media to reach out our constituents. Included also in the barangay assemblies the disseminate information pertaining to solid waste management and at the same time solicit the response of the locale to better serve them during the full blast of operation.

The construction of the sanitary landfill is ongoing and targeted to finish early next year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to devise a control mechanism that will secure compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act No. 11260, thus, ensuring employee productivity and satisfying their families’ basic needs.
observations.managementAction: The Management shall not allow employees with net take home pay below the minimum to renew their loans nor avail additional loans. Moreover, those employees with net take home pay of more than the minimum shall only be allowed to avail loans with maximum monthly amortization amounting to the difference between their current net take home pay and mandated amount to maintain compliance with the above-cited RA.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

a. Strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanism of the Municipality GAD Database to ensure an updated, operational and reliable database, which serve the GAD Focal Point System in attaining an evidence-based GAD planning and policy-making. The retroactive effects of the lack of data provided by the GAD Database may be theoretical in nature, but the prospective outcome of improving the database will most likely be positive, however difficult to measure; and

b. Comply with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the establishment of a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system through the GAD M&E Team’s performance of its functions to evaluate the outcomes of       the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.
observations.managementAction:
Due to lack of updated data sources; this hinders the LGU from gathering and collating data for the GAD Database.  Despite of this, the LGU ensures that the PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget have available data to back up and support the proposal towards the attainment of the desired outcome.




The audit recommendation was noted by the LGU that the GAD M&E Team should be functional.  A monitoring tool should be devised by the team to evaluate the performance of the LGU in terms of project outcomes.

Collection of goodwill fee for new applicants has already been imposed over the years.

Drafting of Lease contracts is on-going.







The Management will comply with the recommendations given in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Management agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality exercise its authority to update the existing Revenue Code of the Municipality in conformity with the provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991, otherwise known as RA 7160 in order to generate additional income to finance programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Second public hearing in the SB Office was conducted on December 11, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the driver’s concerned to accomplish properly the Driver’s Trip Tickets for every use of government vehicle.  It must be pre-numbered, the kilometer reading, distance travelled and balances in tank must be completely and properly filled-up.

Further, the General Services Office to prepare and submit within the prescribed period the Monthly Reports of Official Travel and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report to closely monitor and control the fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction:
Compliance with the recommendation had been noted, however, the Management was not able to implement the recommendation. 



No action was made by the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Sanggunian facilitate the identification of assigned/designated common parking area/s where collection of parking fee shall be imposed. Also, the Municipal Treasurer to strictly implement the provisions of the Regulatory Fees as per provision of the Revised Municipal Revenue Code of the Municipality in order to generate more income to finance    programs            and projects beneficial to the constituents.  Likewise, the regulation of the parking area could prevent vehicular accidents along the national highway of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Sangguniang Bayan is already on the process of identifying parking areas for the generation of additional income of the municipality. 

Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer will strictly implement the provisions of the Regulatory Fees as per Revised Municipal Revenue Code of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit a Certified List of Taxpayers with stated amount due and collectible for the year to serve as a basis in drawing the JEV in setting up RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with Sections 19 (b) and 20 of the NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The LGU allocated a sum of P115,000.00 fir the purchase of PC server/workstation for the implementation of iTAX. This will enable the concerned office to prepare and furnish a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special educations taxes.
observations.reason: The management established the beginning balance based on the assessed amount instead of the certified list of taxpayers with stated amount due and collectible for the year, thus reiterated in this report under Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation:

a) prepare a Request for disposal of the unserviceable properties with COA and comply with the provisions of Section 379 of RA 7160; and



b) institute control measures by requiring the Property Custodian to issue ARE to the end user and renew at least every three years or every time there is a change in accountability of the property.

The Disposal Committee continues to physically identify and locate all the unserviceable properties to comply with the provisions of RA 7160.




The Property Custodian issues ARE/PAR to the end user of the purchased equipment.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the amount to be automatically retained with the LGU to be used for OBO’s programs and projects and the amount to be remitted to BTr representing the 5% share of DPWH.


Henceforth, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare an accounting entry for the share of 15% and 5% of the OBO and DPWH, respectively from the collection of building permit.

We have also recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office as the OBO to prepare Program of Work detailing the manner on the utilization of the 15% share from the collections of building permits, and properly observe the guideline in the preparation of the program of works.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipality to ensure that payment of goods and services are made directly to the suppliers or dealers through the issuance of checks except in cases where needed items are highly necessary and urgent or petty in nature and requires to be paid immediately whereas payments through checks would be deemed impractical in compliance with Section 2 of COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The management continues and tries to comply with the necessary laws, rules and regulations. However, the delivery of services to our constituents is the utmost priority of the LGU. The management exerted effort to lessen the number of expenditures paid through reimbursement basis. Payment through issuance of check directly to the suppliers of essential goods especially this time of pandemic is somewhat impractical. Most of the merchants require cash as a mode of payment when lockdown was implemented.
observations.reason: As per post audit of transactions, payment of goods and services were made through reimbursement even after the lockdown period, thus reiterated in this report under Observation No. 13.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that claims of various expenses should at all times be adequately supported with pertinent documents to validate the claims and ensure the legality and propriety of the transactions. Non submission of the required supporting documents may result to possible suspension or disallowance of the transactions in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that the Municipal Accountant will require the submission of the necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: As per post audit, various expenses were incurred without the necessary supporting documents, thus reiterated in this report under Observation No. 14.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management be more cautious in the implementation of infrastructure projects and strictly comply with the submission of documentary requirements in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations for the final inspection of the said projects and technical evaluation of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: The management will comply with the submission of documentary requirements in compliance with existing laws and regulations.
observations.reason: Management was not able to fully submit the various documentary requirements of the inspected projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 specifically Section 23 and 25.2 (a) and (b) of RA 9184 and comply with the documentary requirements as specified in COA Circular 2012-001 to enable the Audit Team to evaluate projects completely to establish the validity, legitimacy and propriety of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: The management will comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: The projects were inspected and an inspection report was already issued by the Regional Technical Service. Various deficiencies noted were included in this report under Observation No. 10.

observations.recommendation:
1.6. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) require the Acting General Services Office Head and the Acting Municipal
Accountant to (i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment
Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent
years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are
covered; (ii) reconcile physical count results with property records and those
maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy
reference in succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount
of unreconciled PPE account balances; and (iv) strengthen the controls on the
handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic
reconciliation of the records of the accounting, the property and actual inventory
results
b) direct the Acting General Services Office Head to (i) prepare
Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for all PPE items, starting with
current and recent years’ acquisitions until all items are covered and thereafter,
renew the AREs at least every three years; (ii) facilitate the preparation of the
inventory reports and the conduct of physical count and in the event that documents
needed to obtain costing of PPE is no longer feasible, consider the fair value thereof
as point of reference pursuant to PPSAS 17; and (iii) implement the guidelines and
procedures in the conduct of inventory-taking and preparation of the RPCPPE,
recognition of PPE found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE
items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as laid out in COA Circular
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
c) instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an initial inventory of local road
networks in the Municipality in reference to the procedures set by COA Circular
2015-008.

2.4. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) instruct the Acting GSO Head to process all purchasing concerns and keep
track of the movement of stocks on hand from receipt until issuance for accounting,
control and accountability purposes;
b) consider in the budget preparation for the ensuing year, the provision of a
storage room so that the procurement of supplies in significant quantities can be
stored to lessen the administrative cost in frequent procurements and
reimbursements;
c) require the Acting GSO Head to: (i) henceforth, prepare and submit
documents on purchases of the Municipality; (ii) prepare and maintain stock cards
for the purchased supplies; (iii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed
by the above regulation; and (iv) procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly
from the DBM-PS all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers
only upon unavailability of supplies needed; and
d) require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) ensure that all purchase of
supplies and materials are recorded under the proper inventory account; (ii) prepare
supplies ledger cards for delivered items as called for in the above quoted regulation.

4.9. We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
a) continue to exert extra efforts to trace prior years’ records so that remittances
maybe made possible and see to it that all taxes withheld during the current year are
remitted intact and immediately to the BIR within the prescribed period as required
by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007 and
coordinate with the BIR for the proper disposition of prior years’ account;
b) properly maintain subsidiary ledgers and to make proper transfer of records
in case of employees’ turnover to facilitate the immediate preparation of the reports
pertaining to disbursements payable to the BIR; and
c) adhere strictly to the prescribed period in the remittance of taxes to the BIR.

7.7. We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to submit financial reports and documents within the first ten
(10) days of the ensuing month, and strictly adhere with the requirements on the
timely submission of accounts and financial reports, pursuant to COA Circular Nos.
95-006 and 2009-006.

8.3. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct end users in the
next budgeting period to prepare Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as
basis of the budget for the ensuing year; (b) require the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the latter based on submitted PPMPs, as basis in the
preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP); and (c) cause the Acting General
Services Office (GSO) Head to (i) furnish COA copies of all purchase orders/contracts
and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract
and (ii) send notification of the time and date of scheduled deliveries to the Office of
the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006.

9.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer to present the PPAs of the 20% DF in details and
group them in accordance with its prescribed components; (b) instruct the BAC
Members, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to properly plan and strictly utilize the 20%
Development Fund to only eligible capital expenditures to ensure that the Fund is utilized
for its intended purpose; and (c) continue to maximize implementation of PPAs on a
timely fashion and in accordance with the plans and targets for the year.

10.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) strictly enforce the
Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance on the “No Segregation, No Collection” and “No
Littering” policies so that penalties may be imposed on the violators of environmental
laws; and (b) initiate intensive information drive and awareness campaign among the
residents in coordination with barangay officials on the implementation of the five
basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required
under R.A. No. 9003.

11.15. We commended the efforts of the Municipality in complying with some of
minimum standards on disaster preparedness as required in RA 10121 and NDRRM
standards. However, on other minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we
recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting DRRMO to include in
its plan, the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness
based on available resources.

12.21. We recommended that the LCE require all officials concerned to (a) stop the
practice of charging fuel, oil and lubricants expenses of private vehicles against
Municipal funds; (b) require the municipal heads of offices to limit their gasoline
expenses in the amount of their monthly Transportation Allowance; (c) require all
drivers to fill up all data needed in the trip tickets; and (d) adopt policies and
procedural guidelines on the procurements and issuance of fuel and oil limiting the
use thereof to government motor vehicles and conduct monitoring of fuel
consumption.

13.13. We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person (a) conduct a
comprehensive information, education and communication campaign on gender
issues, through direct consultation and dialogues with targeted beneficiaries and see
to it that activities to be undertaken by the LGU are truly gender responsive before
the preparation of the GAD budget; (b) make a representation with the Budget Office
during the budget deliberation seeing to it that at least the required 5% of the total
appropriations for the year is budgeted or attributed for the ensuing year; (c) initiate
efforts in the building up of a GAD Base to serve as basis for easy monitoring of
identified programs; and determine the effectiveness of the projects as implemented.

14.18. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the PHWs and
PSWs to submit the certification and other required supporting documents to the
Audit Team to substantiate their claim for hazard duty payments as required under
COA Circular 2012-001, DBM-DOH JC-1 Series of 2016 and DBM-DSWD JC-1
Series of 2018; (b) ensure the use of the correct and prescribed rates in the
computation of Hazard Duty Payments of PHWs and PSWs; (c) require all concerned
municipal officials and personnel to submit immediately the lacking documents as
required under COA Circular 2012-001 and Administrative Order 26, and comply
with the provisions of the above-mentioned regulation for COVID-19 Hazard Duty
Payments; and (d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to correct the erroneous
journal entry made for COVID-19 Hazard Duty Payments.

15.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) return of the
invalid/expired official receipts to MD One Trading and request for replacements or
issuance of new and valid official receipts; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that
all payments made by the municipality are adequately supported with complete and
valid documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.

16.27. We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to
submit the liquidation report for SAP-1; and prepare the necessary adjusting entries
for the cash received from the DSWD and the cash advances made by the Municipal
Treasurer for the fair presentation of the financial statements of the Municipality in
accordance with the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and other accounting
standards, rules and procedures; (b) Municipal Treasurer or any issuing official to
secure a copy of any issued certification or any evidence of the returned amount of
money by the unqualified recipients of SAP or for the same type of assistance for the
transparency of the Municipal Office; (c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to coordinate with the MSWDO on the unsubmitted documents as
required by DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020 and the MOA
including the additional payroll, accordingly, to this Office; and (d) MSWDO and
other concerned officials to (i) conduct further investigation regarding the claim of a
beneficiary who did not receive the SAP despite being in the DSWD and
Municipality’s payroll where a signature was reflected; and (ii) coordinate with
DSWD Field Office III on the unmatched list, synchronize the data based on the
actual payout made to aid in the posting of accurate data on the DSWD website.

We recommended that the (i) Municipal Accountant submit immediately the above-mentioned reports to Auditor’s Office and to strictly observe and comply with the deadline set for the submission of the accounts (Reports of Disbursement with Disbursement Vouchers and Reports of Collection with Official Receipts), financial statements and other financial reports as required by the above-cited regulations, and (ii) Municipal Treasurer submits to the Accounting Office the Report of Collection and Deposits and its corresponding Receipts and Deposit slips; and Report of Check Issued and its Paid Check Vouchers/Payrolls and its supporting documents so that the latter could facilitate the timely preparation of the financial reports.
We also recommended that the Management cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the employees responsible for the non-submission of the required reports until the same shall have been complied in pursuance to section 7.01 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
If warranted, institute legal action against those official and employees who continued to disregard on the rendering of their respective accounts without justifiable reasons pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GSO to:
• prepare the inventory report in conformity with the format and contents
prescribed under Sections 68 to 73, Chapter II, Volume II of the GAM for
LGUs and ensure that the same be submitted to the Auditor concerned
on or before January 31 of each year;
• completely fill up and include the necessary information on the RPCPPE to
reflect the actual result of the physical inventory count undertaken in
comparison with Accounting records; and
• prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) for all identified unserviceable properties, and initiate the
process of disposing the same in accordance with Section 79 of PD
1445 and the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-386;
and
• strictly follow the prescribed procedures in property recording and
regularly renewing the PAR.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GSO and other
concerned officials to prioritize the registration and transfer of titles of the
157 parcels of land, in compliance with Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and
Section 148 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property
Management in the Local Governments.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
• City Treasurer to prepare the Schedule of Unreleased Checks every
year-end for submission to the Accounting Office as basis of the City
Accountant in the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the
restoration of the cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and the
recognition of the appropriate liability/payable account at year-end; and
• City Accountant to ensure that only those unreleased checks at year-
end would be included in the adjusting entries for fairness in the
presentation of accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
• direct the employees from Waterworks Operation and the Office of the
City Treasurer to exert more efforts in the collections from delinquent
water consumers;
• follow up from the homeowner’s associations and private companies
the payment for production assessment; and
• initiate the necessary legal actions thru the City’s Legal Officer against
defaulting consumers in order to enforce collection of long overdue
receivables.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
• the City Accountant to set-up the amount of due and collectible RPT/
SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List
of Taxpayers submitted by the City Treasurer pursuant to Section 42,
Volume 1 of GAM for LGUs; and
• the official concerned to make or draw JEV for the adjustments
supported by schedules and explanations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to
continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the
balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs by identifying
and analyzing the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary
ledgers for adjustment, if warranted, so as to achieve reconciled account
balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to draw
the necessary accounting entries to reclassify the discount on real
property taxes paid in advance to its proper account for fairness in
presentation of accounts in the City’s financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to make
the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to correct the
erroneous recognition of expense and liability accounts in CY 2020.
For subsequent programs/projects of the City, cause the proper planning
and monitoring by the personnel/offices in charge of the programs/projects
to ensure timely implementation and completion, thus, the attainment of its
purpose/s.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned Department
Heads to –
• submit all the lacking supporting documents for the various
deficiencies noted above within 10 days from receipt of this Report;
• submit justification on the noted inconsistencies related to the supply
and delivery of food packs during the Local Social Amelioration
Program (LSAP) Distribution and the difference noted in the refunds
made in the payments of COVID-19 Health Emergency Program/LSAP;
and
• ensure that payments to suppliers/creditors be made based on
complete documents to support the claims pursuant to Section 4(6) of
PD 1445 and guidelines under COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
• City Accountant to immediately present for audit the lacking documents
within 10 days from receipt of this Report to substantiate
aforementioned BGCM transactions and ensure that all documentary
requirements for emergency purchase are complied with before
certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of the
disbursement vouchers;
• BAC, thru its Secretariat to –
a) submit justification on the noted deficiency/inconsistency on the
prices for the purchase of rice for distribution; and
b) ensure that all the procedures under negotiated procurement
(emergency cases) as required under Item 3 of GPPB Circular 01-
2020 dated April 6, 2020 are undertaken and the required documents
are prepared to prevent lapses in the procurement process.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the
Human Resource Development Officer to submit the necessary documents
within 10 days from receipt of this Report to support the grant of COVID-19
Hazard Pay to officials and employees and job order workers during the
ECQ period. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension will be issued to enforce
submission of the required documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
• reorganize the composition of City Solid Waste Management Board in
accordance with Section 12 of RA 9003; and
• instruct the City Solid Waste Management Board to assist all barangays
in the reestablishment of an individual or common Materials Recovery
Facility (MRF) and ensure that the City’s MRF is existing and fully
operational.

Likewise, we also recommended that the City Mayor take the necessary
actions to implement the plans and programs embodied in the approved
Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan andupdate the Audit Team on the
status of its proposed Sanitary Landfill.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Engineer and other
concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-
going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the
year, in compliance with the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
• Local Development Council, City Engineering Office and City Planning
and Development Office (CPDO), together with the concerned offices,
immediately look into the unimplemented projects under the 20 percent
Development Fund and take appropriate actions on the causes noted of
their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/
solutions to effectively implement development projects. Consider
formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented
projects;
• CPDO to ensure that the programs/projects/activities are properly
planned before undergoing bidding process to avoid problems during the
implementation stage that may cause delay;
• City Engineer to perform in-depth planning and detailed engineering
including thorough site inspections to anticipate and consider
interruptions that may probably arise during implementation of the
projects; and
• CPDO to enhance monitoring of implementation of projects and to
submit regularly to the City Mayor the status reports on projects’
implementation for her reference and guidance as to the appropriate
measures to be taken should there be hindrances on the optimal
utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point System to strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanisms of the Municipality’s CBMS and GAD databases through preparation of master plan indicating the timelines, process, budget and other necessary actions.  Thereafter, start the implementation in CY 2021 in order to ensure updated, operational and reliable information provided by said databases which will serve in attaining an evidenced-based planning, policy-making, and identification of beneficiaries for relief distribution and financial assistance in the future.
We recommended further that if necessary, the Management to augment the number of employees responsible for the maintenance and development of the databases, more specifically to update the data contained therein because data gathering and collating mechanism of the GAD Database and CBMS database is certainly a tedious work.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to revisit the existing plan for the procurement and distribution of relief goods and craft agreements with other suppliers that could timely cater the needs of the Municipalities in times of calamities.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Trace the remaining variance of P1,274,526.22 between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts through the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) duly supported with documents, explanations and/or computations in order to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements;
Ensure that reciprocal accounts have the same balances all the time to facilitate transfer or reversion of cash to the debtor fund/s; and
Call the attention of the Municipal Treasurer to transfer/revert the amount of P12,460,101.64 from Trust Fund to General Fund for errors made in previous years.

We have recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust best effort to locate all relevant records and documents to support the amounts booked from previous years, i.e. through available means such as confirmation and inquiry with former Accountants and other concerned officials. Thereafter, if all remedies had been exhausted, the Municipal Mayor request from the Sangguniang Bayan to revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify the liabilities in conformity with Paragraph 80 of IPSAS 1 for fair presentation in the Financial Statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the BAC Chairman impose retention money for projects whose Statement of Work Accomplished is 50% and below in conformity with Item 6.1, Annex “E” of the RA No. 9184 to safeguard the Municipality for uncorrected discovered defects and third-party liabilities by the contractors.

We have recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection on completed projects after the one-year defects liability period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if there are no defects detected or after such were repaired or corrected.
Further, for the Management to oblige the contractor to post warranty security that will address the structural defects that may be discovered from the final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2.
Lastly, for the Management to furnish the Audit Team of copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed contracts to monitor the posting of the said warranty security by the contractors.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the lacking documentary requirements immediately to the Office of the Auditor, and to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that contracts and its supporting documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enter into a contract with a reputable gasoline station for the procurement of fuel for the usage of the Municipality, upon authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan.
We also recommended that Management establish internal control guidelines on the purchase, use and documentation of fuel and motor vehicle-related transactions of the Municipality such that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished, travel and purchase of fuel are properly authorized.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWD Officer to review/re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures on AICS Program, and adopt a written policy on the determination as to the appropriateness, procedure and payment of financial assistance to individuals/families in crisis situation, considering the nature, eligibility of beneficiaries, amount of financial assistance and such other measures that would facilitate the effective and efficient implementation of the AICS Program of the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card or computer generated report to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for Real Property Taxes and Special Education Taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the local government unit to instruct the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) as a basis to record the dropping from the books of the unserviceable properties and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with the provisions of Section 379 of RA 7160; and
Institute control measures by requiring the Property Custodian to issue PAR to the end user and renew at least every three years or every time there is a change in accountability of the property.

We have recommended Management:
To instruct the Property Custodian to issue PAR to the end user and renew at least every three years or every time there is a change in accountability of the property.
To require the Inventory Committee to adopt a uniform property identification system for PPE wherein a unique Property Number shall be assigned for each item for proper identification of properties in conformity with Section 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
For the General Service Officer to prepare the Report of Physical Count in the prescribed format in accordance with Sections 69, 70, 71, 72 and 73 Volume II of GAM for LGUs and properly install a strong internal control in safeguarding the government properties.

We have recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure complete submission of necessary supporting documents such as the approved DTRs and application for leaves before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit.
That the HRMO regularly verify the DTRs and application for leaves and ensure that before the DTRs are forwarded to the Local Chief Executive for approval, the DTRs have been duly processed/ verified and initialed by the concerned Department Head.
That management may consider the usage of the biometric machine of the Municipality for the personnel to register actual and prescribed office or work hours in order to ensure correctness in the computation/payment of leave credits, salaries and wages.

We have recommended that Management instruct the Office of the HRMO and Sangguniang Secretary to issue and submit a certificate showing the number of workdays of actual service rendered in a month of the department heads and officials claiming RATA to serve as a basis in the computation of actual claims. The certification should be issued by the Office of the HRMO for department heads covered under Section 7.1 of LBC No. 103 and by the Sangguniang Secretary for those officials covered under Section 7.5 of the same Local Budget Circular.

We have recommended that payment of BAC and TWG honoraria be supported with complete documentary requirements as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 also known as the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions to support validity and propriety of the claim.

We have recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasury Office to prepare a duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Ticket to properly control the use of government motor vehicles. Further, monthly report of fuel consumption with PO slip attached be prepared for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed.
For the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for fuel consumption be supported with complete documentation in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.

We have recommended that management:
Strictly adhere to the provisions of the procurement laws, rules and regulations and ensure that all procurement were sourced from suppliers that are duly registered with the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS).
To observe the submission of the PhilGEPS Registration Number issued to suppliers as an eligibility requirement in compliance with Section 54.6 of Republic Act No. 9184 in order to promote transparency in the procurement activities of the municipality under the alternative methods of procurement.

We have recommended to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) the following:
Deficiencies noted be given immediate action and relevant documents needed be timely submitted;
Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 specifically Section 23 and 25.2 (a) and (b) and
Comply with the documentary requirements as specified in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to enable the Audit Team to evaluate projects completely, establish the validity, legitimacy and propriety of the transaction.

We have recommended that the concerned officials and employees submit immediately the documents being required for a holistic evaluation of the project by the Regional Technical Service of this Office and for the Audit Team to generate timely and relevant audit results.
It is to be emphasized herein that non-compliance on the submission may result in the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
In addition, deficiencies noted should be properly addressed to the advantage of the municipality.

We have recommended the following to Management:
Require the concerned Municipal Office to prepare the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to record any balances of donations received be costed and recorded in the books or in the registries.

We have recommended Management the following:
To require concerned officials and employees to submit the corresponding Official Receipts/Sales/Cash Invoice of the transactions mentioned above.
For the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that supporting documents are completely and properly accomplished before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No.1445 to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.
Provisions of the laws, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds should be strictly complied with.

We have recommended that the Municipality ensure that payment of goods and services are made directly to the suppliers or dealers through the issuance of checks except in cases where needed items are highly necessary and urgent or petty in nature and requires to be paid immediately where payments through checks would be deemed impractical in compliance with Section 2 of COA Circular 97-002.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents before affixing his signature in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit. Compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds is strictly recommended.

We have recommended that management exert more effort to collect these real property tax delinquencies especially those that have been long overdue in order for the municipality to augment its income that could be used for other projects beneficial to the constituents of the municipality.

observations.managementAction: The Management has already submitted the Lists/Inventory of the Local Road Network and they will designate GSO staff/clerk to help the newly designated GSO who is still adjusting his work as GSO. With the assistance and cooperation of the OIC Municipal Accountant who is also adjusting to her additional work and responsibility, they will be in charge to prepare the necessary documents and reports needed. The Municipal Engineer was instructed also to lead and continue to facilitate the inventory of the Local Road Networks and all the properties of the Municipality as required by the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
Prior years’ inventory reports are not available thus, costing of properties was not possible thus, the Acting GSO was able to conduct physical count of properties and equipment on a per office basis on items found at station.

Manpower in the Engineer’s Office are not enough to help in the conduct of an initial inventory of local roads network.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to exert effort to trace prior years’ records so that remittances maybe made possible and see to it that all taxes withheld during the current year are remitted intact and immediately to the BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to present the PPAs of the 20% DF in details and group them in accordance with its prescribed components;

instruct the BAC Members, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to properly plan and strictly use the 20% Development Fund for only eligible capital expenditures to ensure that the Fund is utilized for its intended purpose; and 

continue to maximize implementation of PPAs on a timely fashion and in accordance with the plans and targets for the year.
observations.managementAction: The Management will strictly follow the recommendation of the Audit Team as to present in detail the 20% Development Fund in PPAs and to continue to implement that plans, targets and other expenditures are part of the PPAs. All concerned departments will exert effort for the yearly formulation of PPAs to make sure that all plans and programs will be included in PPAs.
observations.reason: No consultation was made by the Local Development Council prior to the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

strictly enforce the Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance on the "No Segregation, No Collection" and "No Littering" policies so that penalties may be imposed on the violators of environmental laws; and 
 
initiate intensive information drive and awareness campaign among the residents in coordination with barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Management is strictly implementing The Local Environmental Law with the help of the Barangay Officials. The Barangay collect and segregate their waste as agreed by the body and the Central MRF (Municipal MRF) will not accept unsegregated waste. Intense and full force of the Management and the Barangay in the information drive and awareness about waste segregation but still the constituents do not comply.  The Management will now start to impose penalties on the violators of the SB Ordinance “No Segregation No Collection and No Littering” policies.
observations.reason: More and more residents are now aware of the implementation of proper segregation and disposal of waste needed in order to protect and to have a clean environment

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

(a) require the Municipal Administrator/BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness of the documents before awarding the contract; (ii) attach to the claims, all the documents, data and information needed properly signed by authorized officials before any payment is made; and 

(b) submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit Team and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on future disbursements and payments.
observations.managementAction: The Chief Executive instructed the Municipal Engineer who is also the BAC Chairman and the other concerned departments to follow and exert effort to strictly comply with the recommendation of the Audit Team as to the completeness of the documents, data and information needed before awarding the contract and payments.

observations.recommendation: We commended the efforts of the Municipality in complying with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness as required by RA 10121 and the NDRRM. However, on other minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources.
observations.managementAction: The Management has a yearly program of upgrading, improving and concreting of road low lying area, being the 4th class Municipality the Management prioritized which area will be improved and upgrade until such time all roads will be improved. The MDRRM also trained personnel or the Emergency Response Team in preparation on disaster preparedness.

The Management had already adopted/practiced the recommendation of the Audit Team as to properly accomplish the trip tickets and prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travel. The Management instructed the in charge of PO to stop issuing Gasoline Allowance or PO to private vehicles and to the Municipal Heads of Offices entitled or receiving Transportation Allowance. The Management will adopt policies and procedural guidelines on the procurement and issuance of fuel limiting to government vehicles only to minimize the fuel and oil consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit financial reports and documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month, and strictly adhere with the requirements on the timely submission of accounts and financial reports, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit Financial Documents and ensure the completeness of the documents attached to the disbursement vouchers. Due to transition period, the Accounting Department staff and the OIC Municipal Accountant are still in the process of learning, adjusting and familiarizing the work in their department in the previous months.
observations.reason: Lack of additional personnel in the Accounting Department and over workload of the Disbursing Officer resulted in the delay of payrolls for submission to Accounting Office.

observations.managementAction: The Management will comply with the recommendation of the Audit Team as to conduct bidding and prohibit the practice of splitting requisition.  All procurement activities of the municipality will be posted in the PhilGEPS regardless of the mode of procurement.

The Local Chief Executive instructed the MSWD and the GAD Focal person with the help and assistance of the other department heads to gather and have comprehensive evaluation and studies on the Gender and Development issues. Regular meeting is held to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the program and activities of GAD.
observations.reason: Lack of familiarity of management on the establishment of a GAD data base which would identify gender issues mostly on women empowerment and gender equality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant	and	the	OIC-	Municipal Treasurer to maintain General and Subsidiary Ledger and	Treasury	records respectively, for all funds and present them to the Office of the Auditor for easy verification of account balances and to record all financial transactions in the corresponding books of accounts pursuant to the provisions of Volume 11, COA Circular No. 2002-003, dated June 20, 2002 to ensure the proper accounting of all financial transactions of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management committed	to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor start the remittance of trust liabilities pertaining to the current year so as not to add to the already huge amount. Thereafter, reduce the outstanding obligation by accelerating the payment until the total obligation is fully paid. Coordinate with the concerned agencies for the condonation of penalties.

We recommended full and complete compliance with the requirements of the Full Disclosure Policy required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134, to ensure transparency on its operations and transactions.

We recommended that before payment is to be made on the transaction of the municipality, the supporting documents that are legal and proper should be attached to the disbursement vouchers. This is also to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed		the concerned officials	not to process vouchers without complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to comply with the provisions of PD 1445 with regard	to		complete documentation of all their transactions so as to avoid further suspension/disallowance in audit. The granting of financial assistance of substantial amount is not in keeping with sound financial		management considering that the cash position of the LGU cannot meet its current mandatory obligation.
observations.managementAction: The	LCE instructed	the concerned officials		to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management ensure proper presentation of the budget consistent with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under Department of Budget and Management Local Budget Circular No. 77 so that corresponding utilization be charged to proper appropriation and consequently, present fairly the financial statements of the municipality which will aid in the sound decision-making of the Management and other stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: Proper adjustments were done in the Budget Office to comply with the audit recommendation of the COA assigned in the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the affected 2018 financial statements pertaining to the afore-mentioned observations for fair presentation. We also recommended that the Management establish cut-off date as part of their annual closing activity to ensure that all transactions are properly recognized in the correct period.
observations.managementAction:
The CY 2019 Financial Statements were already restated and copies were submitted to the Auditor thereafter.

The Local Finance Committee explained that every 15th day of December will be the cut-off date of all transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

•	Direct the Municipal Accountant to: 

(1) prepare the ledgers and subsidiary ledgers supplied with complete data as prescribed by pertinent provisions of MNGAS so that relevant information may be provided to internal and external users;

(2) investigate the unreconciled prior year balances and make necessary actions in compliance with regulations, in order to reconcile the items and present fairly the financial statements; and 




(3) prepare adjusting entries for items falling under the provisions of COA Circular No. 2005-002, as shown in Finding d-ii;

•	Direct the Municipal Treasurer or the GSO, as the case may be, to: 

(1) prepare the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 57 of MNGAS for LGUs and its relevant annexes, so that minimum data required will be supplied, and so that the functions and purposes of the RPCPPE will be attained; and

•	Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the specific items of their records that were not yet reconciled on the initial reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

a) 	instruct end-users in the next budgeting period to initiate the preparation of Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the budget for the ensuing year and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the same as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and 

b)     	cause the General Services Officer (GSO) to exert effort to furnish COA copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract and sending of notification of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.
observations.managementAction: The concerned departments were already instructed to prepare PPMP as basis in the preparation of the APP. The Municipal Engineer will continue posting tarpaulin/billboards with required information in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004. The newly appointed GSO is not aware that the management needs to furnish the COA copies of PO/Contracts and all documents needed before the perfection of contract or scheduled of deliveries.
observations.reason: BAC officials and the GSO are not familiar with the preparation of the required Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and the furnishing of advance copies of purchase orders  and contracts to COA for review and information or notice on deliveries in the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management ensure proper presentation of the budget consistent with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under Department of Budget and Management Local Budget Circular No. 77 so that corresponding utilization be charged to proper appropriation and consequently, present fairly the financial statements of the municipality which will aid in the sound decision-making of the Management and other stakeholders.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the affected 2018 financial statements pertaining to the afore-mentioned observations for fair presentation. We also recommended that the Management establish cut-off date as part of their annual closing activity to ensure that all transactions are properly recognized in the correct period.

Based on the foregoing observations we have, therefore, recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (1) prepare the ledgers and subsidiary ledgers supplied with complete data as prescribed by pertinent provisions of MNGAS so that relevant information may be provided to internal and external users; (2) investigate the unreconciled prior year balances and make necessary actions in compliance with regulations, in order to reconcile the items and present fairly the financial statements; and (3) prepare adjusting entries for items falling under the provisions of COA Circular No. 2005-002, as shown in Finding d-ii;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer or the GSO, as the case may be, to: (1) prepare the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 57 of MNGAS for LGUs and its relevant annexes, so that minimum data required will be supplied, and so that the functions and purposes of the RPCPPE will be attained; and (2) obtain or locate with utmost diligence the documents on the specific land stated in Finding d-i; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the specific items of their records that were not yet reconciled on the initial reconciliation.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the payees as to the whereabouts of the long outstanding checks and thereafter, to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to make the proper adjustment on these cancelled checks, provided it is duly supported by the original check (stale) subject for replacement; and request letter of the payee for the replacement of check.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the correcting entry reclassifying Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Savings Account amounting to P15,412,430.05 to Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Time Deposits in order to fairly present the financial statements and provide the users of financial statements accurate information that may be used in their decision-making.

We have therefore recommended that the Municipality revert / refund to the 20% Development Fund the amount of P233,415.04 pertaining to the penalties incurred due to late payment of loan amortizations and will be subject for re-appropriation to finance development projects.
Likewise, we recommended that the concerned officials and employees:
Submit written justification why the said penalties were charged against the Municipal Fund for further audit verification and validation; and/or
Consult the Municipal Development Office of the Department of Finance on possible remedies for the waiver or reconsideration on the payment of penalties made, if any,
Otherwise, the concerned officials and employees should refund to the Municipality’s account the said penalties.
Thereafter, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned employees to pay promptly their loan amortizations to avoid penalties and surcharges.

Based on the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that appropriations for the above-mentioned specific expenditures must be included in the ensuing years in order to charge each transaction in the specific and appropriate account; and
grant the payment of honoraria sourced from savings of the projects, as authorized by the Sanggunian, if the collection from sale of bid documents is insufficient.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:
The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize RPT and SET Receivables and Deffered RPR and SET Thereafter, prepare necessary journal entries to reflect the real property tax collections made. Also, the Municipal Accountant prepare necessary journal entries to correct the balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts as well as the Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income in order to fairly present said accounts.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the wages of Job Order Workers under Other General Services and for the Municipal Accountant to charge the actual disbursements on the same transactions to the said account. Also, we recommended that the Budget Officer ensure that accounts used in budgetting are reflective of the account’s function and intention of the Management.
We also recommended that Management limit the duties of the Job Order Workers to emergency or intermittent work, other trades and crafts, and manual tasks which are not part of the regular functions of the agency. Moreover, the Job Order Workers should not perform functions which are part of the job description of agency’s existing regular employees.

We have recommended the Management to:
Strictly monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different programs, projects, and activities under the Republic Act No. 7171 and Republic Act No. 8240 in the local government unit concerned in accordance with the approved plan; and
Strengthen the internal control as to the monitoring, coordination, planning, and execution of Republic Act No. 7171 and Republic Act No. 8240.
Submit the necessary documents pertaining to Riprapping of Creek (Slope Protection of Irrigation Source) and Riprapping of Manacliw River (Slope Protection of Irrigation Source) in order for the Audit to perform auditorial and technical review, and also to determine the liquidated damages that the contractors of these projects should pay to the municipality.

Based on the following observations, we have recommended that Management meticulously and adequately plan and identify the projects that are feasible and compliant with Section 3 of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1, including the accumulated savings amounting to P1,140,494.20.  We also recommended that the Management ensure that 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized for the attainment of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of its constituents.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to maintain updated database on basic/minimum information on vulnerable sectors to facilitate the LGU addressing appropriately and accurately the probable measures before, during and after calamities;
require the MDRRMO to include in the propose LDRRMFIP which is to be approved by the MDRRMC the procurement of forklift, jack hammer, shoring, electric drill, concrete cutter, hacksaw and chisel needed for disaster preparedness, response and recovery of the municipality.  Thereafter procure through the Bids and Awards Committee, the needed tools and equipment; and
capacitate the MDRRMO and his staff through seminars like preparation of Business Continuity Plan, Hazardous Materials Management and Management of the Dead and the Missing.

We have recommended that Management enjoin the owners of gasoline stations within the municipality to register their businesses with the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System in order that they may participate in government procurement activities. Thereafter, the Management avail of direct purchases of fuel (gasoline, diesel, oil, and kerosene not in bulk) at retail pump price subject to the controls on fuel purchase and consumption issued by the Commission on Audit [Section 10.1 of Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Resolution No. 05-2018 dated May 18, 2018].
We also recommended that Management establish petty cash fund through Sangguniang Bayan Resolution for payment of disbursements in minimal amounts and all payments be made by authorized disbursing officer.

We have recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary requirements immediately to the Office of the Auditor to verify the validity and regularity of the disbursements.  Thereafter, Management ensure completeness of supporting documents per disbursement specifically the attachment of payroll bearing all the signatures of the recipients together with their Daily Time Records and Accomplishment Reports.
We further recommended that the Accountable Officer prepare the Report of Disbursement as supporting document for liquidation for each specific cash advance to facilitate monitoring of cash advances.

We have recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary requirements immediately to the Office of the Auditor to verify the validity and regularity of the disbursements.  Thereafter, Management ensure completeness of supporting documents per disbursement specifically the attachment of payroll bearing all the signatures of the recipients together with their Daily Time Records and Accomplishment Reports.
We further recommended that the Accountable Officer prepare the Report of Disbursement as supporting document for liquidation for each specific cash advance to facilitate monitoring of cash advances.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to submit immediately the 11 contracts amounting to P32,493,120.45, including all documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation to facilitate timely contract review by the Auditor in order to immediately communicate deficiencies noted thereon and for management to address the weaknesses. Thereafter, submit the contracts within the prescribed period in conformity with COA Circular 2009-001.
We also recommended that additional personnel be added or assigned to BAC Secretariat in order to prepare the contracts and its supporting documents and submit to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We have, therefore, recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to immediately submit the lacking documentary requirements on the afore-mentioned projects to facilitate the auditorial and technical review and to prevent issuance of suspensions and/or enforcement of other sanctions. Thereafter, submit the necessary documentary requirements to support the disbursements in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 to ascertain regularity of the transactions and ensure retention of information through proper documentation.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements in order to assess the regularity of the transactions and prevent suspensions and/or disallowances in the audit. Henceforth, ensure that necessary supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers are complete and in order prior to payment.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to devise a control mechanism that will secure compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act No. 11260, thus, ensuring employee productivity and satisfying their families’ basic needs.

We have recommended that Management efficiently and economically use government funds through conducting the Executive Legislative Agenda Formulation Workshop within La Union in conformity with Section 2 of PD 1445, Administrative Order No. 6 and COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We have, therefore, recommended that Management:
Strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanism of the Municipality GAD Database to ensure an updated, operational and reliable database, which serve the GAD Focal Point System in attaining an evidence-based GAD planning and policy-making. The retroactive effects of the lack of data provided by the GAD Database may be theoretical in nature, but the prospective outcome of improving the database will most likely be positive, however difficult to measure; and
Comply with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the establishment of a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system through the GAD M&E Team’s performance of its functions to evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.

We have recommended and the Management agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality exercise its authority to update the existing Revenue Code of the Municipality in conformity with the provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991, otherwise known as RA 7160 in order to generate additional income to finance programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit a Certified List of Taxpayers with stated amount due and collectible for the year to serve as a basis in drawing the JEV in setting up RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with Sections 19 (b) and 20 of the NGAS for LGUs.

We have recommended that the Local Government Unit to:
a) prepare a Request for disposal of the unserviceable properties with COA and comply with the provisions of Section 379 of RA 7160; and
b) institute control measures by requiring the Property Custodian to issue ARE to the end user and renew at least every three years or every time there is a change in accountability of the property.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the amount to be automatically retained with the LGU to be used for OBO’s programs and projects and the amount to be remitted to BTr representing the 5% share of DPWH.
Henceforth, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare an accounting entry for the share of 15% and 5% of the OBO and DPWH, respectively from the collection of building permit
We have also recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office as the OBO to prepare Program of Work detailing the manner on the utilization of the 15% share from the collections of building permits, and properly observe the guideline in the preparation of the program of works.

We have recommended and Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly adhere with the existing laws, rules and regulation pertaining to the proper handling, custody and disposition of funds in order to safeguard its assets. Management also agreed to stop the practice of encashing issued checks in compliance with Section 67 (3) and 69 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We have recommended that the Petty Cash Fund be strictly utilized for payment of authorized petty or miscellaneous expenses which cannot be conveniently paid through issuance of checks in compliance with Section 48 of NGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 97 – 002 in order to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
Further, we recommend Management to designate a special disbursing officer (SDO) responsible for the payment of the financial assistances and allowances of national government agency (NGA) officials and its subsequent liquidation thereof. This is without prejudice to the intention of the LGU to install other means of internal control that would adhere to the prescribed policy.

We have recommended the Municipality to ensure that payment of goods and services are made directly to the suppliers or dealers through the issuance of checks except in cases where needed items are highly necessary and urgent or petty in nature and requires to be paid immediately whereas payments through checks would be deemed impractical in compliance with Section 2 of COA Circular 97-00.
