We have recommended that Management ensure that the succeeding claims of officials and employees for registration/seminar fee on training, seminars, conferences attended shall not exceed with the prescribed rate set forth under DBM National Budget Circular No. 563 dated April 22, 2016.

We have recommended that claims of various expenses should at all times be adequately supported with pertinent documents to validate the claims and ensure the legality and propriety of the transactions. Non submission of the required supporting documents may result to possible suspension or disallowance of the transactions in audit.

In order to safeguard the interest of the government, we have recommended that the Municipality prepare and issue a Contract of Lease or MOA duly signed by the Municipality and the stallholder and must also be notarized to ensure that the contract is binding between both parties.

We have recommended that management be more cautious in the implementation of infrastructure projects and strictly comply with the submission of documentary requirements in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations for the final inspection of the said projects and technical evaluation of the contracts.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 specifically Section 23 and 25.2 (a) and (b) of RA 9184 and comply with the documentary requirements as specified in COA Circular 2012-001 to enable the Audit Team to evaluate projects completely to establish the validity, legitimacy and propriety of the transaction.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned personnel to submit the perfected contracts to the Audit Team within five working days as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We have recommended that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund book.
The following are the suggested entries:
For the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:
Under the General Fund Book:
Transfer of Unspent Current Year 		1,489,272.74
DRRM Funds to Trust Funds
Cash							1,489,272.74
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE to special trust fund account.
Under the Trust Fund Book:
Cash					 1,489,272.74
Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund				 1,489,272.74
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund.

We have recommended that the Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund by prompt and timely execution of all the planned activities during the year in order to attain its objectives in delivering quality education and alleviate financial constraints faced by the various schools.

We have recommended that the Municipality appropriate one percent (1%) of the IRA to support the related programs, projects and activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children in adherence with the provisions of Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
We have recommended that the local government unit to continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of taxes.
We have recommended that the local government unit to continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of GSIS premiums.
We have recommended that the local government unit to continue to adhere with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of Philhealth premium contributions.
We have recommended that the local government unit to continue to adhere with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums.

1.4.       We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
facilitate the conduct of physical count and the preparation of the RCPPPE and strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of the accounting, the property and actual inventory results;
instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an initial inventory of local road networks in the Municipality in reference to the procedures set by COA Circular 2015-008; and
require the Acting General Services Office Head and the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered.

4.5.    We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to exert effort to trace prior years’ records so that remittances maybe made possible and see to it that all taxes withheld during the current year are remitted intact and immediately to the BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007.

7.10.        We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to present the PPAs of the 20% DF in details and group them in accordance with its prescribed components;
instruct the BAC Members, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to properly plan and strictly use the 20% Development Fund for only eligible capital expenditures to ensure that the Fund is utilized for its intended purpose; and
continue to maximize implementation of PPAs on a timely fashion and in accordance with the plans and targets for the year.

9.3.    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct end users in the next budgeting period to prepare Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the budget for the ensuing year;
require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the latter based on submitted PPMPs, as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP); and
cause the General Services Officer (GSO) to (i) furnish the Audit Team with copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five days after the perfection of the contract; and (ii) send notification of the time and date of scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006.

10.12. We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Administrator/BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness of the documents before awarding the contract; (ii) attach to the claims, all the documents, data and information needed properly signed by authorized officials before any payment is made; and (b) submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit Team and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on future disbursements and payments.

However, on other minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources.

13.6. 	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit financial reports and documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month, and strictly adhere with the requirements on the timely submission of accounts and financial reports, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
• City Accountant and the City GSO to evaluate the causes of the material
discrepancy of P79,685,766.09 to ascertain the fairness and reliability of the PPE
balances in the financial statements; and
• City GSO together with the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical
count of the properties, reconcile the results thereof with accounting records and
submit the same to the Audit Team to ascertain the existence, validity and
correctness of the PPE as well as the reliability of balances presented in the
financial statements.
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to make detailed
evaluation and tracing of the noted unreconciled beginning balances to reflect
reliable balances of these accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City GSO to:
• prepare the RPCPPE in conformity with the format and contents prescribed by
Annex 49 Chapter II of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II and ensure that the same
be submitted to the Auditor concerned on or before January 31 of each year; and
• completely fill up and include the vital information on the RPCPPE pertaining to the
actual result of the physical inventory count undertaken for the reliable comparison of
the report with the records of the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
• Inventory Committee, in coordination with the City Engineering Office, to:
(i) conduct physical inventory of all existing local road networks in the City and
prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network
and reconcile it with the accounting records; and (ii) furnish a copy of the said
report to the Audit Team for verification and audit;
• General Services Officer to: (i) periodically maintain a complete Report on Local
Road Networks; (ii) prepare the Road Map and keep a complete Local Road
Network Property Cards of all roads and their components; and (iii) furnish copies
thereof to the concerned offices, particularly the City Accountant, as basis for
reconciliation with the accounting records;
• City Engineer to provide the City Accountant the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects;
• City Accountant to: (i) keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network and reconcile the same to the result of the
physical inventory taken at the end of each year; and (ii) provide separate
depreciation for the road and the road components;
• City Assessor to facilitate the assessment of the road network and road mapping
to identify the assessed value of the lots where the roads were constructed as
basis of the Accounting Office for the correct cost to be presented in the
registry/subsidiary ledgers; and
• City Accountant, General Services Officer and the City Engineer to: (i) be mindful
of their mandated duties and responsibilities outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-
008 to ensure that the components of Road Network System of the City are
properly identified, valued and accounted for in the books and completely
presented in the financial statements; and (ii) prioritize the immediate
identification of the local road networks for CY 2014 and prior years to reflect the
complete balance of Road Network account in the financial statements.
We further recommended the City Mayor to ensure good working coordination among
City offices to strictly follow and implement COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
• City Accountant to –
a. establish/set up at the beginning of the year the due and collectible RPT/SET
receivables and not at the end of the year based on the Real Property Tax
Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card pursuant to Section 20, MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I;
b. maintain the necessary subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET Receivables
in order to validate the balances of the accounts presented in the financial
statements and to ensure accuracy and reliability of the balances reported
monthly;
• City Treasurer to –
c. exert additional effort to trace and identify the delinquent taxes dated below
CY 2017 for proper classification and ageing of the accounts for the
appropriate actions to be undertaken thereof;
d. implement more effective, efficient and aggressive synergized tax campaign
and develop additional strategies to improve tax revenue generation and to
intensify collection of RPTs, not only current taxes but also the delinquent
taxes in order to generate more income; and
e. regularly provide the Office of the City Accountant the Report of Delinquent
Real Property Tax as the basis of the latter on the reconciliation of their
accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
• Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count of
inventories and prepare the corresponding RPCI to ascertain actual existence of
inventory items and to facilitate systematic reconciliation of records with the
Accounting and GSO. Likewise, furnish the Auditor concerned with the RPCI not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for proper evaluation thereof;
• Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Accounting Office and the GSO to
exert additional effort to determine the actual status of the cited old/beginning
balances recorded in the Other Supplies and Materials Inventory and Textbooks
and Instructional Materials for Distribution for proper accounting thereof; and
• GSO to submit the required distribution lists or proof that the recipients actually
received the inventory items distributed for the Accounting Office to prepare the
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the recognition of the appropriate expenditures.
We further recommended that the City Accountant and the City GSO strictly comply
with the regulations on proper accounting and management of all supplies and
materials in order to have reliable balances of inventories and related expenses
accounts and to safeguard the same against possible misappropriation.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to make the
necessary adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry made upon receipt of the
refund for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Assessor to undertake
appropriate action to secure Certificates of Title from the Land Registration Authority.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to cause the
submission of all lacking documents to allow in audit the purchase of lots and to
strictly comply with the documentary requirements on the procurement of land.
Likewise, we recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the City Accountant see
to it that all claims against government funds be supported with all the required
documentations and refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete
supporting documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:
• ensure that at every year-end, adjusting entries are prepared to recognize the
unexpired portion of the insurance premiums paid in advance to reflect reliable
balances of the affected accounts; and
• effect adjustment on the total insurance expense recorded in CY 2019 to
recognize as asset the unexpired portion of the insurance premiums paid in
accordance with the accrual basis of accounting.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Agriculturist and other
concerned City officials to monitor properly the City’s dispersal program and submit
the progress report and make reconciliation with the Accounting records. Also, the
City Agriculturist prepare and submit semi-annually the inventory and status of these
breeding animals to the Accounting Department for proper recognition in the books
of any increase or decrease on the quantity and value of these assets.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Chairman of the Committee on
Disposal to initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in
accordance with said rules in order not to expose these properties to further
deterioration or loss of recovery of salvage value and to generate additional income
thereof.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the City Mayor:
• require the General Services Officer to identify insurable properties of the City,
taking into consideration the current condition or serviceability of each property,
such that those which are not serviceable must not be included, and include in
their Annual Investment Plan the cost of premiums required for the insurance of
these properties; and
• instruct the City Budget Officer to appropriate funds in the annual budget for the
insurance premiums billed for all insurable property of the City; and
• ensure that properties to be acquired by the City in the future will be fully and
adequately insured in compliance with RA No. 656.
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer and
Property Custodian to prepare and submit the Consolidated PIF to the GSIS and the
Audit Team on or before April 30 of each year in compliance with COA Circular No.
2018-002 as accomplishing said form will facilitate the determination of the
properties of the City that shall be insured with the GSIS.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
• constantly monitor and remind all AOs to immediately liquidate their outstanding
cash advances as soon as the purpose for which they were granted has been
served; and
• send demand letters regularly, particularly to AOs and employees who are still in
active service, and in case of failure of liquidation despite demand, any money
due them may be withheld pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
• assign the preparation of payrolls to the HRMO, while the verification of payroll to the
Accounting Office which shall validate whether the payrolls are accurate, appropriate
and proper before they are paid; and
• require the HRMO and the City Accountant to institute effective controls for payroll
processing and monitor payroll deductions to ensure that the net take home pay will
not be lower than P5,000.00.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Human Resource Management
Officer to:
• prioritize to put in operation the City’s IAS by hiring qualified applicants for the
three vacant positions to assist in the achievement of an efficient and effective
fiscal administration and performance of City’s affairs and functions;
• publish the Notice of Vacancy regularly to fast track the selection of qualified
applicants for the 130 vacant plantilla positions and subsequent appointment
thereof; and
• consider evaluation of the performance of individuals under Job Order Contracts
to open opportunity for possible appointment to permanent/regular positions.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) direct the CDRRMO to plan judiciously the utilization of the LDRRMF and to
accelerate implementation of PPAs to achieve the goal/target set by the City for
disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management;
b) require the CDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the list of PPAs which will be
funded by the unexpended appropriation of previous years or the fund balance
under the Trust Liability-DRRM and submit the list of PPAs funded or to be funded
from the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years; and
c) direct the City Accountant and the City Budget Officer to revert back the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF totaling P5,842,398.56 to the Unappropriated
Surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a) City Engineer to require Romal Construction immediate correction of the
deficiency noted in the Construction of Child Development Center at Brgy.
Poblacion III, Sto. Tomas Batangas. Otherwise, demand payment from the
contractor the amount of P9,089.06; and
b) City Accountant to require the concerned contractors to refund the excess of
contract cost over the COA estimates amounting to P265,220.73.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in coordination with the responsible
departments, aim to reduce unnecessary delays in project implementation and strictly
effect the necessary processes to expedite the accomplishment/completion of the
unimplemented and ongoing projects to allow the early enjoyment of benefits by the
general public and the full utilization of the funds received from NGAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group
(TWG) to:
• scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower and equipment on
all projects to be awarded, not per individual project only, in cases where the
latter is the lowest bidder for several projects having similar or overlapping
implementation period. This is to oblige the winning contractor to supplement
its manpower and/or equipment or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards
or contracts;
• monitor contractors with ongoing projects with other government agencies
and to coordinate/verify with them implementation of other infrastructure
projects which may not be disclosed in the Statement of On-going and
Completed projects to avoid overlapping that may result in possible
substandard quality of work;
b) instruct the City Engineer to:
• monitor strictly and closely all projects which are implemented simultaneously
by contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be
submitted for monitoring purposes and evaluation of the quality of work and
efficiency of said contractors; and
• consider re-evaluation of estimates of project duration based on experience
from previous projects’ actual completion and reduce the stipulated contract
time, if proper. This would allow the City to implement more projects during
the year given the shorter time provided to the contractors to complete the
projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  creates  an  Inventory
Committee to conduct an inventory of road networks in  compliance  with  the
requirement of COA  Circular  No.  2015-008  and  submit  the  corresponding
reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended that  the  Local  Finance  Committee  includes  in  the
ensuing years’ budget an appropriation for the  payment  of  past-due  water
expenses amounting to P13,239,351.25. Thereafter, the  Municipal  Accountant
shall recognize in the books the payable account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

   1.  Require  the  concerned  officials  to  immediately  submit  to   the
      Accounting  Department  the  necessary  documents   to   support   the
      reclassification of completed projects  totaling  P8,941,647.29   from
      the CIP account to the appropriate PPE account; and

   2. Instruct  the  Municipal  Engineer  to  closely  coordinate  with  the
      Municipal Accountant on the submission of required documents  so  that
      the directive of Section 50, Volume 1 of  the  NGAS  Manual  is  fully
      observed, which states:


        During  the  construction  period,   agency   assets   and   public
        infrastructure shall be taken up in the books as  “Construction  in
        Progress” with the appropriate asset classification.  As  soon,  as
        the project is completed, the Construction in Progress  for  agency
        asset is closed to the appropriate asset account.

We recommended that the designated accountable  forms  officer  should
keep adequate permanent record books showing all receipts and  issuances  of
accountable forms  and  should  contain  all  the  information  outlined  in
Section 95, Volume I  of  the  Government  Accounting  and  Auditing  Manual
(GAAM).

We  recommended  that  the  Disbursing  Officer  stamps   “PAID”   all
supporting documents and provide space for the number of  the  check  issued
and date of actual payment after payment is made to prevent  the  re-use  of
the same on similar transactions thereby strengthening the internal  control
system of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should:

   1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and/or officials concerned to  issue
      demand letters to all defaulting beneficiaries;


   2. Direct officials concerned to submit immediately to the Audit Team the
      MOA of the 10 recipients  for  further  validation,  evaluation,   and
      review,


   3. Direct officials concerned to  review  the  Municipality’s  livelihood
      assistance program taking into consideration the following:


           a.    selection criteria of intended beneficiaries
           b.    evaluation of intended livelihood project
           c.      orientation    of    beneficiaries    regarding    their
                 responsibilities as recipients
           d.    reasonableness of payment scheme and amount of grant
           e.    regular monitoring of the assistance program thru required
                 submission of periodic report by focal officials/employees


   4. Instruct the  livelihood  assistance  program  focal  person  and  all
      officials concerned to immediately submit an evaluation report of  the
      status of the program.  The  LCE  is  primarily  responsible  for  all
      government resources of LGU Sto. Tomas.  Fiscal  responsibility  rests
      upon him and that includes the obligation  to  ascertain  that  public
      funds are effectively utilized and properly safeguarded from  loss  or
      wastage.

We recommended that:

   1. The MDRRM Council justify for the non-updated  LDRRMFIP  for  CY  2019
      despite prior recommendation made.


   2. Henceforth, the LDRRMFIP should be reviewed and  updated  annually  to
      include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-
      MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust
      Fund in compliance with section 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipality appropriate budget to  cover  the
increase in the wages of its personnel hired  under  job  order/contract  of
service in compliance with the prescribed minimum wage under Wage Order  No.
RB XI-20.

We recommended the following course of action:

   1. The Local  Chief  Executive  cause  the  execution  of  renewed  lease
      contract,  containing  therein,  among  others,  the  provisions   for
      transfer of lease rights, penalty for non-payment of rentals  and  the
      rights and duties of the parties;


   2.  The  Local  Chief  Executive  enforce  the  prohibitions  and   penal
      provisions in the contract;


   3. The Local Chief Executive enjoin the Local Economic Officer to keep an
      updated list of market stall lessees, noting therein  that  which  are
      still occupied by the original  lessees  or  transferred/subleased  to
      others; and


   4. The Sanguniang Bayan to reconsider the amendment of the  Revenue  Code
      of the Municipality, adjusting rental fees and examining the provision
      of the lease contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  enjoins  the  concerned
officials to ensure the annual preparation of the required reports,  conduct
of inventory of roads and submission of  the  corresponding  report  on  the
physical inventory taken to the Office of the Auditor within the  prescribed
period.

observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already conducted physical count of PPE, thus, RPCPPE was prepared and submitted to the auditor.
observations.reason:
The RPCPPE and Inventory of Local Road Networks were prepared and submitted to the Auditor’s office. However, records were not equipped to provide some of the relevant and reliable information that could be critical to the decision-making of the stakeholders. 

The audit finding was reiterated in the report.

observations.managementAction:
The adjusting /correcting entries were made as per JEV No. 2019-200-09-240 and JEV No.2019-100-09-2786. 

Depreciation expense for Building (New Evacuation Center) will be recognized after all electrical and water system will be installed.
observations.reason: Correcting entries were made as per JEV No. 2019-200-09-240 and JEV No.2019-100-09-2786.

observations.managementAction: The management is in the process preparing the necessary requirements for the request to write-off Accounts Receivables.
observations.reason: Verification revealed that some documents for write-off were already prepared by the management, however, as of this writing, there was no request for write-off received.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor requested to the Sangguniang Bayan a resolution approving the reversion of Accounts Payable to the General Fund.

Resolution No. 118 Series of 2019 was then approved and JEV No. 2019-100-12-3659 and JEV No. 2019-200-12-300 was drawn by the Municipal Accountant thereafter.
observations.reason:
Sangguniang Bayan  Resolution No. 118 Series of 2019 approved the reversion of Accounts Payable to the General Fund.

The adjusting/correcting entries were effected as per JEV No. 2019-100-12-3659 and JEV No. 2019-200-12-300.

observations.managementAction: The amount was reverted back to SEF and charged the same to GF.
observations.reason: The amount was reverted to Special Education Fund and the same amount was charged to General Fund.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer directed all collectors to remit their collections daily and intact.
observations.reason: Although the collectors remit their collections intact, the remittance was not made in daily basis.

observations.managementAction: The management signified compliance on the recommendations.
observations.reason: No action was made by the management to address the matter. The Driver’s Trip Tickets were not properly accomplished, and the Monthly Reports of Official Travel and Fuel Consumption were not submitted.

observations.managementAction: The supporting documents were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason:
The supporting documents for the financial assistance were already submitted to the Office of the Auditor. 

The financial assistance to other agencies were being directly issued to the concerned agency supported by Official Receipts and Liquidation Reports.

observations.managementAction: We are exerting effort to strictly discontinue the reimbursement basis as mode of procurement.
observations.reason: The procurement of some goods was compliant with RA 9184 although there were still some goods being procured using the reimbursement basis.

observations.managementAction: The unimplemented 16 projects programmed for the past three (3) years is subject for bidding in 2019 in order that these projects will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents. Four (4) projects were already completed.
observations.reason: There were four (4) on-going projects, five (5) are scheduled for bidding and three (3) are still for implementation.

observations.managementAction: The LGU evaluated and prepared strategies to address the hindrances noted and implemented projects through procurement of the following heavy duty equipment necessary for the preparedness, response and rescue activities of DRRM: Crawler Loader, Wheel Loader, Excavator, Boom Truck, Dump Truck.
observations.reason: The LGU implemented its LDRRM Plan through the procurement of Crawler Loader, Wheel Loader, Excavator, Boom Truck, and Dump Truck.

observations.managementAction: The unutilized balances were already transferred and utilized for the procurement of various DRRM equipment.
observations.reason: The unutilized balances of LDRRMF were already transferred to General Fund and was utilized for the procurement of various DRRM equipment.

observations.managementAction: The three (3) plantilla positions of the LDRRMO have already been created, incorporated in the FY 2020 Annual Budget and filled-up.
observations.reason: The other three (3) plantilla positions that will serve as staff of the LDRRMO have already been created, incorporated in the FY 2020 Annual Budget and filled-up.

observations.managementAction: The management agreed to implement the projects embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: No action made by the management in addressing the issue/deficiencies noted.

observations.managementAction: To address the problem on the transport of solid wastes, the Management have already procured additional garbage truck to cover all the service barangays this year 2019.
observations.reason: The LGU procured additional garbage truck in 2019 to comply with the requirements on the safe and efficient transport of solid wastes.

observations.managementAction: The management identified new road section since ROW was not resolved.
observations.reason: The LGU identified new road section and the project already pushed through.

observations.managementAction: All financial transactions were already submitted to the Auditor’s office.
observations.reason: The accounts were not submitted within the prescribed period except for the 4th quarter accounts.

observations.managementAction:
The Sangguniang Bayan is already on the process of identifying parking areas.

The Municipal Treasurer will strictly implement the provisions stated in the Revised Municipal Revenue Code.
observations.reason: There were no final designated parking areas, thus, additional income was not realized.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the:

a)	Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to periodically coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer regarding the existence of stale checks and to require that the latter shall send written notices to the concerned payees before checks become stale.

b)	Municipal Accountant is likewise advised to take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the stale checks by debiting Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable.

c)	For the double posting of checks, the Municipal Accountant should make the needed correcting entries in the books of accounts to reverse the double entry made. In the same manner, the Municipal Treasurer should cancel/remove the second posted check recorded in the cashbook and to reconcile its record with the ledger balances in order to reflect the correct balances of the account Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account of both records.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjustments were made for the stale checks and double posting of checks in the books.
observations.reason: Adjustments were made in the books under various JEVs.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Treasury Office should prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and the Report of Disbursements (RD) and to strictly adhere to the above cited provision for proper monitoring of checks and proper disbursement of cash.
observations.managementAction: Treasury Office prepares the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursement
observations.reason: RCI and RD were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer account the missing checks, prepare a corrected Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and duly accounted the same and should be presented to the Audit Team for audit whether these are cancelled or still on hand.

In addition, the Municipal Treasurer is required to prepare Report of Checks Issued (RCI) for proper monitoring of issuance of checks which shall also be recorded chronologically.

Lastly, for the management to observe sound internal control on the issuance and custody of checks to dispense with possible loss, misapplication and unauthorized use.
observations.managementAction: The five checks were already accounted and management informed that these were recorded after the cut-off date. The remaining five checks were declared cancelled and informed that these checks were not encashed due to absence of an Accountant’s Advice
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the agency explains in writing the reason why these checks were encashed/debited in the bank and took significant period of time before they were recognized in the books.

We also have recommended that the management to strictly conform to Section 43 on the Manual of New Government Accounting Manual and Section 3.3 of COA Circular 96-011 for the immediate recording of checks issued in the Reports of Checks Issued for recognition in the books. Furthermore, management should improve existing internal control and policies in processing, recognizing and recording its financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: Records were updated and compliance to recommendation were made to avoid late recording of checks issued in the books.
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Government Unit to ensure compliance with the Manual of NGAs for LGUs and COA Circular No. 97-002 in handling and maintaining the Petty Cash Fund.
observations.managementAction: Imprest Fund System is adopted in the establishment and replenishment of Petty Cash Fund.
observations.reason: Management is now implement-ing Imprest Fund System.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer should prepare a certified list of taxpayers with stated amount due and collectible for the year to serve as a basis in drawing the JEV in setting up RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report under Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the local government unit to a) comply with the provisions of Section 379 of RA 7160 and b) institute control measures by requiring the Property Custodian to issue PARs to the end user and renew at least every three years or every time there is a change in accountability of the property.
observations.managementAction: There is already an organized Disposal Committee.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report under Observation No. 2

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all officials responsible to submit inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS to ensure that all these assets are adequately insured to protect the LGU in the event of loss or damage to properties.
observations.managementAction: The management coordinates with the GSIS to estimate the amount to be set aside for the insurance cost and incorporate it to the next budget preparation.
observations.reason: Due to lack of appropriation to cover the insurance cost, the LGU’s assets were still not insured.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the claimants to attach a certification duly signed by the Human Resource Officer. We further recommend that the guidelines provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be strictly adhere.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office requires the attachment of Human Resource Officer’s Certification for every claim of Loyalty Cash Incentive
observations.reason: Based on post audit of CY 2019 transactions, the required certificate was already attached to every claim of loyalty incentive.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the requisitioning officer to sign and provide certification that the procured medicines conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition and strictly adhere to the guidelines of Executive Order No. 49 and Administrative Order No. 163, s. 2002.
observations.managementAction: Certification of the requisitioning officer were attached to every procure-ment of drugs and medicines.
observations.reason: Based on post audit of CY 2019 transactions, the required certificate was already attached to every procurement of drugs and medicines.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management stop reimbursement as a mode of procurement particularly for supplies which are necessities in the regular operations of the LGU and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and to avoid possible incurrence of irregularities. 

Furthermore, we have also recommended that the payment on regular purchase of various materials, supplies and equipment by the Municipality should be made through check in the name of the supplier/creditor.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that they discourage to reimburse all purchase ordinary and regular supplies and other expenses. Reimbursement only applies to travelling and training expenses of employees
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report under Observation No. 6

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 specifically Section 23 and 25.2 (a) and (b) of RA 9184 and comply with the documentary requirements as specified in COA Circular 2012-001 to enable the Audit Team to evaluate projects completely to establish the validity, legitimacy and propriety of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents of some projects were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Submitted supporting documents of some projects were evaluated by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the 20% Local Development Fund be optimally utilize and ensure that the programs/projects/activities are implemented fully within the time frame in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents. We further recommended that the local government unit strictly adhere with the existing laws, rules and regulations covering the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Local Development Fund to avoid possible sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: All on-going projects related to 20% Development Fund for CY 2018 were already finished and completed on the first quarter of 2019.
observations.reason: The implementation rate for CY 2019 Development Fund projects were 91.13%, thus the fund was fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU exert more efforts in intensifying its collection of delinquent accounts and apply remedies as provided under Section 174, Chapter IV of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office increased their collections by doing tax campaign.
observations.reason: Though there’s a minimal increase in the collection for CY 2019 compared to CY 2018, the Treasury Office should exert more efforts to intensify its collection of delinquent accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require (a) the Municipal Treasurer to exert best efforts to locate the stale checks; and (b) the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustment in the books on stale checks and strictly monitor outstanding checks at year end for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant already prepared the necessary adjustments in the books this February 2019 to revert the amount of the stale checks to the Cash in Bank account. Despite continues follow up and reminders on the part of the payees, no responses were received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the authorized government depository bank daily or the next banking day, pursuant to Section 69 of P. D. No. 1445 and Section 32, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS, in order to avoid possible risk of loss or misuse.
observations.managementAction: Job order personnel and Barangay Treasurers extended help during the first quarter on the collection and issuance of mayor’s permits and licenses.
observations.reason: Lack of additional personnel was the usual problem encountered especially during the 1st Quarter of the year, the busiest and peak season of collection, resulting in the delay of preparation of RCDs.

observations.managementAction:
The concerned personnel are still adjusting in their new assigned work. They are currently working on the actual physical count of the properties of the Municipality and trying to sort out their actual or the current cost since no records or lists were available especially on the previous acquisitions. 

The GSO submitted almost 80% of all the Municipal Offices supplies & equipment as BFP, PNP and Dep Ed supplies and equipment given/donated to them were accompanied with corresponding ARE. Preparing of Property Cards (PCs) and Property Plant Ledger (PPELC) by the two concerned departments are ongoing. All newly acquired properties and equipment with proper documents are already recorded. The newly appointed Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Accountant and the GSO are currently conducting physical inventory to provide a lists of all buildings and local roads newly and previously constructed. Additional personnel were hired to help in preparing of other reports and documents needed in the process.
observations.reason:
Prior years’ inventory reports are not available thus, costing of properties was not possible thus, the Acting GSO was able to conduct physical count of properties and equipment on a per office basis on items found at station.

Manpower in the Engineer’s Office are not enough to help in the conduct of an initial inventory of local roads network.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to remit directly to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), the employees’ and employer’s contributions within the first ten days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant exerted efforts to reconcile and trace the actual payrolls deducted from employees to GSIS ëBCS’or Electronic Billing Collection System and ensure the accurate GSIS premiums will be remitted on time.
observations.reason: The employee in charge of GSIS remittances failed to coordinate with the GSIS on previous year’s liabilities. However, current year payables were being remitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the prompt remittance of the withheld taxes on or before the 10th day of the following month in which the withholding was made as prescribed by the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The concerned departments are still in the process of reconciling the Due to BIR balances. They were instructed to exert more efforts to reconcile the account and to continue to remit the current withheld taxes accurately and intact.
observations.reason: Lack of awareness of LGU personnel on the strict rules and regulations on the prompt remittance of taxes.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management for being compliant with the HDMF Law, thus, we recommended that it sustains its efforts in the correct deduction of premiums and loan amortizations as well as in the timely remittance thereof pursuant to the prescribed rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is compliant on the provisions of RA 9679, on the proper deduction of HDMF contributions and repayments of loan amortizations of its personnel

The Municipal Planning and Development Office is gathering the data needed to present the PPA’s and started to follow the guidelines set forth in JMC Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM in project/program identification.
observations.reason: No consultation was made by the Local Development Council prior to the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan.

observations.managementAction: The newly designated GAD Focal Person is still adjusting in the work assigned to her and the attribution is acceptable in GAD Budget preparation.  The Focal Person and the MBO conducted  a  Budget Deliberation and  thorough study of GAD matters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

 (a) Municipal Treasurer to submit financial reports and documents to the Municipal Accountant within the prescribed period and 

 (b) the Municipal accountant to submit financial reports to COA within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month and strictly adhere with the requirements on the timely submission of accounts and financial reports, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer delegated to other personnel some of  the work of the  Disbursing Officer who is still adjusting to her new work so that she can comply on the early liquidation of payrolls.
observations.reason: Lack of additional personnel in the Accounting Department and over workload of the Disbursing Officer resulted in the delay of payrolls for submission to Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

Human Resource Officer to issue monthly biometric print outs of DTRs  as basis of employees in preparing their daily record of attendance  indicating therein their travels out of official station/field work to be duly supported with their Travel Orders or Locator Slips reflecting their actual location on field and

Municipal Accountant check payrolls to include complete documentation of proof of actual services rendered.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Officer conducted a meeting of  job order employees  reminding  them to strictly follow Civil Service Rules and Regulations in using the biometrics and in the filling up of their DTRs.  She  started to require pass slips per office to use as locator slips whenever they need to leave the office, head of offices will also be signing on the logbook handled by the Public Information Desk Officer for proper documentation of their attendance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

require enforcement of the Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance on the “No Segregation, No Collection” and “No Littering” policies such as imposition of penalties to violators of environmental laws; and 
 
initiate intensive information drive and awareness campaign among the residents in coordination with barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
The Designated MENRO stated that they are just waiting for the official review of the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan submitted last November 07, 2018. He also reiterated that they have started the use of entry pass in the MRF so that only garbage trucks with segregated garbage will be given entry pass to force barangays to collect and accept waste which already been segregated from households and commercial establishments.
  That they are now strictly enforcing the “No Segregation, No Collection” policy on garbage.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Lack of massive awareness of residents on proper disposal of waste and proper hygiene especially on their environment

observations.recommendation:
We commended the efforts of the Municipality in complying with some of the minimum standards of disaster preparedness as required in RA 10121 and NDRRM standards. 

However, on some minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

Acting-DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and 

Municipal Treasurer to transfer the CY 2018 unexpended/unobligated balance of ₱2,103,055.41 to the fund to the Trust Liability DRRM Fund with actual cash transfer pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
The Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer reported that they are already compliant with the said standards based on their approved Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan 2019-2021 certified last October 31, 2018 by the Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.

The Municipal Treasurer stated that they have transferred the amount of  ₱1,438,542.31 to the Trust Liability DRRM Fund last March 11, 2019.

The BAC is not aware that it is mandatory and they are required to submit in advance the contract/ documents to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant and the BAC Chairman are now complying with the required documentation to ensure the completeness of the documents needed before awarding of contract and checking the attached documents and signature of the officials.
observations.reason: The concerned chiefs of offices lacked adequate knowledge on the proper documentation of claims before payment is to be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require sworn statements and ITR supported by the financial statements to serve as a legal basis in the computation of business taxes as required in the Updated Revenue Code of the municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Treasurer made different strategies and required needed documents from the taxpayers as proof of the accuracy of their declared/stated gross income in the Application/Renewal of Mayors Permit. The Taxpayers are strictly interviewed as to the flow of operation and income of their business. There are instances that the ITR of the taxpayer have lower or small declared gross income when compared to the assessment made by the Treasury Staff.
The Management instructed the Municipal Treasurer to continue their practice of strict assessment of business establishments in the Municipality and to monitor the accuracy and prompt payment of Business Tax and other regulatory fees.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office lacked diligent efforts to require sworn statements and ITR in the computation of business taxes.

observations.managementAction:
a. Municipal Accountant to – The City Accountant had not completely reclassified all small value procurements.
b. General Services Officer (GSO) to – The GSO has already started issuing ICS and cancelled the ARE related to the small tangible items.
c. GSO to take immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the abovementioned unserviceable properties as required by PD No. 1445 to prevent exposure to different elements and further deterioration of its values. Not See Audit Observation No. 09 of Part II, AAR CY 2019.
d. A Committee has been created for the purpose.
observations.reason: The City Accountant had not completely reclassified all small value procurements.; Not all small tangible items have been issued ICS.; See Audit Observation No. 09 of Part II, AAR CY 2019.; The committee has not started reconciling with the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• GSO to prepare and maintain Road Networks property cards;
• Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
• Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain property ledger cards for Local Road Network and disclose the information for the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
a) GSO to prepare and maintain property cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
The GSO has already started ensuring providing the necessary information. b) Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
The accounting office is updating its Schedule of Public Infrastructures and is preparing to disclose required information in the Notes.
c) Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain property ledger cards for Local Road Network and disclose the information for the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
The City Accountant has already prepared Schedule of Public Infrastructures and disclosed information.
observations.reason: The City has disclosed information for the Local Road Network in the Notes per prescribed format but the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components were still not yet prepared/maintained; identification and segregation of road components is still in process; recording of road network projects from CY 2015 and below were being started.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
• accountable officers who were given cash advances to liquidate the same within the prescribed period and refund or return any unexpended balance to the Cashier/Collecting Officer who will issue the necessary official receipt.
• ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the above-cited rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The City complied by reminding and requiring accountable officers to liquidate outstanding advances, and ensured the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as per COA Circular No. 97-002. The Municipal Accountant implemented monitoring of liquidation and issuance of demand letters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
• continue the reconciliation of the balances in the accounting records with the records of GSIS in order to ascertain the reliability of the amount Due to GSIS account for the unremitted contributions of employer. The Municipal Treasurer is to call up the employees to reconcile and settle their outstanding loans and make a written representation with GSIS.
• effect timely and accurate remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and contributions.
observations.managementAction:
a. continue the reconciliation of the balances in the accounting records with the records of GSIS in order to ascertain the reliability of the amount Due to GSIS account: The City Accountant had already ensured reconciliation efforts and the Municipal Treasurer called up employees to reconcile and settle outstanding loans and made representation with GSIS.
b. effect timely and accurate remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and contributions: The balance of the Due to GSIS account for the unremitted contributions of P2,498,692.26 was already remitted in January 2019.
observations.reason: The City Accountant was in the process of reconciling accounting records with GSIS records; the unremitted contributions were remitted in January 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary reclassification and adjustments in the accounting records to reflect the proper accounts and ensure compliance with prescribed regulations.
observations.managementAction: The City reviewed the nature of transactions and processed reclassification and adjustments in the financial records. The Municipal Management responded that they will look into the nature of the transactions and effect the necessary reclassification of the affected accounts and will correct the misclassified accounts in the book for the charges on next transactions to fairly present the financial statements.
observations.reason: There remained items needing reclassification and adjustment to properly reflect the charges in the correct accounts; some corrections were pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary reclassification and adjustment to correct the misclassification of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant processed the necessary reclassification and adjustments to correct the misclassification of the accounts. They have reclassified the misclassified balances accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
• Municipal Health Officer to: (a) consider use of the Philippine Drug Price Reference Index (DPRI) as guide; (b) certify drugs being procured are in conformity with essential drugs list contained in the PNDF; (c) purchase medicines within PNDF; (d) submit the Procurement Plan (PPMP) or complete technical specifications.
• Municipal Accountant: ensure required documentary requirements supporting all purchases especially medicines are present.
• Bids and Awards Committee (BAC): submit detailed breakdown of ABC containing Schedule of Prices and detailed financial component of bids.
observations.managementAction:
a) Municipal Health Officer to use PNDF and DPRI as guides: The City Health Officer will use the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Volume I and the Drug Price Reference Index (DPRI) where possible. Some purchases, however, were not fully within PNDF.
b) Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents: The Accounting Office ensured required documents supporting purchases are presented.
c) BAC to submit detailed ABC breakdown: The BAC complied and submitted the required details.
observations.reason: Some purchases of medicines were still not within the PNDF; partial compliance with recommendations, subject to further validation.

observations.managementAction:
a. MPDO, BAC and others have conducted pre-procurement conferences as required.
b. The BAC is already holding pre-procurement conferences and complied with the required procedures.
c. Municipal Accountant ensured completeness of supporting documents before payment processing; distribution lists were partially submitted to the Audit Team subject to detailed validation.
observations.reason: Some distribution lists and supporting documents were submitted but subject to detailed validation and further review.

observations.managementAction:
a) BAC – required winning bidders to submit performance securities with validity periods up to final acceptance; the BAC complied.
b) Municipal Treasurer – issued Official Receipts as supporting documents and forwarded them to the Audit Team.
c) BAC Secretariat/TWG – exercised greater caution in evaluating submissions and submitted lacking documents.
d) Municipal Accountant – ensured completeness of supporting documents as required before processing payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and TWG to: (a) scrutinize the sufficiency of winning bidder’s manpower and equipment on all projects to be awarded, not per individual project only, in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder for several projects having similar or overlapping implementation period; and (b) monitor contractors with ongoing projects with other government agencies and coordinate with those involved in implementing infrastructure projects not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and Completed projects to avoid overlapping and delay in project accomplishment or substandard work. Also the Municipal Engineer and MPDO should strictly and closely monitor all simultaneous projects and prepare individual status reports.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and TWG were directed to scrutinize sufficiency of manpower and equipment and monitor contractors with ongoing projects. The Municipal Engineer and MPDO were directed to monitor projects closely and prepare individual status reports. The BAC and TWG implemented these measures in subsequent procurements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the guidelines, maximize utilization of the 20% Development Fund for implementation of projects, programs and activities so that desired services and benefits be delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay and to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for various development projects for the benefit of constituents and reported optimal utilization rate of 97.43% for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the PRAISE Committee refrain from granting certain bonuses unless supported with legal basis and required documentation; and comply with COA, DBM, CSC rules on utilization of government resources.
observations.managementAction: Municipal PRAISE Committee submitted justification and legal basis for granting PRAISE incentives and reviewed the validity of giving incentives. Management ensured that employees did not receive PRAISE bonuses in CY 2019 where legal basis was not established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management exercise prudence in utilization of resources, observe austerity measures, evaluate requests and plan programs vital to operational mandate, submit justifications for excessive expenditures, and ensure completeness of supporting documents before processing payments.
observations.managementAction: Management stated it would lessen expenses for meals and snacks, apply prudence and austerity, evaluate program requests, and submit justifications. The Municipal Accountant checked completeness of supporting documents before processing claims.
observations.reason: Some assistance and expenditures for gatherings and activities were reduced in CY 2019 compared to CY 2018 but certain activities still incurred expenditures; submitted justifications remain subject to further review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO ensure compliance with AO 04 s.2016, secure daily attendance list with photographs, submit lacking documents, open a bank account for transfer of funds for programs, prepare procurement plan for the SFP and ensure immediate liquidation and submission of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted liquidation reports and supporting documents, opened a bank account for DWSD transfers, prepared procurement plan and complied with various recommendations. They provided required documentation and made adjustments accordingly.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were submitted for review and evaluated; certain items remained pending but overall compliance improved with submission of required reports and bank account establishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce immediate settlement or liquidation of outstanding cash advances and instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor grant of cash advances and ensure no additional advances are issued unless previous ones are settled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant enforced collection and liquidation, sent demand letters, and reminded accountable officers to settle advances; monitoring systems were strengthened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer adjust the amount of fidelity bond corresponding to their total maximum accountability in succeeding renewals in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates (Annex C) of Treasury Circular No. 2-2009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer increased fidelity bond premiums of accountable officers to correspond to their maximum accountability and enforced compliance with bond requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor adhere strictly with COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant and utilization of cash advances and stop the practice of granting cash advances to non-municipal officials/employees, require submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for proper evaluation and audit otherwise transactions be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required liquidation and supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team; the practice of granting cash advances to non-municipal officials/employees was discontinued and liquidation was effected.
observations.reason: The cash advance was fully liquidated and liquidation documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to: (a) ensure allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness as presented in the LDRRMFIP contains details of projects and activities to be funded; (b) meticulously plan programs and ensure immediate implementation; and (c) coordinate with concerned Municipal Offices like BAC and Municipal Engineering Office for proper and timely implementation of scheduled projects.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO was directed to ensure allocation and inclusion of details in the LDRRMFIP; planning and coordination with implementing offices (BAC, Engineering) were undertaken and implementation of PPAs was accelerated. Management reported reprogramming/unexpended balance use for resettlement site development as per CY 2020 AIP.
observations.reason: Only part of the planned projects were implemented in CY 2019; unutilized balances were reprogrammed for CY 2020 projects; list of prior years’ charged projects needed inclusion in LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person properly monitor and report the status of implementation of GAD Plan activities and ensure that GAD funds are used solely for GAD related activities, aligned to GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point Person monitored implementation and submitted the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report showing GAD budget and expenditures for CY 2019. Some GAD activities were implemented and reported; attributed programs were undertaken.
observations.reason: Some projects in the GAD plan were implemented but some expenditures were not GAD-related; improvements in compliance were observed though certain activities remain to be aligned fully with GAD mandates.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
