observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the:        

a)    Human Resource Officer to issue monthly biometric print outs of DTRs as basis of employees in preparing their daily record of attendance and require personnel employed on job order basis to submit duly verified accomplishment reports;  and          

b) Municipal Accountant check payrolls to include documentation of proof of actual services rendered and accomplishment reports for those under JO status.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO strictly mandated all municipal employees to submit Daily Time Record/Biometric print out monthly otherwise salary will be hold as have agreed by the Management... The Municipal Accountant  also checked the completeness of the attached documents every payrolls and the submission of accomplishment reports of Job Order employees monthly

observations.recommendation:
Create a committee tasked to establish the extent of participation of all officials responsible for the processing, facilitation and/or approval of the deposits of government funds in the amount of P11,872,094.80 to a bank not among the Authorized Government Depository Banks.

The services of a lawyer be sought to institute a collection suit against the defunct Davao Cooperative Bank for the recovery of the whole amount especially the one covered by a time deposit.
observations.managementAction: Meantime, an executive order will be issued for the creation of the committee
observations.reason: Initial actions were taken by the previous administration but due to management transition as a result of 2016 National Election, those initiatives were deferred.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory of all the
properties of the Municipality including public infrastructures;
Inventory Committee upon completion of the inventory, prepare and
submit the required reports such as Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), Inventory and Inspection Report
of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System; and
Accounting and Property Officer–Designate shall reconcile their
records with the result of the inventory and investigate the differences.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions mentioned above for
fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Management request write-off taking
into consideration the guidelines set-forth under COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016 in order to fairly present the accounts
“Receivables” in the financial statements.

We have recommended to the Management request from the
Sangguniang Bayan to revert the amount of P2,769,961.29 to the
unappropriated surplus of the Municipality. Upon approval by the SB, the
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect a
reliable balance of the Payable account in the Financial Statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the
driver’s concerned to accomplish properly the Driver’s Trip Tickets for
every use of government vehicle. It must be pre-numbered, the kilometer
reading, distance travelled and balances in tank must be completely and
properly filled-up.

Further, the General Services Office to prepare and submit within the
prescribed period the Monthly Reports of Official Travel and Monthly Fuel
Consumption Report to closely monitor and control the fuel consumption.

We have recommended that Management submit the lacking
documents to avoid further issuance of Notice of Suspension and to ascertain
the propriety and validity of the expenses incurred.

We further recommended that financial assistance to be provided to
other agencies must be directly issued to the concerned agency and an
Official Receipt and Liquidation report be required from the recipient
agency.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to discontinue
adopting the reimbursement basis as mode for paying procured items and
should be adequately supported by documents to prove the validity and
propriety of the transactions. For the Department Head to procure their
office requirements based on the Annual Procurement Plan thru the general
mode of procurement which is public bidding.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) initiate
the implementation of the priority programs and projects which remained
unimplemented in alignment with the country’s development plan and in
order to uplift the lives of its constituents.

We have recommended that the LGU implement the LDRRM Plan and
utilize its budget accordingly. We further recommended that
Management, thru its MDRRMC, study and evaluate its LDRRMFIP to
determine constraints/reasons for the non-implementation of some plans,
programs and activities resulting in the failure to attain its intended
objectives. Further, for the MDRRM Office to be equipped with the
required and necessary tools and equipment necessary for the preparedness,
response and rescue activities and to ensure compliance to disaster
preparedness minimum standards for DRRM.

We have recommended the Management to transfer to the General
Fund the 2011 to 2013 unutilized balances of LDRRMF totaling
P4,595,761.85 as mandated under the above regulation in doing so,
additional funds could be used for social services after subsequent enactment
by the local Sanggunian.

We have recommended that the LGU should fill-up the mandatory
positions as required by the JMC No. 2014-001 to improve the chance of
achieving its mission to provide disaster risk resilience and climate change
adaptive municipality and the expected outcome of having a safer, resilient
and vigilant community.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to implement the
programs under the GAD Plan and Budget so that the identified gender issue
will be addressed and that the intended beneficiaries will enjoy the benefits of
the programs.

We have recommended the Management to comply with the
requirements for the transport of solid waste for the sage and efficient
collection and transport of the same.

We have recommended that the Management should fully settle the
issues on the ROW in order to finish the project and henceforth, ensure that
issues of ROW are fully resolved prior to the commencement of the
procurement process not only to support the timely implementation of
projects but most significantly, deliver the expected beneficial impact to the
constituents.

We have recommended that the Accounting and the Treasury Offices
submit immediately the financial transactions and the corresponding reports
thereof to COA Office in compliance with the existing laws, rules and
regulations.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to see to it that
all the required supporting documents be attached to the disbursement
vouchers before payment is made to avoid disallowance or suspension in the
future.

We have recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to periodically coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer regarding the existence of stale checks and to require that the latter shall send written notices to the concerned payees before checks become stale.
Municipal Accountant is likewise advised to take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the stale checks by debiting Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable.
For the double posting of checks, the Municipal Accountant should make the needed correcting entries in the books of accounts to reverse the double entry made. In the same manner, the Municipal Treasurer should cancel/remove the second posted check recorded in the cashbook and to reconcile its record with the ledger balances in order to reflect the correct balances of the account Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account of both records.

We have recommended that the Treasury Office should prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and the Report of Disbursements (RD) and to strictly adhere to the above cited provision for proper monitoring of checks and proper disbursement of cash.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer account the missing checks, prepare a corrected Report of Checks  Issued (RCI) and duly accounted the same and should be presented to the Audit Team for audit whether these are cancelled or still on hand.
In addition, the Municipal Treasurer is required to prepare Report of Checks Issued (RCI) for proper monitoring of issuance of checks which shall also be recorded chronologically.
Lastly, for the management to observe sound internal control on the issuance and custody of checks to dispense with possible loss, misapplication and unauthorized use.
We have recommended that the Local Government Unit to ensure compliance with the Manual of NGAs for LGUs and COA Circular No. 97-002 in handling and maintaining the Petty Cash Fund.

We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer should prepare a certified list of taxpayer with stated amount due and collectible for the year to serve as a basis in drawing the JEV in setting up RPT/SET Receivables.

We have recommended that the local government unit to a) comply with the provisions of Section 379 of RA 7160 and b) institute control measures by requiring the Property Custodian to issue PARs to the end user and renew at least every three years or every time there is a change in accountability of the property.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all officials responsible to submit inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS to ensure that all these assets are adequately insured to protect the LGU in the event of loss or damage to properties.

We have recommended that management require the claimants to attach a certification duly signed by the Human Resource Officer. We further recommend that the guidelines provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be strictly adhere.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the requisitioning officer to sign and provide certification that the procured medicines conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition and strictly adhere to the guidelines of Executive Order No. 49 and Administrative Order No. 163, s. 2002.

We have recommended that the Management stop reimbursement as a mode of procurement particularly for supplies which are necessities in the regular operations of the LGU and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and to avoid possible incurrence of irregularities. Furthermore, we have also recommended that the payment on regular purchase of various materials, supplies and equipment by the Municipality should be made through check in the name of the supplier/creditor.

We have recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 specifically Section 23 and 25.2 (a) and (b) of RA 9184 and comply with the documentary requirements as specified in COA Circular 2012-001 to enable the Audit Team to evaluate projects completely to establish the validity, legitimacy and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that the 20% Local Development Fund be optimally utilize and ensure that the programs/projects/activities are implemented fully within the time frame in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents. We further recommended that the local government unit strictly adhere with the existing laws, rules and regulations covering the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Local Development Fund to avoid possible sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance.
We recommended that the LGU exert more efforts in intensifying its collection of delinquent accounts and apply remedies as provided under Section 174, Chapter IV of  R.A. 7160.
We have recommended that the local government unit to continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of taxes.
We have recommended that the local government unit to continue to adhere with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums.

We have recommended that the local government unit to continue to formulate their annual Gender and Development Plan and  Budget, ensure gender mainstreaming in their policies, programs and projects and comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on Gender and Development to guarantee the promotion of public welfare services addressing gender issues.

5.13 We recommended that the (i) Municipal Accountant submit immediately the above-mentioned reports to Auditor’s Office and to strictly observe and comply with the deadline set for the submission of the accounts (Reports of Disbursement with Disbursement Vouchers and Reports of Collection with Official Receipts), financial statements and other financial reports as required by the above-cited regulations, and (ii) Municipal Treasurer submit to the Accounting Office the Report of Collection and Deposits and its corresponding Receipts and Deposit slips; and Report of Check Issued and its Paid Check Vouchers/Payrolls and its supporting documents so that the latter could facilitate the timely preparation of the financial reports.
5.14 We also recommended that the Management cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the employees responsible for the non-submission of the required reports until the same shall have been complied in pursuance to section 7.01 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
5.15 If warranted, institute legal action against those official and employees who continued to disregard on the rendering of their respective accounts without justifiable reasons pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

1.7. We recommended that management require (a) the Municipal Treasurer to
exert best efforts to locate the stale checks; and (b) the Municipal Accountant make
the necessary adjustment in the books on stale checks and strictly monitor
outstanding checks at year end for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial
statements.

2.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly require the
Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the authorized government
depository bank daily or the next banking day, pursuant to Section 69 of P. D. No.
1445 and Section 32, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS, in order to avoid possible
risk of loss or misuse.

3.29 We reiterate our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) mobilize additional personnel to facilitate the preparation of the inventory
reports and the conduct of physical count and in the event that documents needed to
obtain costing of PPE are no longer feasible, consider the fair value thereof as point
of reference and direct the Designated General Services Officer to conduct physical
inventory-taking for all PPE items, starting with current and recent years’
acquisitions until all items are covered;
(b) instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an initial inventory of local road
networks in the Municipality in reference to the procedures set by COA Circular
2015-008;
(c) require the General Services Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to
(i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards,
respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’
acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are
covered; and (ii) continue efforts in the reconciliation of inventory results with
property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items
not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking; and
(d) require the (a) Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for
each commodity/stock which should be reconciled with the stock cards maintained
by the designated Supply/Property Officer; and (b) Supply/Property Supply Officer
to (i) maintain stock cards for the supplies on hand; (ii) make use of the RIS to
document supplies issuances and prepare the monthly summary report thereon; and
(iii) conduct physical count of inventory of supplies/materials.

4.5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to remit directly to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS),
the employees’ and employer’s contributions within the first ten days of the
calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply in adherence
to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291.

5.5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to comply with the prompt remittance of the withheld taxes on or before
the 10th day of the following month in which the withholding was made as
prescribed by the BIR.

6.2. We commended the Management for being compliant with the HDMF Law,
thus, we recommended that it sustains its efforts in the correct deduction of
premiums and loan amortizations as well as in the timely remittance thereof
pursuant to the prescribed rules and regulations.

7.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to present  the
PPAs of the 20% DF in sufficient detail for easy reference and to rule out
that the appropriation was a lump sum;
(b) instruct the Municipal Development Council, the Municipal Engineer and
the MPDC to (i) closely follow the guidelines set forth in JMC Circular No.
2017-1 of the DILG and DBM in project/program identification for inclusion
in the 20% DF; and (ii) continue to implement the PPAs in a timely fashion
as targeted for the year.

8.13. We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person:
a) conduct a comprehensive information, education and communication
campaign on gender issues, through direct consultation and dialogues with targeted
beneficiaries and see to it that activities to be undertaken by the LGU are truly
gender responsive before the preparation of the GAD budget;
b) make a representation with the Budget Office during the budget deliberation
seeing to it that at least the required 5% of the total appropriations for the year is
budgeted or attributed for the ensuing year;
c) initiate efforts in the building up of a GAD data base to serve as basis for easy
monitoring of identified programs; and determine the effectiveness of the projects
as implemented.

9.6. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal
Treasurer to submit financial reports and documents to the Municipal Accountant
within the prescribed period; (b) the Municipal accountant to submit financial
reports to COA within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month and strictly
adhere with the requirements on the timely submission of accounts and financial
reports, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006.

10.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
a) Human Resource Officer to issue monthly biometric print outs of DTRs as
basis of employees in preparing their daily record of attendance indicating therein
their travels out of official station/field work to be duly supported with their Travel
Orders or Locator Slips reflecting their actual location on field and
b) Municipal Accountant check payrolls to include complete documentation of
proof of actual services rendered.

11.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) require enforcement of the Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance on the “No
Segregation, No Collection” and “No Littering” policies such as imposition of
penalties to violators of environmental laws; and
b) initiate intensive information drive and awareness campaign among the
residents in coordination with barangay officials on the implementation of the five
basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required
under RA No. 9003.

12.12. We commended the efforts of the Municipality in complying with some of the
minimum standards of disaster preparedness as required in RA 10121 and NDRRM
standards.
12.12. However, on some minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we
recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) Acting-DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the said
standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to transfer the CY 2018 unexpended/unobligated
balance of ₱2,103,055.41 to the fund to the Trust Liability DRRM Fund with actual
cash transfer pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

13.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) require the Municipal Administrator/BAC Chairman and the Municipal
Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness of the documents before awarding the
contract; and (ii) attach to the claims, all the documents, data and information
needed properly signed by authorized officials before any payment is made;
b) submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit
Team and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on
future disbursements and payments.

14.13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) instruct end-users in the next budgeting period to initiate the preparation of
Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the budget for the
ensuing year and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate
the same as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and
b) cause the General Services Officer (GSO) to exert effort to furnish COA
copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5)
days after the perfection of the contract and sending of notification of the time and
date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

15.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to strictly require sworn statements and ITR supported by the financial
statements to serve as a legal basis in the computation of business taxes as required
in the Updated Revenue Code of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all small value assets with useful life of more
than one year but costing less than P10,000.00 are properly classified and
recorded accordingly and to prepare journal entries and effect the necessary
adjustments on the PPE, Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity
accounts for a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements;
b. General Services Officer (GSO) to issue the corresponding Inventory Custodian
Slip (ICS) to all the end-users of the small tangible items and to cancel the
Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) previously issued;
c. GSO to take immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the
abovementioned unserviceable properties as required by Presidential Decree
(PD) No. 1445 to prevent exposure to different elements and further deterioration
of its values;
d. creation of a Committee to reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPE
with the property and accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare and maintain Road Networks property
cards;
b) Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the
complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
c) Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain property ledger cards for Local
Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to
Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format as provided under
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable officers who were
given cash advances to liquidate the same within the prescribed period and to refund
or return any unexpended balance to the Cashier/Collecting Office who will issue the
necessary official receipt.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the above-cited rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant in
coordination with Municipal HRMD Office to:
a. continue the reconciliation of the balances in the accounting records with the
records of the GSIS in order to ascertain the reliability of the amount Due to GSIS
in the financial statements; and
b. effect timely and accurate remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and contributions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary reclassification and adjustments in the book for the transactions
pertaining to the misclassified accounts in order to fairly present the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to make the
necessary reclassification and adjustments in order to correct the misclassification of
the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor Municipal Health Officer to:
a) consider the use of the Philippine Drug Price Reference Index (DPRI) to serve as a
guide in the efficient and sourcing of pharmaceutical products;
b) certify drugs and medicines being procured as to its conformity with the essential
drugs list as contained in the Philippines National Drug Formulary (PNDF) to ensure
delivery of effective medicines to the general public;
c) purchase medicines that are within the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF)
Volume I, current edition; provide complete product specifications for the drugs and
medicines requested to be procured and be reminded that these items should be in
their generic names, not brand names; and
d) submit the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) or the complete technical
specifications of the drugs and medicines to be bid out for these procurements which
were used as basis by the Bids and Awards Committee to come up with the ABC.

Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documentary requirements
supporting all purchases especially for medicines are present.

BAC to submit the detailed breakdown of the ABC containing the Schedule of Prices
and the detailed financial component of the bids, to establish the correct calculated
prices of the bids, which was their basis for the evaluation of bids and awarding of
contract to bidder.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Municipal Planning and Development Officer
(MPDO), Municipal General Services Officer (GSO), Municipal Budget Officer and
Municipal Accountant to ensure the conduct of pre-procurement conference
before the Procuring Entity start with the procurement process;
b. BAC to strictly comply with the provision of Section 20.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184; and
c. Municipal Accountant to:
- ensure the completeness of the supporting documents as required under Section
4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing the
payment; and
- submit to the Office of the Auditor the: (a) Distribution lists duly signed by the
actual recipients/beneficiaries as well as evidences to support the actual
distribution of gamit eskwela, books, rice, grocery items and books; (b) Activity
Design/Schedule of Activities and photos or any documents to support the
conduct of the Year-end Assessment; and (c) pictures of the purchased four (4)
units of Toyota Innova.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC):
- to ensure at all times that winning bidders post performance securities
within the prescribed period to make certain that the interests of the
government is adequately covered in the event it is established that a
winning bidder fails to perform any of its obligations under the contract;
- to require the winning bidders to submit performance securities with a
validity period up to final acceptance or after the defects liability period, and
if there are contract time extensions, require the contractor to submit the
corresponding extension of the security’s validity period.
b. Municipal Treasurer to inform the respective contractors to issue Official Receipts
for the following projects:
- Site Development of New Municipal Cemetery at San Roque;
- Completion of Multi-Purpose Covered Court at San Bartolome;
- Construction of Multi Purpose Covered Court (Phase 2) at Sta. Cruz;
- Full payment for the Completion of Multi-Purpose Covered Court at San
Juan, Sto. Tomas, Batangas;
- Completion of Multi-Purpose Hall at Brgy. Sta. Ana;
- Completion of Climate Resilient Multi-Purpose Covered at Sta. Anastacia;
- Improvement and renovation of Multi-Purpose Hall at San Agustin; and
- Construction of Climate Resilient Canal and Canal Cover at Poblacion I.
c. BAC Secretariat/TWG to be more cautious in evaluating the legal, technical and
financial documents submitted by bidders during preliminary evaluation and post-
qualification and submit all the lacking documents; and
d. Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents
as required under Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001
before processing the payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to:
a. scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower and equipment on all
the projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where
the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or
overlapping implementation period. This is to oblige the winning contractor to
supplement its manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the projects awarded
to it or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity
with the provisions of Sections 34.1 and 34.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No.
9184; and
b. monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies and
to coordinate with those involved in implementing infrastructure projects not
143
 disclosed
in the Statement of On-going and Completed projects to avoid overlapping and delay
in project accomplishment and/or substandard quality of work.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer to strictly and closely monitor all
projects simultaneously implemented by the contractors and prepare an individual
status report per project to be submitted to the Honorable Municipal Mayor for
monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work
and efficiency of the said contractors.

We recommended that the management comply strictly with the abovementioned
provisions, maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund allocated for
the implementation of various projects, programs and activities so that the desired
services and benefits which go with these projects be delivered to the constituents of
the Municipality without unnecessary delay and optimally utilize the 20%
Development Fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipality of Sto. Tomas PRAISE Committee submit
immediately the following documents in order to validate that the Municipal officials
and employees have met all the requirements warranting the grant of the
abovementioned PRAISE incentives:
a. Employees’ individual appraisal/ Evaluation reports or any documentation
recognizing suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other
personal efforts which resulted in monetary savings making the Municipal
officials and employees eligible for PRAISE award; and
b. Report detailing the savings generated during CY 2018 which prompted the grant
of these incentives and other relevant supporting documents.

We further recommended that the Sto. Tomas PRAISE Committee refrain from
granting 14th Month Incentive Bonus, Rice Allowance and Grocery Allowance to
officials and employees of the Municipality unless supported with legal basis.
Otherwise, the same will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management revisit the indicated monetary incentives in their
LGU PRAISE and carefully evaluate the validity of giving out these incentives,
esspecially the basis thereof and whether those personnel who were granted are
qualified to receive the same. Likewise, comply with the existing rules and
regulations of governing bodies like COA, DBM, and CSC and others in the utilization
of government resources.

We recommended the Management to:
a. exercise prudence in the utilization of resources intended for meals and snack
during regular meetings with local officials, employees, member organization
attended by various LGU officials and all other gatherings and activities;
b. observe the limitations or prohibitions and undertake austerity measures for such
expenditures;
c. evaluate those requests from offices/organization and judiciously plan the
program and activities that are vital in the operational mandate of the municipality
and those that are beneficial to the public; and
d. submit justification on the incurrence of excessive amount for meals, financial
assistance and other expenditures.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness,
validity and propriety of supporting documents before processing claims for
payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
(MSWDO), the Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations, specifically:
a. Follow strictly the Agreement of the Supplemental Feeding Program and secure daily
attendance list with photographs duly certified by the Day Care Worker In-charge in
the conduct of supplemental feeding as well as nutritional status reports and attach
the same to the Liquidation Report to establish propriety of the claims;
b. Immediate submission of the lacking documents discussed above to the Audit
Team for review and evaluation; and
c. Cause the opening of a bank account for the transfer of funds for DWSD Programs
which includes, among others, the Supplementary Feeding Program.

We further recommended that the MSWDO prepare its procurement plan for the SFP
according to the prescribed menu by the nutritionist or the DSWD to serve as basis for
the food purchases.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. enforce the immediate settlement or liquidation of outstanding cash advances;
and
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the granting of cash advances
and ensure that no additional cash advances be drawn or granted unless the
previous ones are liquidated/settled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that the cash advances granted to officers and employees do not exceed their
maximum cash accountability.

We also recommended that the amount of bond of the Municipal Treasurer be
adjusted corresponding to their total maximum accountability in the succeeding
renewal of their bond in accordance with the rates provided in the Revised Schedule
of Premium Rates (Annex C) of Treasury Circular No. 2-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adhere strictly with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 97-002 on the grant and utilization of cash advances and stop the
practice of granting cash advance to non municipal officials/employees.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit the liquidation report and its supporting documents to the Office of the
Auditor for proper evaluation and audit otherwise, the transactions will be
suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to:
a. ensure that the allocation for disaster, mitigation, prevention and preparedness as
resented in the LDRRMFIP contains the details of the projects and activities to be
funded, including those planned for the utilization of the previous year’s LDRRMF
as required under Section 6.2 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-
1 and Items 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
b. meticulously plan the specific programs and projects necessary in the prevention,
mitigation and preparedness of the Municipality in case of calamity and ensure the
immediate implementation; and
c. coordinate with other concerned Municipal Offices such as the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Municipal Engineering Office for the proper and timely
implementation of scheduled projects, programs and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to
properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the activities
stated in the Gender and Development Plan in order to address the gender issues.
Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD Plan are gender
responsive processes, procedures are properly followed and other factors that would
affect the implementation must be considered to avoid problems when it comes to
implementation. Likewise, utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to
specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.

We recommended that a written authority be issued to an officer who shall regularly secure bank statements from the depository bank.  Also, the Municipal Accounting Office shall exert due efforts to prepare bank reconciliation statements on time in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to have accurate and reliable presentation of cash available at the end of each month.

We recommended that management call the attention of the contractors to submit immediately the billing documents.  Upon submission, concerned offices should process the documents for immediate preparation of disbursement vouchers and issuance of checks.  After payment, the Accounting Office should adjust the account “Due to Other NGAs” effecting the completion of projects and liquidation of the fund transfers in the amount of P22,800,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO Municipal Treasurer maintain cashbook by bank accounts and by fund which shall be used to record deposits and withdrawals in compliance with the provisions of Section 27, Volume II of the Manual on New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer maintains separate cashbook for every account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer now maintains separate cashbooks by bank accounts and by fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exert more efforts in collecting the remaining balance of advance payments amounting to P892,399.20 from the concerned contractors so that funds could be used to implement other priority development projects/programs which are beneficial to constituents of the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: The management coordinated with the contractors and that a promissory note was made by the two contractors to strengthened the LGU’s claim against them.
observations.reason: The amount was reclassified to Notes Receivables representing the promissory notes made by the two contractors and that the management assured that they will exhausts all possible means to collect the remaining balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exhaust all possible means to collect the receivable from the Sto. Tomas Water District and cause the immediate transfer of the amount collected to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund book to ensure that there is a stand by fund and it will be used solely for the purpose of carrying disaster risk reduction and management activities.
observations.managementAction: The management found out the remaining uncollected amount corresponds with the provision, installation and commissioning of one unit 60 KV A Generator on 2013 and adjustments were already made.
observations.reason: The management adjusted and reclassified the amount to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account since it is still located and within the control of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that claims for Representation and Transportation Allowances and Bids and Awards Committee honoraria be supported with the required documents to avoid suspension of transactions. The ICO Municipal Accountant and ICO Municipal Treasurer see to it that the claims for RATA and BAC honoraria are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment.
observations.managementAction: Cash advance for the payment of Representation and Transportation Allowances were supported with the required documents.
observations.reason: The management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the programs/projects/activities designed under the 20% Development Fund are implemented fully within the time frame. Optimal utilization of the 20% of the IRA is also recommended in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents which is essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.
observations.managementAction:
The management implemented the associated projects in the unutilized 2017 20% Development Fund. 

The management also made a supplemental budget to realign the unused fund for Procurement of Medical equipment/Supplies for Lying in Facility.

The said unused fund form part for the Purchase of Ambulance.
observations.reason: The management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/ unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book.

Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 prescribing accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund is further recommended.

The following are the suggested entries for the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:

	Under the General Fund Book:

Transfer of Unspent Current Year 		x x x
   DRRM Funds to Trust Funds				x x x
                    Cash

To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE to special trust fund account.

Under the Trust Fund Book:

	Cash	x x x
                  Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction 
                   and Management Fund			x x x

To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund.

We further recommended that the local government unit require the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit the monthly sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The amount of Quick Response Fund from year 2013 to 2017 amounting to              P3,398,160. 58 were transferred to previously opened Special Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: The management complied with the recommendation to transfer unexpended/ unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Local School Board, adhere strictly with the existing laws, rules and regulations in the prioritization and utilization of Special Education Fund particularly for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development as provided in Sections 100(c) and 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in order to ensure an effective educational development within the locality.
observations.managementAction: Construction projects were prioritized before charging any expenses to SEF to adhere to the utilization guidelines
observations.reason: Post audit of SEF transactions revealed that the expenses charged to SEF  adhered to the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairman of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), appropriate one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the LCPC in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and  DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. We further recommended that all of the members of the council be encouraged to participate in the preparation of Local Development Plan and Local Investment Plan for Children and other reports in order to have more accurate basis in determining the needs of children and other children related issues that needs immediate attention and action.
observations.managementAction: The management provided an appropriation for LCPC PPAs.
observations.reason: The management complied with the recommendation to appropriate an amount to LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned personnel submit GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report be submitted to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period as required under Item V of COA Circular No. 2014 – 001 to provide the Audit Team ample time to verify the LGU’s compliance with existing laws and regulations on GAD in the implementation of GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget was submitted to DILG Pangasinan Provincial Office and was approved on May 21, 2018. GAD Accomplishment report was submitted to appropriate authorities.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report were submitted to COA Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all of the projects funded by fund transfers from the National Government be implemented within the time frame and require the employees concerned to faithfully supervise and monitor the construction activities, including but not limited to the inspection of works to ensure that the construction is completed as planned in order to deliver timely the essential benefits of the projects to constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Projects funded by LGSF-AM and Excise tax were already implemented.

Management assured that strict monitoring will now be implemented.
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendations.

observations.managementAction:
Bids and Awards Committee will ensure that all procurements made by the agency will adhere with all the standards set forth by RA 9184 and its IRR.

The management informed that some projects have no ten percent retention money and no warranty security submitted. Thus, they will require the contractors to submit warranty security and provide a surety bond callable on demand in lieu of ten percent retention money.
observations.reason: On-going implementation of the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the supporting documents being required for further evaluation of the COA ROI Technical Evaluation Services and Audit Team and in order to avoid suspension in audit. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should ensure the completeness of supporting documents before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payment of transactions related to the concerned infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Completion of the supporting documents required is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that management furnish the Office of the Auditor the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity and post signboards and/or public notices in the project site in compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-04 and in order for us to validate the infrastructure projects on time.
observations.managementAction: The management furnished the Office of the Auditor the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award.
observations.reason: The management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned personnel to submit the copies of perfected contracts to the Audit Team within five working days as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order to allow the Audit Team to perform effective review and generate timely and relevant audit results. Administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 4 of the same circular against the concerned employee be imposed upon unjustified failure of compliance.
observations.managementAction: The management assured that copies of perfected contracts will be submitted within five working days.
observations.reason: Some contracts were submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management release the share of the barangays from the basic real property tax earnings on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with the provision of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 also know and cited as the Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the barangays to use the additional income for development projects which are beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management will release Barangay shares on RPT on the prescribed period and intensify collections.
observations.reason: Some release of shares to Barangays from the Basic Real Property Tax earnings conforms to the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the concerned officials and employees to submit the required documents. The ICO - Municipal Accountant and ICO - Municipal Treasurer ensure the completeness of supporting documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit. Compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds is strictly recommended. 

	We further recommended that the ICO Municipal Treasurer mark the paid vouchers and supporting documents in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of government funds and avoid the risk of double payment or reuse of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Treasurer requires the concerned officials and employees to submit the necessary documents. In the absences of the required documents, supplemental documents were submitted.
observations.reason: Post audit of transactions revealed that there is no marks on the paid vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor observe due diligence in the exercise of its basic powers, duties and functions, specifically what is emphasized under Section 444,(b) (1)(x), Article One, Chapter 3, R.A. 7160 by directing the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer, to collectively cause timely submission of the paid disbursement vouchers and payrolls including related reports of disbursements, used accountable forms and corresponding reports of collections and remittances, financial and other reports, and monthly/quarterly/yearly financial statements within the reglamentary period. Otherwise, appropriate charges shall be instituted against the officials who are primarily responsible for violating the requirements of law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to maintain General and Subsidiary Ledger and Treasury records, respectively, for all funds and present them to the Office of the Auditor for easy verification of account balances and to record all financial transactions in the corresponding books of accounts pursuant to the provisions of Volume 11, COA Circular No. 2002-003, dated June 20, 2002 to ensure the proper accounting of all financial transactions of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:

The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger and prepare the schedules to support the balances of the above trust liabilities;

The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to cause the prompt remittance to the BIR the taxes withheld from employees and suppliers/contractors to avoid accumulation of penalties and surcharges against the Municipality.

The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the employee’s master list with GSIS records. Cause the prompt remittance of the GSIS compulsory contributions of both employee’s and employer’s share as well as the loan repayments withheld in accordance with RA No. 8291 to avoid accumulation of two (2) percent penalties and other surcharges and likewise to avoid suspension of all loan privileges.

The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit the premium contributions and loan repayments to PAG-IBIG and PHILHEALTH to avoid imposition of penalties and surcharges and to avail of the corresponding benefits.

The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit the LGU shares due the Province and barangays in a regular/timely manner. Funds from this source could be utilized for the timely implementation of projects for the benefit of the barangays and the Province of Isabela as a whole.

We recommended that management reconstitute the Inventory Committee to regularly conduct a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s property to establish the existence of the PPE. It is further required that timetable/deadlines set by management be strictly followed and that required reports be submitted pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386. We likewise recommended for the Municipal Accountant to compute depreciation expense on depreciable assets every year pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) No. 17.

We recommended full and complete compliance with the requirements of the Full Disclosure Policy required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134, to ensure transparency on its operations and transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to apply the civil remedies provided for by law under Sections 174 (a) and (b) and Section 175 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 to enforce the collection of tax. Concerned officials should be reminded to be vigilant in the issuance or execution of levy within one (1) year from the time tax became delinquent.

Also, require the Municipal Assessor to update all information pertaining to taxable real property to have an accurate basis in the set-up of RPT/SET receivables.

Likewise, deputize Barangay Treasurers to collect taxes on real property located within the barangay to intensify tax collections.

We recommended that before payment is to be made on the transaction of the municipality, the supporting documents that are legal and proper should be attached to the disbursement vouchers. This is also to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare regularly bank reconciliation statements and to submit to the audit team for verification.

We reiterate our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to comply with the provisions of PD 1445 with regard to complete documentation of all their transactions so as to avoid further suspension/disallowance in audit. The granting of financial assistance of substantial amount is not in keeping with sound financial management considering that the cash position of the LGU cannot meet its current mandatory obligation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor follow the terms and conditions of the restructuring of the loan to avoid payment of accrued interests, penalties and surcharges and that the amount should be included in the succeeding budget until the loan is fully settled.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) furnish timely the audit team the list of projects in detail under the Development fund and the status so as to conduct validation/inspection of projects implemented during the year. Furthermore, accounts pertaining to the disbursements should also be submitted for audit within the prescribed period in order for the audit team to conduct a timely audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all their collections to the AGDB, in compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to stop using collections for the payment of various expenses of the LGU and to maintain petty cash fund for payment of petty cash operating expenses.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the authorized government depository bank daily or the next banking day at the latest to protect funds from possible loss.
observations.managementAction: The Management transferred few Job Order Employees to the Office of the Treasurer to assist and help to lessen permanent employee’s workload thus resulting   to accurate and on time submission of   reports        and   regular intact deposit     of       collection.
observations.reason: The 1st quarter of every year is the busiest period because it is the peak season of Tax Collection resulting to the delay of preparation and submission our Collectors and Liquidating Officer’s Report of Collection and Deposit.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have already prepared adjusting and reconciling entries for the unresolved difference of the ledger and cashbook. Amounting to      ₱675,581.07
observations.reason: Needed more time to reconcile and verification of the accuracy of the cashbook and ledger balances and the Bank Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Executive:   

a)      require the General Services Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) continue efforts in the reconciliation of inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking.   

b)     direct the Designated  General Services Officer to prepare Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for all PPE items, starting with current and recent years’ acquisitions until all items are covered and thereafter, renew the AREs at least every three years;    

c)   instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an initial inventory of local road networks in the Municipality in reference to the procedures set by COA Circular 2015-008; and     

d)  mobilize additional personnel to facilitate the preparation of the inventory reports and the conduct of physical count and in the event that documents needed to obtain costing of PPE is no longer feasible, consider the fair value thereof as point of reference pursuant to PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: The municipality followed the recommendation of the Audit Team. The Local Chief Executive created committee on   Inventory   and included each department head in inventory taking by office. The GSO updates the posting of the ledger cards of the Property and Equipment of the Municipality. She also prepares the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) form if necessary and attached copy to the   Disbursements Voucher. The three departments concerned (Office of the Municipal Engineer, Accountant & General Service Officer) are   currently conducting physical inventory of the local road networks in the Municipality The Management hired J.O. employees in the Office of the Engineer and General Service Office to help in facilitating the actual physical count and preparation of inventory reports and other documents needed to obtain costing of PPE which is no longer feasible and the fair value.
observations.reason: The newly designated General Services Officer is still adjusting in her new assigned work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, thru the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, exert conscious efforts to improve the timeliness of the submission of accounts, reports and transactions to the COA.
observations.managementAction: The two concerned departments have already followed the recommendations of the Audit Team to submit on time the financial documents needed. They have agreed the deadline of the submission of all the vouchers to the Acting office and the date that all records must be reconciled from, the cashbook and ledger and the date for submission to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The workload of the two concerned department were not properly delegated to their staff resulting to delay of works in the two departments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board:  

a)      continue to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization in reference to the abovementioned rules and regulations, maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprogramming, if necessary; and    

 b) Submit to the Audit Team the required documentations as enumerated above for further audit action; improve compliance on the documentation of claims to establish their regularity as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant checked and verified the completeness of the attached documents every SEF voucher submitted.  The Local School Board with the assistance of the Municipal Budget Officer prepared the SEF budget in accordance of the existing rules and regulatory requirements in budget preparation, allocation or realignment or reprogramming if needed.

The Local Chief Executive instructed all the concerned departments to strictly comply with the recommendation of the Audit Team. The newly designated GSO and the Municipal Accountant are currently adopting the proper procedure as recommended by the Audit Team in procurement, purchasing, issuance of supplies and the updating of the stock cards. The Municipality build temporary stock room near the office of the GSO though it is small and could not accommodate bulk supplies so other departments keep or store their office supplies in their offices. But this year we have a proposed bigger stock room included in the new municipal building to be constructed within the year.
observations.reason: Limited space for stock room and office personnel in GSO Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:  

a)    continue with the practice of remitting amounts due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 and               

b)       continue   to coordinate with the GSIS to reconcile previous years’ balance appearing unpaid in the books at year end.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer continues the practice of remitting GSIS premiums on time and still in the process of coordinating and reconciling with the GSIS regarding the unreconciled GSIS premiums but up to this date they cannot locate or find their differences. According to GSIS Account Specialist the current remittances of the Municipality is accurate, complete and updated. The ERF handler always monitor the accuracy of our remittances and the   unreconciled amount was from previous years transactions.
observations.reason: They exerted much effort to reconcile and coordinate with the GSIS regarding the unreconciled balance.

observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records as to withholding taxes and remitting intact the BIR withholding taxes. They are still in the process of reconciling the unremitted balances of BIR.
observations.reason: Still in the  process of reconciling and locating the Due to BIR balance

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:               

a)   require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to present  the PPAs of the 20% DF in sufficient detail for easy reference and to rule out that the appropriation was a lump sum;           

b) instruct the Municipal Development Council, the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to (i) closely follow the guidelines set forth in the JMC Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 of the DILG and DBM in project/program identification for inclusion in the 20% DF; and (ii) continue to implement the PPAs in a timely fashion as targeted for the year.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and the Municipal Engineer were already instructed to follow the guidelines set forth in the JMC Circular No. 2017-1. The MPDC   already   prepared    PPA’s       for the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:               

a)    improve enforcement of the Sangguniang  Bayan Ordinance on the “No Segregation, No Collection” and “No Littering” policies such as imposition of penalties to violators of environmental laws; and       

b)      initiate     intensive information drive and awareness campaign among the residents in coordination with barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Management is gradually enforcing the Local          Environmental   Law with the help   of   the barangay officials which presently responsible in the collection solid waste in their respective Barangays, They were instructed to   inform all their constituents   to segregate their waste. Otherwise, their unsegregated garbage will not be collected. We will enforce all barangays to have their own MRF for their recyclables and biodegradable. The central MRF also serve as transfer   station   will        only         accept non-biodegradable, special and residual waste.
observations.reason: Limited barangay resources and  man power/personnel for implementation of the Sangguniang Ordinance

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Social Services Officer:                     

exert efforts to conduct a survey on the actual needs of children in a manner which allows the child to participate and to express himself/herself freely;               

encourage the participation of children, thru their responsible parents, in the preparation of the plan/ program, policy formulation and implementation related to juvenile justice and welfare; and                   

make     a proper representation during the budget preparation on the need to adhere closely to the required allocation of 1% of IRA for the LCPC for the ensuing year or if not feasible, identify or attribute other programs of the Municipality where the ultimate beneficiaries are the same under the LCPC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive               

instruct end-users in the next budgeting period to initiate the preparation of Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the budget for the ensuing year and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the same as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP);  

cause the General Services Officer (GSO) to exert effort to furnish COA copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract and sending of notification of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006; and                

require     the Municipal Engineer or the Project Engineer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 specifically in posting the required information on tarpaulins/billboards to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people.
observations.managementAction: The GSO starting to collate or files copies of Purchase Order/Contract  and other documents needed  to be submitted to Auditor Team before the perfection of projects . She is also instructed to send notification to the Auditor the time and date of deliveries of items or goods. The concerned departments are instructed to prepare PPMP that could be a basis in the preparation of APP. The Municipality thru the newly appointed Municipal Engineer is posting tarpaulin/billboards with required information in compliance in COA Circular 2013—004.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person:     

conduct a comprehensive information, education and communication campaign on gender issues, through direct consultation and dialogues with targeted beneficiaries and see to it  that activities to be undertaken by the LGU are truly gender responsive before the preparation of the GAD budget;     

make a representation with the Budget Office during the budget deliberation seeing to it that at least the required 5% of the total appropriations for the year is budgeted or attributed for the ensuing year;                                 

initiate     efforts in the building up of a GAD  Base to serve as basis for easy monitoring of  identified programs; and determine the effectiveness of the projects as  implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted GAD Forum to gather information related to GAD to be their basis in GAD Budget preparation. They invited DEP ED, PWD’s and 4P’’s representative during the Budget preparation and deliberation. The Management followed the  Audit Team recommendation that at least 5% of the Total Budget Appropriation is budgeted for GAD projects

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:  

require the Municipal Administrator/BAC Chairman and the Municipal     Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness of the documents before awarding the contract;  and (ii) attach to the claims,  all the documents, data and information needed properly signed by authorized officials                    before any payment is made;     

submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit Team and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on future disbursements and payments;  and                         

return the unutilized balance of the fund transfer to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: The concerned departments followed the Audit Team recommendations that before awarding the contract and payments made all documents needed and attached are complete, accurate and verified by the Accountant. The unutilized balance of the fund transfer are return to the source agency and the municipal accountant  submit all the required documents to the Audit Team on the prescribed time

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider hiring additional accounting staff to:                      

maintain the Special Accounts in the General Fund; and    

prepare the corresponding schedules to support the financial statements which will serve as tools in informing Management in evaluating the viability of the economic enterprise operated by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is now in the process of preparing separate account of the Economic Enterprise, although the income derived from economic enterprise in the Office of the Treasurer and Accounting has already a separate ledger.  The Municipal Accountant have already trained her three (3) staff members and delegated particular assignment among them so that the preparation and submission of the needed Financial Reports would not be delayed.
observations.reason: The concerned department  is not aware on the need of separate account of the economic enterprise

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the General Services Officer instruct all drivers concerned to prepare and submit Driver’s Trip Ticket by affixing all data needed in the document, Monthly Report of Official Travel sand Report of Fuel Consumption and other required procedures in adherence to the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption.
observations.managementAction: The Management have already implemented/practiced the Audit Team Recommendation to attach and fill up the Drivers Ticket Trip per P.O. Issuance.  The concerned departments strictly monitored the issuance of P.O. and the purpose and place visited to minimized fuel expenses or consumption.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the   Municipality sustain its efforts in complying with some of minimum standards of disaster preparedness as required in RA 10121 and NDRRMC standards.                 However, on some minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we recommend  that the Local Chief Executive direct the:                                 

Acting-DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and   

The Municipal Treasurer to transfer the CY 2017 unexpended/ unobligated balance of ₱2,741,815.51 to the fund to the Trust Liability DRRM Fund with actual cash transfer pursuant to COA Circular No. No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LGU reviewed all the plans so as to    be       parallel   to the minimum standard set by the NDRRM Council particularly on disaster. We are properly coordinated with all the Barangay Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Committee before, during and after the disaster.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LGU officials continue with their efforts to effectively implement programs/ projects sourced out fund transfers from other government agencies.  However, we recommend that the DSWD Officer in charge of the TODA Livelihood Kabuhayan Project coordinate with officials and beneficiaries of the organization on how to settle unresolved issues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continue to monitor and implement the projects/programs and the proper procedure and documents needed. With regards to the unresolved issues of TODA it is unfortunate that when you conducted interview there is already set of new officers in which the consultation of the project proposal came from the previous set of officers. The tricycle parts and supplies are now in the custody of the new set of officers and ready for sale.

observations.recommendation:
1. exercise Recording and monitoring and documentation process was already discussed with the Municipal Office
2. draw the necessary accounting entries to correct/adjust the error inadvertently committed to fairly present the financial statements;
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The management tried its best to comply on the timely submission of required accounting and financial reports within the prescribed period. The concerned officers to look into the root cause of the delay in the preparation and submission of the financial reports; and make possible the early submission of the same.
observations.reason: The amount is yet to be adjusted and payment in advance, the Municipality is yet to present documents to correct the error

observations.recommendation: 1. direct the Office of the General Services Officer (GSO) to consolidate weekly the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for supplies and materials issued using the proper Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the Accounting Office for recording issuances in the books of the agency; 2. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to record the expenditures using appropriate accounts; 3. instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute cost of the supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method based on the RIS and SSMI submitted by the OIC-GSO for the eventual preparation of the JEV to record issuances to present correct inventory and appropriate expense account balances.
observations.managementAction:
The SSMI is being consolidated and submitted to the Accounting Office together with the original copy of the RIS for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the expenditures using appropriate accounts;
The Municipal Accountant implemented the use of moving average method as basis to record inventories.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of gathering the documents to complete the supporting transactions

observations.recommendation: 1. conduct physical inventory and prepare the RPCPPE and reconcile results of the count with the property and accounting records; 2. properly classify the computer development cost taken up in CY 2015 amounting to P3,461,110.80 in the Construction in Progress (CIP) - Infrastructure Assets account as of December 31, 2017 instead of Intangible Asset - Computer Software account; 3. create an Inventory Management Committee to conduct complete physical inventory taking; 4. properly classify the equipment found during the inventory taking that should be reclassified to Technical and Scientific Equipment and to reflect the correct account classification based on the new accounts prescribed by COA; 5. reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting records; and 6. submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant implemented necessary adjustments to correct the error in recording the development cost of computer program to fairly present the financial statements; The Municipal Management created the Inventory Management Committee and conducted the inventory committed on July 2018; The Municipal Accountant implemented adjustments to record inventories.
observations.reason: Deficiencies were still noted in audit in the inventory reports submitted by the Municipal GSO as mentioned in finding no. 1

observations.recommendation: 1. Municipal Accountant to recognize liabilities with supporting documents and strictly comply with the provisions of the law; 2.
