Municipal Treasurer to notify the creditors about their claims and require them the presentation of documents to support their claim within a grace period of five (5) calendar days. If no claim has been made until the grace period, require the Accountant to revert the payables for a fair presentation of the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Budget Office to refrain from processing supporting transactions and to complete the documents. The Municipal Treasurer sent notifications to the suppliers to provide the necessary information.
observations.reason: Two suppliers were not notified due to their lack of presentation of necessary information. The Accounting Office is still in the process of gathering the documents to support the transactions.

observations.recommendation: 1. compel the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Social Worker to reconcile the List of Beneficiaries as against the Family Profile record and submit justification/explanation on the discrepancies noted; 2. direct the Sanggunian Bayan to pass a resolution creating a task force who will handle the eviction of the informal family settlers/inhabitants near the railroad to the relocation site considering that the target dates for the relocation was March 2015 and the housing units were completed almost two years ago; 3. define the duties and functions of the task force to fast tract clearing of the railroad area including the dissemination on the availability of affordable housing loan project to facilitate occupancy of the housing units; 4. require the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages on the uninstalled water closets and faucets until such time the said items have been installed by the contractor and deduct the same from the retention fee, when necessary; and 5. require the concerned Contractor to undertake the necessary repair of the deficiencies and completion of unfinished works noted during the ocular inspection to prevent further damage pursuant to Section 62.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and Municipal Social Worker coordinated with the Sanggunian Bayan for the creation of task force; The Municipal Accountant sought the Engineering Office regarding the application of the damages to the suppliers. The concerned contractor was required to undertake the necessary repairs.
observations.reason: Task Force is still on-going. The Engineering Office did not recommend the application of the damages to the suppliers. The contractor did not make the necessary repairs; the task force is yet to be created.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive processes, procedures are properly followed and other factors that would affect the implementation must be considered to avoid problems when it comes to implementation. Likewise, utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: Noted. The Municipality implemented measures to monitor and report on GAD activities.
observations.reason: Similar finding was noted in CY 2018 audit reiterated in finding no. 19

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor create an Inspectorate composed of representative from the offices of the Accounting, Treasury, Budget and General Services in order to strengthen internal control to reduce the possible risk of manipulation in the procurement of goods. Further instruct the General Service Officer to direct personnel concerned not to affix their signature on the lower portion of the canvass sheet which is specifically for the signature of supplier only.
observations.managementAction: Creation of the Inspectorate team to conduct inspection of deliveries of supplies, equipment, food staff, drugs and medicines and others of the Municipality of Sto. Tomas was created on April 18, 2018 and was headed by Ms. Preciousa V. Balota – Exec. Assistant III. The GSO was directed and the canvass sheet practice was remedied.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the timely submission of the required accounting and financial reports within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant tried its best to comply on the timely submission of the required accounting and financial reports within the prescribed period. The concerned officers to look into the root cause of the delay in the preparation and submission of the financial reports; and make possible the early submission of the same.
observations.reason: Incomplete submission of liquidation reports as required. The concerned personnel of the agency would like to seek consideration for delayed submission as stated in their response to the AOM.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor direct the assigned Agency department head/s or personnel to fast tract the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) of the CY 2016 Annual Audit Report, and every year thereafter to facilitate monitoring and validations of the actions taken by the LGU on the related audit observations and recommendations, and eventually to submit a validation report to the Regional Office IV-A, for monitoring purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to submit the AAPSI to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor direct the assigned AAPSI to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to submit copies of all PPAs to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Management Fund for the quarter ending December 31, 2017 on March 14, 2018 and for the succeeding quarters in CY 2018.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Fund for the quarter ending December 31, 2017 on March 14, 2018 and for the succeeding quarters in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: 1. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and concerned personnel of the General Service Office (GSO), to submit copies of the perfected contracts together with the required supporting documents and notice of delivery to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006; 2. BAC Chairman or Municipal General Services Officer, as the case maybe, to monitor the submission of the above-mentioned documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Secretariat submitted copies of the perfected contracts to the Auditor within the prescribed period. The Municipal General Services Officer monitors the submission of the above-mentioned documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.reason: Some contracts were furnished beyond the 24 hours/ five day period; review and validation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Management should intensify efforts to collect the rental receivables from the delinquent lessees.  In case of persistent refusal to settle their bills, enforce civil remedies provided for under Sections 169 and 174 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Sending of Demand Letters to collect receivables
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2018, the account increased to P2,409,224.77

observations.recommendation:
The stockroom at the Municipal Health Center should be renovated in accordance with DOH standards.  Likewise, a qualified employee shall be designated as a property custodian.  

Henceforth, all acquisitions of drugs and medicines whether procured by the Municipality or donated by other agencies shall first be accounted for as inventory and subsequent issuance thereof shall be supported with sufficient documents in order to record the issuance and to have fair presentation of the financial statements at year-end.

Fast track the implementation of the projects/ programs/ activities funded out by Fund Transfers.  Also, the unspent balance of the projects/ programs/ activities shall be returned to the Source Agency as required by laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, the LGU did not fully implement PPAs funded by fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: Management should exercise prudence and due diligence in accepting donated drugs and medicines to avoid being tagged as a conduit of wasted government properties and funds.

We hereby reiterate our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sanggunian Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Appropriation Budgets.

It is also recommended that a study should be made whether the available manpower can handle engineering jobs in proportionate to the various programs, projects and activities included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan.

Management withhold the amount of liquidated damages incurred by the Contractor from the final billing without prejudice to the imposition of other legal remedies, if evidence warrants, as mentioned in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.

Management should send letter of demand to delinquent taxpayers.  In case of persistent refusal to settle their bills, enforce remedies provided for under Sections 169 and 170 of RA No. 7160.

Moreover, the present administration should set the tone in addressing the increasing amount of uncollected revenues by appropriating a fraction of its revenues to payment of water bills totaling P5,070,392.75.
observations.managementAction:
Series of Demand Letters and Notice of Disconnection were sent to the subscribers. Monitoring Reports will be updated by the meter readers for consolidation with supporting documents for purposes of requesting write off to the Local Auditor.

To be included in the next budget year.
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2018, there is uncollectible amounting to P22,556,780.

observations.recommendation: Monitor the accomplishment of the Committee charged with the duty of ensuring the titling of the acquired lots in order to avoid legal repercussions that may arise in case of failure to transfer ownership to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Pertinent documents were already secured and endorsed to the Committee who will facilitate the transfer of ownership.
observations.reason: It would take a longer period to process the titles of the said lots.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV reclassifying the unserviceable assets to the “Other Assets” account.

Create a Committee primarily to dispose of the unserviceable properties totaling P9,515,470.85 through public auction, pursuant to the provisions of law.
observations.managementAction: To finalize the physical inventory of properties for the first semester of 2017 and dispose the unserviceable properties through public auction before the year ends.
observations.reason: On-going inventory to include the additional unserviceable properties identified in 2016 and 2017.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Treasurer should ascertain the possibility on the collection of all the outstanding loans receivable by sending again demand letters to all beneficiaries with outstanding balance.  If after doing so, the results prove futile, request from the Commission on Audit an authority to write-off the accounts, submitting the result of the demand letters, as proof of collection efforts done.  It is only after the COA has approved the request that the account may be dropped from the books of the LGU.

However, for those who are still in the government service, especially those in the local government of Sto. Tomas, make arrangement with the employee concerned for the settlement of their accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management commits to exert extra efforts to be able to collect the said receivable.
observations.reason: Demand letters were already sent to some debtors

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions mentioned above so as to present fairly the presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management to continue with the conduct of physical inventory.
observations.reason: No records were available for basis in computing the depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, we required management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Management to comply with the submission of lacking documents.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no. 14 of this audit report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to prioritize the implementation of an effective and efficient waste disposal facility as required under the above-mentioned regulation to protect the environment and the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management is in the process of developing their sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no. 13 of this audit report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor observe due diligence in the exercise of its basic powers, duties and functions, specifically what is emphasized under Section 444,(b) (1)(x), Article One, Chapter 3, R.A. 7160 by directing the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer, to collectively cause timely submission of the paid disbursement vouchers and payrolls including related reports of disbursements, used accountable forms and corresponding reports of collections and remittances, financial and other reports, and monthly/quarterly/yearly financial statements within the reglamentary period. Otherwise, appropriate charges shall be instituted against the officials who are primarily responsible for violating the requirements of law, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to maintain General and Subsidiary Ledger and Treasury records, respectively, for all funds and present them to the Office of the Auditor for easy verification of account balances and to record all financial transactions in the corresponding books of accounts pursuant to the provisions of Volume 11, COA Circular No. 2002-003, dated June 20, 2002 to ensure the proper accounting of all financial transactions of the municipality.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:

The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger and prepare the schedules to support the balances of the above trust liabilities;

The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to cause the prompt remittance to the BIR the taxes withheld from employees and suppliers/contractors to avoid accumulation of penalties and surcharges against the Municipality.

The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the employee’s master list with GSIS records. Cause the prompt remittance of the GSIS compulsory contributions of both employee’s and employer’s share as well as the loan repayments withheld in accordance with RA No. 8291 to avoid accumulation of two (2) percent penalties and other surcharges and likewise to avoid suspension of all loan privileges.

The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit the premium contributions and loan repayments to PAG-IBIG and PHILHEALTH to avoid imposition of penalties and surcharges and to avail of the corresponding benefits.

The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit the LGU shares due the Province and barangays in a regular/timely manner. Funds from this source could be utilized for the timely implementation of projects for the benefit of the barangays and the Province of Isabela as a whole.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management reconstitute the Inventory Committee to regularly conduct a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s property to establish the existence of the PPE. It is further required that timetable/deadlines set by management be strictly followed and that required reports be submitted pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386. We likewise recommended for the Municipal Accountant to compute depreciation expense on depreciable assets every year pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) No. 17.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended full and complete compliance with the requirements of the Full Disclosure Policy required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134, to ensure transparency on its operations and transactions.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that before payment is to be made on the transaction of the municipality, the supporting documents that are legal and proper should be attached to the disbursement vouchers. This is also to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to comply with the provisions of PD 1445 with regard to complete documentation of all their transactions so as to avoid further suspension/disallowance in audit. The granting of financial assistance of substantial amount is not in keeping with sound financial management considering that the cash position of the LGU cannot meet its current mandatory obligation.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) furnish timely the audit team the list of projects in detail under the Development fund and the status so as to conduct validation/inspection of projects implemented during the year. Furthermore, accounts pertaining to the disbursements should also be submitted for audit within the prescribed period in order for the audit team to conduct a timely audit.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all their collections to the AGDB, in compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to stop using collections for the payment of various expenses of the LGU and to maintain petty cash fund for payment of petty cash operating expenses.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the:  

(a) Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for each commodity/stock which should be reconciled with the stock cards maintained by the designated Supply/Property Officer; and 

(b) Supply/Property Supply Officer to (I) maintain stock cards for the supplies on hand; (ii) make use of the RIS to document supplies issuances and prepare the monthly summary report thereon; and

 (c) conduct physical count of inventory of supplies/materials.
observations.managementAction: The  Management instructed the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Supply Officer to maintain and reconcile the stock Index Cards and prepare the required documents in the issuance and supplies requisition
observations.reason:
Stocks and supplies ledger cards were not yet maintained. On September 29, 2017, through Executive Order No. 22-2017,  Ms. Maria Lourdes M. Sulit was designated as Acting  General Services Office Head. She is presently familiarizing herself on her duties.

The GSO committed to prepare stock cards, RIS and monthly summary reports on supplies.


The Municipal Treasurer is presently exerting efforts to locate previous years’ inventory reports as a guide in the preparation of the CY 2017 inventory taking.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Health Officer and the concerned department to make an annual procurement plan on medicine and programs to avoid emergency purchases and to make public bidding to all procurements.
observations.reason: Procurements made were thru shopping below the threshold of ₱100,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents forming part thereof pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.  Considering that the subject deficiency had been repeatedly discussed in prior years’ report, there is a compelling reason that the recommended course of action and corrective measure offered be given serious consideration by Management.
observations.managementAction: The Management have complied in submitting or furnishing the Audit Team copies of the purchase order, contracts and other documents needed before the payments of the projects. The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned departments to submit copies of the above stated documents before the payments of their supplies which is applicable to all disbursements small or big amount.
observations.reason: To date, management failed to furnish COA copies of Purchase Orders, contracts and other documents which will provide necessary information on the agency's day to day operations and for reference in the post audit of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) instruct the GSO to prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report for unserviceable properties for their eventual disposal; b) direct the Accounting and Inventory Committee to properly classify all the PPE per account to determine the variances in each account record; and c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the donated vehicles based on the fair value of the vehicles at the time of donation and reclassify the program design for the Municipal Data and Disaster Management System to the proper account Computer Software.
observations.managementAction: The GSO already prepared and submitted the Report for Unserviceable Property on February 9, 2018. Accounting and GSO committed to work on the reconciliation of unserviceable properties for disposal; No reports of donated vehicles submitted yet by the Accounting Department. Thus, the Municipal Accountant had not yet recorded the donated vehicles based on their fair value at the time of donation and not yet reclassified the program design for the Municipal Data Network and Disaster Management System to the proper account Computer Software.
observations.reason: No Disposal yet of unserviceable properties was made.

observations.recommendation:
a.	Submit comments relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies noted in the bidding process.  Likewise, the members of the Bids and Awards Committee should adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA No. 9184.

b.	Initiate the recommendation to the GPPB the blacklisting of the Contractor without prejudice to the immediate refund of 15% mobilization expenses amounting to P585,000.00 from the Contractor plus liquidated damages pursuant to Section 68 of the Revised IRR, RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal mayor rescinded the contract, nevertheless, still allowed the contractor to pursue the construction works without further notice.

observations.recommendation:
Create a committee tasked to establish the extent of participation of all officials responsible for the processing, facilitation and/or approval of the deposits of government funds in the amount of P11,872,094.80 to a bank not among the Authorized Government Depository Banks.

The services of a lawyer be sought to institute a collection suit against the defunct Davao Cooperative Bank for the recovery of the whole amount especially the one covered by a time deposit.
observations.managementAction: For the meantime, an executive order will be issued for the creation of the committee
observations.reason: Initial actions were taken by the previous administration but due to management transition as a result of 2016 National Election, those initiatives were deferred.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the management the following:
Conduct physical inventory of all existing properties of the municipality as required under Section 124 of NGAS Manual;
Submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor’s office not later than January 31 of each year; and
Maintain property cards duly reconciled with the accounting records pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the same manual.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions mentioned above so as to present fairly the presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended and the management agreed to prepare the following journal entry to properly classify the accounts mentioned above for fair presentation of the financial statements, to wit:
Seaport System                                       19,574,537.48
Government Equity                               19,574,537.48

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements as December 31, 2017 so as the necessary adjustments to reconcile the books of the agency and the bank are made and to present fairly the Cash in Bank balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended and the management agreed to record the financial assistance as “Receivables” and monitor the utilization of such release by requiring the liquidation report and its supporting documents from the recipient, in doing so the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be evaluated.

We have recommended and the management agreed to exhaust all remedies to collect the receivables such as but not limited to the service of demand letter.  After exhaustion of all remedies to collect, the management is enjoined to request write-off taking into consideration the guidelines setforth under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to fairly present the accounts “Receivables” in the financial statements.  We recommend further that the OIC-Municipal Accountant monitor the grant and liquidations of cash advances and fund transfers of NGAs/LGUs.

We  have recommended and the management agreed to review and reconcile their records to establish the accounts payable for which no actual claim had been filed or which is no valid contract or Purchase Order nor proof of delivery and/or rendition of service; and to recommend the reversion of the accounts payable for approval by the Sanggunian Bayan.

We have recommended and the management agreed to limit the practice of serving meals and snacks to various visitors of the municipality and observe prudence in utilizing government funds.  In doing so, additional funds could be used for programs and projects to promote the great welfare of the constituents.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to discontinue adopting the reimbursement basis as mode for paying procured items and should be adequately supported by documents to prove the validity and propriety of the transactions.  For the Department Head to procure their office requirements based on the Annual Procurement Plan thru the general mode of procurement which is public bidding.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to revisit the organizational structure of the municipality so as to create positions which are essential in the delivery of public service to the constituents and to lessen the number of JOCs.  We have further recommended to the Local Chief Executive submit certification that the activities/services cannot be provided by regular and permanent personnel to prove the validity and legality of the transactions.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to comply with the regulations cited above so as not to result to penalties and surcharges which are personal liabilities of the officers and employees having control of the disbursement of income payment.
We have further recommended to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to look into the reasons why there were over-remittances.

We have recommended and the management agreed to transfer to the General Fund the 2011 and 2012 unutilized balances of LDRRMF totaling P2,971,844.40 as mandated under the above regulation in doing so, additional funds could be used for social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

We have recommended and the management agreed to require every barangay the mandatory segregation of solid waste and to prioritize the implementation of an effective and efficient waste disposal facility as required under the above-mentioned regulation to protect the environment and the constituents.

It is recommended that management strictly comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, the management is required to submit the lacking documents noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to implement the priority programs and projects which remained unimplemented inorder to uplift the lives of its constituents.

We have recommended and the management assured the audit team on compliance with the quality design standards in the preparation of plans and specifications in the construction of FMR to promote road safety to all road users and to prolong the useful lives of the FMR.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to supervise and monitor market collections.  For an effective operation of the market, income collected should be sufficient/enough to cover its maintenance.
We have further recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant maintain a special account of the economic enterprise of the municipality as required under the above mentioned regulations to determine whether the income generated from the market operation is sufficient to meet its operating cost.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to see to it that all the required supporting documents be attached to the disbursement vouchers before payment is made to avoid disallowance or suspension in the future.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the ICO - Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that management exhaust all possible means to collect the receivable from the Sto. Tomas Water District and cause the immediate transfer of the amount collected to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund book to ensure that there is a stand by fund and it will be used solely for the purpose of carrying disaster risk reduction and management activities.

In view of this, we have recommended that management exert more efforts in collecting the remaining balance of advance payments amounting to P892,399.20 from the concerned contractors so that funds could be used to implement other priority development projects/programs/activities which are beneficial to constituents of the local government unit.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit ensure that all of its insurable assets, properties and interests are insured with the General Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System in compliance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as the “Property Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973 and Administrative Order No. 33 dated August 25, 1987 by identifying all its insurable assets, submitting an inventory report of insurable assets to the System, setting aside in its appropriation the amount needed for premiums and remitting the premiums to the System within the prescribed period to avoid unnecessary losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, assets and properties brought by fortuitous events and guarantee immediate repair or replacement and uninterrupted delivery of public service.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit comply with the provisions of Section 379 of Republic Act 7160 on disposal of unserviceable properties in order not to mislead the readers on the actual condition of its properties.

We have recommended that management furnish the Office of the Auditor the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the project/program/activity and post signboards and/or public notices in the project site in compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-04 and in order for us to validate the infrastructure projects on time.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Local School Board, adhere strictly with the existing laws, rules and regulations in the prioritization and utilization of Special Education Fund particularly for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development as provided in Sections 100(c) and 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in order to ensure an effective educational development within the locality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned personnel to submit the copies of perfected contracts to the Audit Team within five working days as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order to allow the Audit Team to perform effective review and generate timely and relevant audit results. Administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 4 of the same circular against the concerned employee be imposed upon unjustified failure of compliance.

We have recommended that management require the concerned officials and employees to submit the required documents. The ICO - Municipal Accountant and ICO - Municipal Treasurer ensure the completeness of supporting documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit. Compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds was strictly recommended.
We have further recommended that the ICO Municipal Treasurer mark the paid vouchers and supporting documents in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of government funds and avoid the risk of double payment or reuse of supporting documents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the supporting documents being required for further evaluation of the COA ROI Technical Evaluation Services and Audit Team and in order to avoid suspension in audit.
We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should ensure the completeness of supporting documents before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payment of transactions related to the concerned infrastructure projects.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that claims for Representation and Transportation Allowances and Bids and Awards Committee honoraria be supported with the required documents to void suspension of transactions. The ICO Municipal Accountant and ICO Municipal Treasurer see to it that the claims for RATA and BAC honoraria are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Health Officer/requisitioning officer to sign and provide certification that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition to support the local government unit’s procurement for drugs and medicines. We have further recommended that the guidelines provided by Executive Order No. 49 and Administrative Order No. 163, s. 2002, be strictly adhered to in order to avail the local government unit of safe, efficacious and quality drugs at affordable cost and also to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended that the ICO Municipal Treasurer maintain cashbook by bank accounts and by fund which shall be used to record deposits and withdrawals in compliance with the provisions of Section 27, Volume II of the Manual on New Government Accounting System.

We have recommended that management release the share of the barangays from the basic real property tax earnings on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with the provision of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 also known and cited as the Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the barangays to use the additional income for development projects which are beneficial to its constituents.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the programs/projects/activities designed under the 20% Development Fund are implemented fully within the timeframe. Optimal utilization of the 20% of the IRA is also recommended in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents which is essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all of the projects funded by fund transfers from the National Government be implemented within the timeframe and require the employees concerned to faithfully supervise and monitor the construction activities, including but not limited to the inspection of works to ensure that the construction is completed as planned in order to deliver timely the essential benefits of the projects to constituents.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit exert more efforts in intensifying collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the collection of unpaid local taxes. This will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of taxes.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of GSIS premiums.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to adhere with the existing rules and regulations concerning the remittance of PhilHealth premium contributions.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to adhere with the existing rules and regulations concerning the remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums.

We have recommended that the local government unit to continue formulate their annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPBs), ensure gender mainstreaming in their policies, programs and projects and comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on Gender and Development to guarantee the promotion of public welfare services addressing gender issues.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairman of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), appropriate one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the LCPC in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and  DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. We further recommend that all of the members of the council be encouraged to participate in the preparation of Local Development Plan and Local Investment Plan for Children and other reports in order to have more accurate basis in determining the needs of children and other children related issues that needs immediate attention and action.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book.
Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 prescribing accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was further recommended.
The following are the suggested entries for the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:
Under the General Fund Book:
Transfer of Unspent Current Year 		x x x
DRRM Funds to Trust Funds			x x x
Cash					x x x
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the       QRF and DRRM-MOOE to special trust fund account.
Under the Trust Fund Book:
Cash					x x x
Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund				x x x
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the       QRF and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund.
We have further recommended that the local government unit require the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit the monthly sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

5.13 We recommended that the (i) Municipal Accountant submit immediately the above-mentioned reports to Auditor’s Office and to strictly observe and comply with the deadline set for the submission of the accounts (Reports of Disbursement with Disbursement Vouchers and Reports of Collection with Official Receipts), financial statements and other financial reports as required by the above-cited regulations, and (ii) Municipal Treasurer submit to the Accounting Office the Report of Collection and Deposits and its corresponding Receipts and Deposit slips; and Report of Check Issued and its Paid Check Vouchers/Payrolls and its supporting documents so that the latter could facilitate the timely preparation of the financial reports.
5.14 We also recommended that the Management cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the employees responsible for the non-submission of the required reports until the same shall have been complied in pursuance to section 7.01 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
5.15 If warranted, institute legal action against those official and employees who continued to disregard on the rendering of their respective accounts without justifiable reasons pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the General Services Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are   covered; (ii) continue efforts in the reconciliation of inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking.
direct the Designated  General Services Officer to prepare Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for all PPE items, starting with current and recent years’ acquisitions until all items are covered and thereafter, renew the AREs at    least every three years;
instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an initial inventory of local road networks in the Municipality in reference to the procedures set by COA Circular 2015-008; and
mobilize additional personnel to facilitate the preparation of the inventory reports and the conduct of physical count and in the event that documents needed to obtain costing of PPE is no longer feasible, consider the fair value thereof as point of reference pursuant to PPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the authorized government depository bank daily or the next banking day at the latest to protect funds from possible loss.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the Municipal Administrator/BAC Chairman and the Municipal     Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness of the documents before awarding the contract;  and (ii) attach to the claims,  all the documents, data and information needed properly signed by authorized officials before any payment is made;
submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit Team and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on    future disbursements and payments;  and
cause the return of the unutilized balance of the fund transfer to the source agency.

We recommended that management, thru the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, exert conscious efforts to improve the timeliness of the submission of accounts, reports and transactions to the COA.

We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board:
a)        continue to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts    of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization in reference to the abovementioned rules and regulations, maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary; and
b)        submit to the Audit Team the required documentations as enumerated above for further audit action;
c)    improve compliance on the documentation of claims to establish their regularity as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a)   improve enforcement of the Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance on the “No Segregation, No Collection” and “No Littering” policies such as imposition of penalties to violators of environmental laws; and
b)    initiate intensive information drive and awareness campaign among the residents in coordination with barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Services Officer:
a)    	exert efforts to conduct a survey on the actual needs of children in a manner which allows the child to participate and to express himself/herself freely;
b)       	encourage the participation of children, thru their responsible parents, in the preparation of the plan/ program, policy formulation and implementation related to juvenile justice and welfare; and
c)      	make a proper representation during the budget preparation on the need to adhere closely to the required allocation of 1% of IRA for the LCPC for the ensuing year or if not feasible, identify or attribute other programs of the Municipality    where the ultimate beneficiaries are the same under the LCPC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive
a)   instruct end-users in the next budgeting period to initiate the preparation of Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the budget for the   ensuing year and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the same as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP);
b)    cause the General Services Officer (GSO) to exert effort to furnish COA copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5)            days  after  the  perfection  of the contract and to send notification of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to  COA   Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006; and
c)   require the Municipal Engineer or the Project Engineer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 specifically in posting the required    information on tarpaulins/billboards to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider hiring additional accounting staff to (a) maintain the Special Accounts in the General Fund; and (b) prepare the corresponding schedules to support the financial statements which will serve as tools in informing Management in evaluating the viability of the economic enterprise operated by the Municipality.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the General  Services Officer instruct all drivers concerned to prepare and submit Driver’s Trip Ticket by affixing all data needed in the document, Monthly Report of Official   Travel sand Report of Fuel Consumption and other required procedures in adherence to the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption.

We recommended that management require the:
Human Resource Officer to issue monthly biometric print outs of DTRs  as basis of employees in preparing their daily record of attendance and require personnel employed on job order basis  to submit duly verified accomplishment reports;  and
Municipal Accountant check payrolls to include documentation of proof of actual services rendered and accomplishment reports for those under JO status.

15.6.	We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to exert extra efforts to trace prior years’ records so that remittances may be made possible; (b) Municipal Accountant isolate prior year’s subsidiary records with unresolved issues and set-up clean SL for the current year’s transactions and thereafter comply with the prompt remittance of the withheld     taxes as prescribed by the BIR.

We recommended that the Municipality sustain its efforts in complying with some of minimum standards of disaster preparedness as required in RA 10121 and NDRRM standards.
However, on some minimum requirements not met by the LGU, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Acting-DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the said standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and
the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the CY 2017 unexpended/unobligated balance of ₱2,741,815.51 to the fund to the Trust Liability DRRM Fund with actual cash transfer pursuant to COA Circular No. No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the LGU officials continue with their efforts to effectively implement programs/ projects sourced from other government agencies.  However, we recommended that the DSWD Officer in charge of the TODA   Livelihood Kabuhayan Project coordinate with officials and beneficiaries of the organization on how to settle unresolved issues.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Municipal Assessor to enhance the present operating system
installed on property assessment to eliminate errors noted and
capture the actual receivables.
b. The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant
certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount due and
collectible for the year; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to comply strictly to Sections 19 and 20 of
the NGAS Manual, Volume I to fairly present the financial statements
and maintain Subsidiary Ledger (SL) which will identify the names of
the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal
Accountant:
1. to exercise diligence in the monitoring and supervision over person/s
involved/concerned in the preparation of the financial reports; and
2. draw the necessary accounting entries to correct/adjust the error
inadvertently committed to fairly present the financial statements, as
follows:
Proposed Adjusting Entry: Debit Credit
Other Deferred Credits P3,854,413.55
Prior Period Adjustments P3,854,413.55
a. To correct the accounting entry made due to errors in recording
Collection of Real Property Taxes for CYs 2002 - 2011
Other Deferred Credits P5,985.889.98
Deferred Real Property Tax Income 5,985.889.98 P5,985.889.98
RPT Receivable 5,985.889.98
b. To correct the entry in recording collection of RPT in advance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. direct the OIC-GSO to consolidate weekly the Requisition Issue Slip
(RIS) for which supplies and materials were issued using the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to
the Municipal Accountant together with the original copy of the RIS
for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the
expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts;
2. require the OIC-GSO and the Municipal Accountant to review the
documents maintained to reclassify musical instruments falling under
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory to Technical and Scientific
Equipment and to reflect the correct Report of Physical Count of
Inventory/ Property, Plant and Equipment and account balance; and
3. instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute cost of supplies issued
and ending inventory using the moving average method based on the RIS
and SSMI submitted by the OIC-GSO for the eventual preparation of
the JEV to record issuances to present correct inventory and
appropriate expense account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain
complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively.
2. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary
adjustment to correct the error inadvertently committed in recording
the development cost of computer program to fairly present the
financial statements;
3. create an Inventory Committee to:
a. conduct complete physical inventory taking;
b. properly classify the equipment found during the inventory taking
in their appropriate account classification based on the new
accounts prescribed by the Commission on Audit (COA);
c. reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting
records; and
d. submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
1. the Municipal Accountant to recognize liabilities only in the books of
the agency, transactions with obligation requests and supported with
complete documents and strictly comply with the provisions of the
law; and
2. the Municipal Treasurer to notify the creditors about their claims and
require them the presentation of documents to support their claim
within a grace period of five (5) calendar days. If no claim has been
made until the grace period, require the Accountant to revert the
payables for a fair presentation of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. compel the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and
the Municipal Social Worker to reconcile the List of Beneficiaries vis-
a vis issued First Batch of Awardees as against the Family Profile
record to facilitate the determination of deserving/qualified
beneficiaries and submit justification/explanation on the discrepancies
noted;
2. direct the Sanggunian Bayan to pass a resolution creating a task force
who will handle the eviction of the informal family settlers/inhabitants
near the railroad to the relocation site considering that the target
60
dates for the relocation was March 2015 and the housing units were
completed almost two years ago;
3. define the duties and functions of the task force to fast tract clearing
of the railroad area including the dissemination on the availability of
affordable housing loan project to facilitate occupancy of the housing
units;
4. require the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages
on the uninstalled water closets and faucets until such time the said
items have been installed by the contractor and deduct the same from
the retention fee, when necessary; and
5. require the concerned Contractor to undertake the necessary repair
of the deficiencies and completion of unfinished works noted during
the ocular inspection to prevent further damage pursuant to Section
62.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point
Person to properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation
of the activities stated in the Gender and Development Plan in order to address
the gender issues. Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD
Plan are gender responsive processes, procedures are properly followed and
other factors that would affect the implementation is considered to avoid
problems when it comes to implementation. Likewise, utilize the fund solely
for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive
and are aligned to the GAD mandate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create Inspectorate Team
composed of representative from the offices of the Accounting, Treasury,
Budget and General Services in order to strengthen internal control to reduce
the possible risk of manipulation in the procurement of goods.

Further instruct the General Service Officer to direct personnel
concerned not to affix their signature on the lower portion of the canvass sheet
which is specifically for the signature of supplier only.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly enforce the submission of required accounting and
financial reports within the prescribed period.

The concerned officers to look into the root cause of the delay in the
preparation and submission of the financial reports and effect appropriate
action/s when necessary, to make possible the early submission of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the assigned
department head/s or personnel to fast tract the submission of the Agency Action
Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) of the CY 2016 Annual Audit
Report, and every year thereafter to the Auditor concerned to facilitate
monitoring and validations of the actions taken by the LGU on the related audit
observations and recommendations, and eventually to submit a validation report
to the Regional Office IV-A, for monitoring purpose. Compliance on the
submission of the same report to the above-mentioned oversight bodies should
also be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Engineer and the Officer-In-Charge (OIC) General Services Office (GSO) to
ensure that a list of all government PPAs for infrastructure and non-
infrastructure projects, respectively, to be implemented during the year is
submitted to the Audit Team Leader at the beginning of the year as required in
COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the certified Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization
of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit all the required
reports/documents to the concerned agencies within the prescribed period in
consonance with the afore-cited guidelines /issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
1. the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and concerned
personnel of the General Service Office (GSO), to submit copies of
perfected contracts/purchase orders together with the required
supporting documents and Notice of Delivery, respectively, to the
Office of the Auditor in accordance with the provisions of COA
Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006 for proper evaluation and
technical review of the contracts as well as timely inspection of
deliveries; and
2. the BAC Chairman or Municipal General Services Officer, as the case
maybe, to monitor submission of the above-mentioned documents to
avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that management intensify efforts to collect the rental receivables from the delinquent lessees.  In case of persistent refusal to settle their bills, enforce civil remedies provided for under Sections 169 and 174 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the stockroom at the Municipal Health Center be renovated in accordance with DOH standards.  Likewise, a qualified employee shall be designated as a property custodian.
Henceforth, all acquisitions of drugs and medicines whether procured by the Municipality or donated by other agencies shall first be accounted for as inventory and subsequent issuance thereof shall be supported with sufficient documents in order to record the issuance and to have fair presentation of the financial statements at year-end.

We recommended to fast track the implementation of the projects/ programs/ activities funded out by Fund Transfers.  Also, the unspent balance of the projects/ programs/ activities shall be returned to the Source Agency as required by laws, rules and regulations.
We recommended that management should exercise prudence and due diligence in accepting donated drugs and medicines to avoid being tagged as a conduit of wasted government properties and funds.

We hereby reiterate our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sanggunian Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Appropriation Budgets.  It is also recommended that a study should be made whether the available manpower can handle engineering jobs in proportionate to the various programs, projects and activities included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as required under the above mentioned regulation in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.  Likewise, we have recommended to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to drop from the books the costs of the demolished old public markets by preparing journal entry voucher.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate are in the process of reconciling their records.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, we required management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Management to comply with the submission of lacking documents.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no. 14 of this audit report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to formulate plans and strategies to enhance collection of its delinquent real property taxes.  We further recommended that the Municipality consider sending demand letters to delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligations and avail of the administrative and legal remedies provided by law to enforce collection of delinquent taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: The management particularly the Office of the Municipal Treasurer has complied with the recommendation, thus resulting to increase in collection.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the above-mentioned provisions so that the Audit team will be able to conduct timely evaluation/review of items purchased and any defects/deficiencies noted thereon will be immediately communicated to the management.
We have recommended and the management agreed to observe the provisions cited above on the prescribed date of submission of documents for audit and evaluation of the transaction of the agency.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the ICO - Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that management exhaust all possible means to collect the receivable from the Sto. Tomas Water District and cause the immediate transfer of the amount collected to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund book to ensure that there is a stand by fund and it will be used solely for the purpose of carrying disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We have recommended that management exert more efforts in obtaining the documents that could provide the details of the advance payment or mobilization fund payment amounting to P391,492.00 and exhaust all possible means to collect the advance payments amounting to P1,092,399.200 from the contractors so that funds could be used to implement other priority development projects/programs which are beneficial to constituents of the local government unit.

We have recommended that management furnish the Office of the Auditor the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity and post signboards and/or public notices in the project site in compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-04 and in order for us to validate the infrastructure projects on time.

We have commended the efforts of the local government unit in conducting a complete physical count of its Property, Plant and Equipment and also reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit comply with the provisions of Section 379 of Republic Act 7160 on disposal of unserviceable properties in order not to mislead the readers on the actual condition of its properties.

We have recommended that the local government unit ensure that all of its insurable assets, properties and interests are insured with the General Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System in compliance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as the “Property Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973 and Administrative Order No. 33 dated August 25, 1987 by identifying all its insurable assets, submitting an inventory report of insurable assets to the System, setting aside in its appropriation the amount needed for premiums and remitting the premiums to the System within the prescribed period to avoid unnecessary losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, assets and properties brought by fortuitous events and guarantee immediate repair or replacement and uninterrupted delivery of public service.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the Accounting Unit record the purchase of all supplies and materials as inventory following the perpetual inventory method. Issuances thereof be recorded as they take place using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) which shall be submitted by the General Service Officer or Local Treasurer as the case maybe.

We have recommended that the concerned personnel exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that management require the concerned officials and employees to submit the required documents. The ICO - Municipal Accountant and ICO - Municipal Treasurer ensure the completeness of supporting documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit. Strict compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds was also recommended.

We have recommended that claims for Representation and Transportation Allowances and Bids and Awards Committee honoraria be supported with the required documents in order for us to have basis in determining whether the provisions of the afore-cited laws, rules and regulations were observed in granting RATA and BAC honoraria. The ICO Municipal Accountant and ICO Municipal Treasurer see to it that the claims for RATA and BAC honoraria are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment.

We have recommended that management release the share of the barangays from the basic real property tax earnings on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with the provision of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 also known and cited as the Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the barangays to use the additional income for development projects which are beneficial to its constituents.

We have recommended that management ensure that the projects/programs/activities designed under the 20% Development Fund and fund transfers from National Government agencies are implemented within the timeframe. Optimal utilization of the foregoing funds was also recommended in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents and address the needs of the poor constituents and marginalized sectors in the municipality towards the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit exert more efforts in intensifying collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the collection of unpaid local taxes. This will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the unexpended balance of PDAF and DAP totaling P1,930,000.00 be returned to the Bureau of Treasury so that the funds could be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and augment future legitimate funding requirement of the government.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book to ensure that there is a stand by fund and it will be used solely for the purpose of carrying disaster risk reduction and management activities. 


Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 prescribing accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was further recommended.

The following are the suggested entries for the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:



Under the General Fund Book:

Transfer of Unspent Current Year 
DRRM Funds to Trust Funds	x x x
Cash x x x

To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE to special trust fund account.

Under the Trust Fund Book:

Cash		x x x
Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
x x x

To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund.

We have further recommended that the local government unit require the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit the monthly sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to apply the civil remedies provided for by law under Sections 174 (a) and (b) and Section 175 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 to enforce the collection of tax. Concerned officials should be reminded to be vigilant in the issuance or execution of levy within one (1) year from the time tax became delinquent.

Also, require the Municipal Assessor to update all information pertaining to taxable real property to have an accurate basis in the set-up of RPT/SET receivables.

Likewise, deputize Barangay Treasurers to collect taxes on real property located within the barangay to intensify tax collections.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare regularly bank reconciliation statements and to submit to the audit team for verification.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor follow the terms and conditions of the restructuring of the loan to avoid payment of accrued interests, penalties and surcharges and that the amount should be included in the succeeding budget until the loan is fully settled.
observations.reason: Not validated due to non-submission of accounts and Reports

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require:  

(a) the Municipal Treasurer to exert best efforts to locate the stale checks and 


(b) the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustment in the books on stale checks and strictly monitor outstanding checks at year end for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The stale checks with a total amount of P32,881.50 were already adjusted in the books last January and October 2017.  The Municipal Accountant made the necessary entries to correct the cash balance in the book

The Philippine National Bank East Gate Branch, San Fernando, Pampanga accounts of the Municipality of Sto. Tomas has been closed and transferred the three accounts to the Land Bank of the Philippines Apalit Branch last March 13-23, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 

a) require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to coordinate their efforts in    the conduct of a regular tax mapping to cleanse and update the records of the Assessor’s and Treasurer’s Offices in order to come up with a realistic and reliable set up of RPT/SET Receivables;  

b) require the Municipal Treasurer to set timetables/target dates on the completion of the updated RPTARs and request for additional personnel needed in the updating of records to regularly monitor the progress in the encoding of the information needed as inputs in the system; and 

c) require  the  Municipal  Assessor  to  facilitate   the  computation  of  adjustments on   restricted   and  tax-exempt   government   properties   for the approval of  the Department of  Finance to pave the way for full enhancement of the system.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor have already reported the property with restrictions and other property with tax exemptions to the Provincial Assessor Office and Finance last September of this year that could decrease RPT receivables and target of the municipality in the succeeding year.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor worked out for the cleansing and updating of records on RPT.

Additional personnel were transferred to the MTO for data encoding and completion of updated RPTARs.

The Municipal Assessor has facilitated the request for the dropping from the Assessment Roll, the  properties with restrictions for a more realistic RPT Receivable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management cause the immediate return to the Special Education of the borrowed funds amounting to P373,645.82 from the General Fund so that funds may be made available for the educational welfare of school children.
observations.managementAction: The management have returned the amount of P150,000.00 last March 22, 2017  to the SEF and the remaining balance will be returned before end of the year.
observations.reason: Partial return by the General Fund of the funds Due to SEF of ₱150,000.00 was made on March 22, 2017 under LBP Check No. 280255, DV No. 430 and JEV No. 3686.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to:                                

(a) see to it that acquisition of property and purchases of semi-expendable items are covered by ARE/ICS; and 

(b) renew the ARE /ICS at least every three years or whenever there is a change in accountability over a property item in order for management to pinpoint responsibility.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed also the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to file the documents and to monitor if there is a need to renew or change the accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

(a) reconstitute an inventory committee to include each Department Head to facilitate inventory taking by Office of all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality; and
