(b) require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to (I) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim to increase the coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: The municipality followed the recommendation of the Audit Team; the Local Chief Executive already created the Inventory and Appraisal Committee and includes all department heads to help in facilitating the inventory taking by the office. The Acknowledgement Receipt (ARE) and Memorandum Receipt are already in use and the GSO/Municipal Accountant will prepare and maintain the ledger cards for property and equipment of the municipality and submit the report on Physical Inventory annually to the Auditing Team.
observations.reason: The Acting GSO,  disclosed in an interview that she is still familiarizing herself on the required PPE and inventory reports on supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require: 

(a) the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of documents before processing of claims;


(b) limit representation disbursements to  simple meals only and use funds for projects and activities that will be more beneficial to the Municipality and its constituents;                          

(c) specify in the Purchase Request and canvass sheets, the particular food items to be procured with unit cost;           

(d) submit the minutes and approved program design or project proposal for meals claimed during meetings;          

(e) submit the duly signed list of attendees of the various programs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality have already implemented or complied the recommendation of the Auditing Team as to avoid excessive/expensive meals during meetings and attendance of the meeting is also attached to disbursement voucher.  The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned department to serve simple meals/snacks especially for short meeting/gatherings.
observations.reason:
There are still claims for meals not supported with the required documents, only ORs/Sales Invoices were attached to the DVs.


The menu or food served was not specified on PR on some claims for meals.

Minutes of meetings was indicated on some disbursements only.

observations.managementAction: The management have already adopted/practiced  the recommendation of the Audit Team as to properly accomplished the trip tickets and prepare  the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption  and Official Travels. The management limit and monitor the issuance of PO of fuel to the Municipal Heads resulting to decrease of fuel consumption.
observations.reason:
Issuance of fuel and oil for use of private vehicles was monitored by management.

Direct payments to suppliers of gasoline was made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

(a)  require the Municipal  Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documents and that all  data and information needed are supplied including proper approvals before any payment is made;

(b)  submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit Team and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on future disbursements and payments; and 

(c)  submit the legal basis for the payment of ₱3,000.00 Bayanihan Fund and henceforth, identify the specific provision of law, rule or regulation covering disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the documents attached in the disbursement vouchers and to strictly monitor and verify all needed documents before payments.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant exerted full efforts to assure that documents required supported the DVs before payment of claims. 

Some of the required documents on the claims were attached to the DVs.

The management required the Liga to submit legal basis.

observations.managementAction: The management followed and practiced the recommendation of the Audit Team as to the completeness of the documents before awarding and the returned immediately the unexpended balance to the source agency.
observations.reason:
Some of the required documents on BUB projects were submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

In August 2017, DOLE Funds for livelihood projects not implemented as of December CY 2016 were implemented for TODA procurement of spare parts and for Buko Vendors carts and supplies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

(a) properly plan and allocate the 20% DF budget according to purpose and prioritization strictly for developmental projects and prepare the development plan in close consultation with the local development council. Priority consideration shall be given to programs and projects which provide long term benefits that partake the nature of capital expenditures or investment; 

(b)  consider the inclusion of applicable livelihood opportunities in the development projects intended for a long-term benefit of the majority of the poor marginalized sector of the Municipality; and

(c)  exclude from the 20% Development Fund programs and activities that are mostly in the nature of maintenance and other operating expenses that do not bring about long-term benefits to the community consistent with the guidelines under Joint Memorandum Circular No, 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The Management have already created a well-trained Emergency Response Team and prepared the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Plan and submit other related reports regularly.  The unexpended balance of LDRMF was transferred to Trust Fund at the end of the year.  The Municipal Social Welfare Office maintains complete records on the receipts and distribution of reliefs and the status of the residents in evacuation center.
observations.reason:
The CY 2017 Annual Investment Plan included capital investment projects such as construction of local roads, bridges and line canals, Flood Control programs and purchase of lot for the covered court. However, the construction/repair of office building is  an administrative cost which should not be charged to the 20% DF. 

There are other urgent priorities of the LGU.

Management lacked full awareness on the requirement that all maintenance projects should be excluded from the AIP.

observations.managementAction: The Management strictly followed the recommendation of the Audit Team. The LDC formulated plans and budget according to purpose and priorities as instructed or recommended by the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
The MDRRMO is presently preparing the long-term MDRRMP.




The MSWO is presently completing her records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 

(a) require the GAD Focal Person to carefully plan and evaluate the GAD plan, budget, activities/programs/ projects and accomplishments in order to determine the effectiveness of the GAD programs, projects and activities implemented and develop GAD codes based on gender issues to serve as basis for easy monitoring of GAD identified programs and     

(b) conduct a comprehensive information, education and communication campaign on gender issues, through direct consultation and dialogues with targeted beneficiaries and to ensure that activities to be undertaken by the LGU are truly gender responsive and effective.
observations.managementAction: The  Municipal Social Welfare  Development/Gender and Development made budget, programs of activities and projects.  They strictly complied with the recommendation of Audit Team as to gather and comprehensive evaluation and studies on the Gender and Development issues.
observations.reason: Information campaign was already conducted. However, expenses charged on GAD activities  was for seminars, supplies, miscellaneous expenses and salaries of students hired on the Special Program for employment of Students (SPES) which are not in accordance with the objectives of GAD which is the empowerment of women in nation building.

observations.managementAction: The Management with proper coordination with the Barangay Officials campaigned for the strictly implementation of "No Segregation, No Collection “policy but still the people did not cooperate in waste segregation. The management is planning to have a joint force with the Barangay for the strict implementation of the policy and to fully educate them   on the basic laws in preserving and protecting the environment.
observations.reason: Limited sources of funds did not enable most of the barangays to construct their own Material Recovery Facility (MRF).

observations.managementAction: The Senior Citizen and Persons with Disability plan was strictlyevaluated  and the  prioritization of  the needs of the concerned Senior Citizen and Person with Disability.  The Local Chief Executive instructed the focal person for Senior Citizen, Gender and Development and Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer to monitor from time to time the needs of the members.
observations.reason: A livelihood project on Candle Making was undertaken by the Persons with Disabilities (PWDs).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management 

(a) conduct an actual survey on the actual needs of children in a manner which allows the child to participate and to express himself/herself freely; 

(b) encourage the participation of children, thru their responsible parents, in the program and policy formulation and implementation related to juvenile justice and welfare; and 

(c) adhere strictly to the required allocation of 1% of IRA for the LCPC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare Development Office already made plans and programs/policy for the protection and welfare of children which was implemented this year and was already included and approved in the Annual Budget.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | In CY 2017, the LGU budgeted a total amount of ₱250,000.00 which is lesser than the required amount of ₱877,838.46 , 1%  of the total IRA  of ₱87,783,846.00. Charges to the fund are seminar expenses for the youth and other supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a General Service Office, or designate a GS Officer who would take charge of supply and property management control and management of properties of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Executive Order No. 22-2017 was issued on September 29, 2017 designating Ms. Maria Lourdes M. Sulit as General Services Head.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a)  require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere with the requirements on the timely submission of financial accounts and consider the deployment of additional personnel in the respective offices to resolve the manpower constraint’s

b)   require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that DVs are duly supported with complete documents before the preparation of check; 

c)  require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from preparing DVs without approved SDs; return to respective claimants those DVs without approved supporting documents for completion and process claims with complete SDs only; and strictly observe existing government rules and regulations on disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned departments to submit Financial Reports on time and ensure the completeness of the documents attached to the disbursements vouchers. The management hired additional employees to concerned offices/departments.
observations.reason:
Limited manpower complement in the Accounting Office 


DVs were submitted to the Accounting Office with incomplete signatures thus, were returned for compliance and incurred delay for submission to COA.
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-001 was issued on July 7, 2017 inviting the attention of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant on the delayed submission of accounts. To date, the latest submitted accounts were for September 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant and the Treasurer continue the practice of remitting amounts due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant have already reconciled with the concerned agency, the balance of GSIS premiums/ payables. It was already adjusted/corrected in the book.  The management ensures that every monthly remittance of the premiums are complete, accurate and remitted intact. The GSIS furnished the municipality certificate of 100% remittances on premiums and loans. ERF handler always monitors the accuracy of the remittances to concerned agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to withhold taxes and ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and immediately to the BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records as to withholding taxes and continue the practice of withholding taxes and remitting intact.  They are also further advised/instructed to remit the balance of  withholding taxes before the end of the 4th quarter.
observations.reason: The Management is presently conducting reconciliation of accounts with the BIR. Verification of accounts and interview conducted with the Municipal Accountant disclosed that out of the total balance of at year end CY 2016 of ₱1,604,981.82, ₱1,400,000.00 were for previous years with no subsidiary records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to transfer all road networks capitalized in the registry to the books of accounts, keep and maintain subsidiary records for every road component and prepare schedule for the computation of depreciation for the recorded roads and road components; b. GSO to render Report on Local Road Network/Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the municipality in accordance with the above schedule; and c. Municipal Engineer to provide the Local Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for each of the recorded road components for the computation of depreciation for the component in accordance with the above schedule.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the computation of depreciation and Journal entries were prepared and recorded in the books of accounts. The report on Local Road Network/Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System is currently on going. The GSO is currently on-going on the process by GSO.
observations.reason: The report on Local Road Network/Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System is currently on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) clarify the condition for the entitlement of free water concessionaire, and up to what level of affinity from the donor, otherwise, if it is not possible due to the condition cited in the MOA or Deed of Donation, them receivables should not be included in the system and in the recorded receivables; b) the Municipal Mayor direct the employees from the Waterworks Operation and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to strictly implement disconnection based on their guidelines. Follow-up employees from the Waterworks Operation regularly the collections from their delinquent water consumers; require the Municipal Treasurer to include in their Annual Budget the corresponding appropriation for water fees incurred in the previous years; c) follow-up from the homeowner’s association and private companies the payment for production and assessment; and d) initiate the necessary legal actions thru the Municipality’s Legal Officer, if any, against defaulting consumers in order to enforce collection of long overdue receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Operations of Waterworks Department forwarded a request letter to the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to strictly implement disconnection based on their guidelines. Initial coordination meeting have been initiated with four (4) private companies and one (1) of them had paid their obligations. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are coordinating regularly the collections from the Waterworks Department based on their guidelines. For the P22,500 Trust Fund receivable, it is still in the process of securing the documentary requirements to file new request to write-off such receivable. The Municipal Employees from the Waterworks Operation are regularly coordinating with the various barangay and water consumers to include in their Annual Budget the corresponding appropriation for water fees incurred in the previous years.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had not yet effected the necessary legal remedies to rid the books of the stated dormant receivables; however, initial coordination and payments from some private companies have been initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prepare all the documentary requirements in the proper remedy for the writing-off of the dormant receivables in the GF and TF and submit the same to the Audit Team to rid the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Livelihood Department is in the process of establishing the documentary requirements in the proper remedy to write-off such receivable. However, for the P22,500 Trust Fund receivable, it is still in the process of securing documentary requirements to file new request to write-off such receivable.
observations.reason: It is still in the process of securing the documentary requirements to file new request to write-off such receivable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to credit the account Textbook and Instructional Materials Inventory for the cost of books which were already issued by the GSO; the GSO to locate the NAT and NCAE books, NAT and books were acquired as early as 2005 wherein the condition or status is not known; and the GSO to determine the status or condition and whereabouts of the other Supplies and Materials Inventory account and to prepare the necessary reports such as Acceptance Report, Accounting Acknowledgement Receipt, Waste Materials Report, Requisition and Issue Slips or other records to prove issuance and submit the same to the Accounting Office for proper adjustment of the inventory account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that it was already reduced to P1,372,450.50 as of May 31, 2018. The remaining balance was not yet adjusted due to the absence of RIS for NAT and NCAE acquired in CY 2013, Disaster Management Book and other instructional materials acquired in 2014 and 2017, respectively. The GSO is in the process of preparing the reports to be submitted to the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: On the Textbook and Instructional Materials Inventory as of December 31, 2017 amounting to P5,712,258.00, it was already reduced to P1,372,450.50 as of May 31, 2018. Remaining balance not yet adjusted due to absence of RIS for some items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO personnel to make full inventory of all the vehicles which are not yet insured and cause Insurance coverage for all government buildings in the GF and SEF to guarantee indemnification in case of occurrence of catastrophic perils.
observations.managementAction: Insurance coverage for all buildings is currently in process. The Municipal Accountant and GSO provided adequate insurance coverage for all government buildings in the GF and SEF to guarantee indemnification in case of occurrence of catastrophic perils. Motor vehicle insurance is currently in process.
observations.reason: The preparation of the Inventory Report of all vehicles is still on-going; Insurance coverage for all buildings is currently in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council to conduct feasibility of all projects of the 20 percent Development Fund to avoid delay in the implementation of the projects funded out of the 20 percent Development Fund to maximize the implementation of projects and to ensure that the development projects would benefit all constituents and carefully choose each project to be included in the planned program.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council conducted feasibility studies for the projects to be included in the 20 percent Development Fund. The Council implemented measures to ensure project feasibility and proper selection.
observations.reason: Projects were carefully chosen and feasibility studies conducted by the Local Development Council.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point Person to establish a GAD Database and Sex-disaggregated data as required under Section 4(b) of JMC No. 2013-01 of the DILG, DBM, NEDA and the PCW in order to create a well defined programs and projects that are best suited to the need of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already directed the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) together with the GFPS TWG to establish a GAD Database and sex disaggregated data as required in JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: GAD Database and sex-disaggregated data establishment is ongoing; full institutionalization pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the activities stated in the Gender and Development Plan in order to address the gender issues. Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive processes, procedures are properly followed and other factors that would affect the implementation is considered to avoid problems when it comes to implementation. Likewise, utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate. Submit justification on why the above programs are considered GAD related activities by the GAD Focal Point Persons.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor clarified that the GAD Plan of the Municipality was officially reviewed and endorsed by the DILG Provincial Office. Management will ensure that GAD fund will be used for gender-responsive activities and will monitor and report implementation status.
observations.reason: GAD Plan was reviewed and endorsed by DILG Provincial Office but elements of GAD mandate not yet fully reflected in implemented PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require that the MDRRMO along with the other implementing agencies such as GSO, Engineering and BAC to closely monitor the undertaking of the proposed projects to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity for risk reduction and management be fully implemented. Also that the Municipal Mayor prioritize LDRRMF projects and ensure implementation in succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already directed the MDRRMO to coordinate with the GSO, Engineering and BAC to closely monitor the PPAs. As of March 12, 2018 the MDRRMO submitted a response letter and implemented some of the PPAs utilizing the MDRRM Fund for CY 2016: Training, Disaster Preparedness Trainings, Purchase of Disaster Emergency equipment, supplies and logistics - All implemented. Management expressed intention to fully comply with implementation of remaining PPAs in succeeding years.
observations.reason: Some PPAs were implemented; others remain to be implemented in succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the required information under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 are posted in tarpaulin or similar materials to inform the public of the projects of the Municipality; remind other Department Heads on the posting to the programs/activities for implementation by the concerned department as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already directed the Municipal Engineer to inform the concerned Department Heads to post the required information under COA Circular No. 2013-004. The Management justified that other Department Heads were reminded to comply on the posting of signboards; some have complied while others are in the process of posting.
observations.reason: Some departments have posted required signboards; others still to comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 and NBC No. 563 in the appropriation and payment of membership fees and annual dues to various leagues and ensure that payments of such kind to private organization shall not be charged against the government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 and NBC No. 563. The Municipality already implemented measures to ensure membership fees are appropriated and paid only if allowable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop approving the payment of honoraria to Presidents of Senior Citizens and ERPAT associations unless sufficient legal basis for the grant of honorarium has been submitted to the Audit Team for further evaluation; otherwise the payment of said honoraria may be disallowed in audit. As an alternative if sufficient legal basis is presented and expenses incurred, transportation assistance of P1,500.00 monthly may be given to each Senior Citizen President.
observations.managementAction: Payment of honoraria to Presidents of Barangay’s Senior Citizen and ERPAT associations was already stopped. As an alternative, Transportation Assistance of P1,500.00 monthly was given to each Senior Citizen Barangay President where sufficient legal basis was submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: Management should submit comments/ justifications relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies.  Likewise, the members of the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA No. 9184.
The Local Chief Executive address the pressing problem on personnel shortage by hiring qualified and working personnel who have the capability to implement the BUB programs on time.

Management should submit comments/ justifications relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies in order to complete the conduct of technical review thereof.  Likewise, the members of the Bids and Awards Committee should adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA No. 9184.

The Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sanggunian Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects covered by 20% Development Fund.  Further, consider the hiring of working personnel who have the capability to implement the programs, projects and activities incorporated in the yearly 20% Development Fund.
The Local Government create plantilla positions and hire full time personnel to be assigned at the Local DRRM Office pursuant to NDRRMC/ DILG/ DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance had already provided funds for the plantilla position of Local DRRM Officer (SG 24) under the Supplemental Budget No. 3.
observations.reason: Supplemental Budget No. 3 was approved on July 10, 2017 and the hiring process will follow soon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that LSB submit legal basis justifying the utilization of MOOE in the amount of P371,818.20 for payment of honoraria of LSB teachers and non-formal educators. 

Henceforth, evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.
observations.managementAction: The LSB will ensure to correct this audit observation on the next budget year. As of this report, clarifications and inquiries were still conducted to proper agencies to resolve this issue.
observations.reason: Plantilla position will be created first.  For the meantime, they were hired under the job order basis and charged under the account of other professional services of MOOE.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive should consider the specific standards and general objectives of internal controls in appointing a Municipal Administrator without sacrificing the available manpower at the Municipal Engineering Office.

Moreover, the Sangguniang Bayan may conduct inquiry on the status of implementation of the programs, projects and activities prioritized under the 20% Municipal Development Funds for calendar years 2012 to 2016.

Management should send letter of demand to delinquent taxpayers.  In case of persistent refusal to settle their bills, enforce remedies provided for under Sections 169 and 170 of RA No. 7160.

Moreover, the present administration should set the tone in addressing the increasing amount of uncollected revenues by appropriating a fraction of its revenues to payment of water bills totaling P5,070,392.75.
observations.managementAction:
Series of Demand Letters and Notice of Disconnection were sent to the subscribers. Monitoring Reports will be updated by the meter readers for consolidation with supporting documents for purposes of requesting write off to the Local Auditor.

To be included in the next budget year.
observations.reason: Available source of fund is not enough to cover the priority PPAs of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
The LCE in coordination with the Sanggunian Bayan should closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the AIP.  Henceforth, take into consideration whether the available manpower has the capability to implement on time the programs, projects and activities to be included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan.

Employ the services of additional personnel who will focus on the preparation of needed documents and program of works.

Management should submit comments relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies.  Likewise, the members of the BAC adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA 9184.  Further, the BAC Secretariat should exert extra efforts in checking the technical and financial documents submitted by the bidders.

Submit justifications relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies.  Likewise, the members of the BAC adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA 9184.  Further, the BAC Secretariat should exert extra efforts in checking the technical and financial documents submitted by the bidders.

Establish a Local DRRM Office headed by an LDRRM Officer and assisted by three staff as envisioned under NDRRMC/ DILG/ DBM/ CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated Apri1 4, 2014.

The LDRRMC should consider the implementation of programs and projects embodied in the Annual Investment Plan in order to protect and keep the constituents and property safe from recurring calamities.

Monitor the accomplishment of the Committee charged with the duty of ensuring the titling of the acquired lots in order to avoid legal repercussions that may arise in case of failure to transfer ownership to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Pertinent documents were already secured and endorsed to the Committee who will facilitate the transfer of ownership.
observations.reason: It would take a longer period to process the titles of the said lots.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV reclassifying the unserviceable assets to the “Other Assets” account.

  Create a Committee primarily to dispose of the unserviceable properties totaling P9,515,470.85 through public auction, pursuant to the provisions of law.
observations.managementAction: To finalize the physical inventory of properties for the first semester of 2017 and dispose the unserviceable properties through public auction before the year ends.
observations.reason: On-going inventory to include the additional unserviceable properties identified in 2016 and 2017.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Treasurer should ascertain the possibility on the collection of all the outstanding loans receivable by sending again demand letters to all beneficiaries with outstanding balance.  If after doing so, the results prove futile, request from the Commission on Audit an authority to write-off the accounts, submitting the result of the demand letters, as proof of collection efforts done.  It is only after the COA has approved the request that the account may be dropped from the books of the LGU.

However, for those who are still in the government service, especially those in the local government of Sto. Tomas, make arrangement with the employee concerned for the settlement of their accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management commits to exert extra efforts to be able to collect the said receivable.
observations.reason: Demand letters were already sent to some debtors

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:  (a) Municipal Accountant maintain the supplies ledger cards   for each commodity/stock which should be reconciled with the stock cards maintained by the designated Supply Officer, and 

(b) Supply/Property Officer;  (i) maintain stock cards for the supplies on hand; (ii) make use of the RIS to document supplies’ issuances and prepare the monthly summary report thereon; and (iii) conduct of physical count of inventory of supplies in conformity with Section 124 of NGAS.
observations.reason: No supplies ledger cards prepared by the former and new Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local chief Executive direct the Acting General Services Officer to:

 (a) brief the concerned drivers and agency personnel on the proper accomplishment of the Driver’s Trip Ticket and the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of 
Fuel consumption; and 

(b) comply with                     the preparation and submission of the said reports to the audit Team for verification to ensure that the expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable, effectively controlled and properly accounted for.
observations.reason:
Lack of awareness of agency officials on the preparation of the driver’s trip tickets

No LGU monitoring and control on fuel and oil expenses and the failure to adopt agency policies that will set limitations on gasoline consumption

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Executive:

(a) reconstitute an inventory committee to include each Department Head to facilitate inventory taking by Office of all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality; 

(b) require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim to increase the coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances; and  

(c) direct the General Services Officer to prepare Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for all PPE items, starting with the current and recent years’ acquisition until all items are covered and thereafter, renew the AREs at least every three years or whenever there is transfer of accountability.
observations.reason:
Management committed to create an inventory committee

Lack of diligent efforts by the GSO and the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare PPE ledger cards and property records which are needed for reconciliation of inventory records.

Management lacked awareness on the strict regulations especially the preparation of the ARE for PPE acquisitions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to renew the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) and maintain Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS) on the issuance of property and semi-expendable items at least every three years or whenever there is a change in accountability over a property item.
observations.reason: Management lacked diligent efforts to prepare AREs  in order to pinpoint responsibility of officials and employees on property issued to them and to properly  account all assets owned by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents forming part thereof pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.  Considering that the subject deficiency had been repeatedly discussed in prior years’ report, there is a compelling reason that the recommended course of action and corrective measure offered be given serious consideration by Management.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat is not fully aware that contracts and POs should be submitted in advance to COA for review for possible communication  of errors (if any) hence, there was delay in the evaluation of said documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management cause the immediate return to the Special Education of the borrowed funds amounting to P373,645.82 from the General Fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was instructed by management to return to the SEF the balance of borrowed funds of ₱373,645.82 through partial transfer. She was not well informed that this borrowing if not returned may lead to illegal use of public funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) set aside at least 5% of the appropriated budget as required in Section 36 of RA 9710 for  integration on  GAD-related projects, programs and activities; and (b)  require the focal person on GAD to submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2015 and every year thereafter.
observations.reason: Management has allocated at least 5% for the GAD Program and assigned a GAD Focal Person to see to it that the objectives of the fund are strictly observed. However, some programs were not GAD-related issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to withhold taxes and ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and immediately to the BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007.
observations.reason: The LGU continued not to remit intact all taxes withheld from employees and suppliers due to lack of awareness of management on strict regulations on remittance of taxes.

the Acting GSO may dispose the same in   accordance with existing guidelines and procedures.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper documentation and draw the adjusting entry to drop from the books the cost of the assets disposed of to reflect the correct assets of the Municipality as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assured that the corresponding adjusting entry will be prepared to record  disposal of assets. However during the year, no disposal of assets were processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Head of Livelihood and Cooperative Office and project implementer to exert more efforts in collecting the amounts due from the borrowers particularly the beginning balance and non-moving accounts totaling P302,245.83; establish alternative payment schemes and plans for defaulting borrowers to enforce efficient collection; and include in the Memorandum of Agreement the provisions in case of non-payment due to death of the borrower. Also, identify the collectability of the doubtful loans and if found that collection is nil, coordinate with the Accounting Department to request the write-off of those accounts from the Commission on Audit by accomplishing the required documentations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor is consistent with her mandate to exert more efforts in collecting the amount particularly the non-moving accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II Observation No. 4

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor  already gave instruction  to the Municipal Administrator to research   the guidelines on how to establish IAU in the municipality but due to lack of funds, such was not undertaken during the year.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commented that they have already filed the request for write-off in previous years’ but the status of the request was not known.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as required under the above mentioned
regulation in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.
Likewise, we have recommended to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to drop from
the books the costs of the demolished old public markets by preparing journal
entry voucher.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Since there are no records available to establish the cost of the demolished old
market, we therefore, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconstruct the cost
of the existing market and any difference will be recorded as other asset.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions mentioned above so as to
present fairly the presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended and the management agreed to comply on the
utilization of additional one percent Special Education Tax so that the fund will
be optimally used for the purposes cited above. Further, we recommended that
the District Supervisor submit the Liquidation Report on the utilization of the
financial assistance.

We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the
documentary requirements on the release of cash assistance under the Aid in
Crisis Situation Program of the DSWD as required under the above-mentioned
regulation to prove the propriety and validity of the transactions. We further
recommended that the amount of cash assistance to be given to indigents be
based on the Social Worker’s assessment of the case of the client.

We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the
above regulation to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions, to
wit:
1. Municipal Accountant to certify the obligation of the allotment
and completeness of the supporting documents in the
disbursement voucher;
2. Municipal Treasurer to certify the availability of fund in the
disbursement voucher;
3. Municipal Budget Officer to certify the existence of available
appropriations in the Obligation Request;
4. Head of the Requisitioning Office to certify on the necessity
and legality of charges to appropriation and allotment under
his direct supervision and the validity, propriety and legality of
supporting documents.

Further, we have required the OIC-Municipal Accountant, the Municipal
Treasurer, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Head of the Requesting Office
to affix their signatures on the unsigned ObRs and DVs.

We have recommended and management agreed to discontinue from
granting financial assistance for the procurement of school needs and comply
with the above-mentioned regulation inorder to achieve transparency,
accountability and economy.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC
Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to
comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001. In addition, we required
management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity
of the procurement of infrastructure projects.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC
Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to
comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001. In addition, we required
management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity
of the procurement of infrastructure projects.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to formulate plans
and strategies to enhance collection of its delinquent real property taxes. We
further recommended that the Municipality consider sending demand letters to
delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligations and avail of the
administrative and legal remedies provided by law to enforce collection of
delinquent taxpayers.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to prioritize the
implementation of an effective and efficient waste disposal facility as required
under the above-mentioned regulation to protect the environment and the
constituents.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to assess the causes
of delays in the project implementation and address all issues accordingly in
order to fast track the implementation of projects funded under the 20%
Development Fund, R.A. Nos. 7171 and 8240 so as not to deprive the constituents
from the use of the projects’ expected outputs.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
MDRRMO to ensure that programs and activities included in the LDRRMFIP
pertaining to disaster preparedness are implemented as planned to strengthen the
LGU’s institutional capacity to disaster risk reduction and management and
building their resilience to disaster.

We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the
above-mentioned provisions so that the Audit team will be able to conduct timely
evaluation/review of items purchased and any defects/deficiencies noted thereon
will be immediately communicated to the management.

We have recommended and the management agreed to observe the
provisions cited above on the prescribed date of submission of documents for
audit and evaluation of the transaction of the agency.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to see to it that all
the required supporting documents be attached to the disbursement vouchers
before payment is made to avoid disallowance or suspension in the future.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the ICO - Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that management exhaust all possible means to collect the receivable from the Sto. Tomas Water District and cause the immediate transfer of the amount collected to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund book to ensure that there is a stand by fund and it will be used solely for the purpose of carrying disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We have recommended that management exert more efforts in obtaining the documents that could provide the details of the advance payment or mobilization fund payment amounting to P391,492.00 and exhaust all possible means to collect the advance payments amounting to P1,092,399.200 from the contractors so that funds could be used to implement other priority development projects/programs which are beneficial to constituents of the local government unit.

We have recommended that management furnish the Office of the Auditor the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity and post signboards and/or public notices in the project site in compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-04 and in order for us to validate the infrastructure projects on time.

We have commended the efforts of the local government unit in conducting a complete physical count of its Property, Plant and Equipment and also reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit comply with the provisions of Section 379 of Republic Act 7160 on disposal of unserviceable properties in order not to mislead the readers on the actual condition of its properties.

We have recommended that the local government unit ensure that all of its insurable assets, properties and interests are insured with the General Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System in compliance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as the “Property Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973 and Administrative Order No. 33 dated August 25, 1987 by identifying all its insurable assets, submitting an inventory report of insurable assets to the System, setting aside in its appropriation the amount needed for premiums and remitting the premiums to the System within the prescribed period to avoid unnecessary losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, assets and properties brought by fortuitous events and guarantee immediate repair or replacement and uninterrupted delivery of public service.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the Accounting Unit record the purchase of all supplies and materials as inventory following the perpetual inventory method. Issuances thereof be recorded as they take place using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) which shall be submitted by the General Service Officer or Local Treasurer as the case maybe.

We have recommended that the concerned personnel exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that management require the concerned officials and employees to submit the required documents. The ICO - Municipal Accountant and ICO - Municipal Treasurer ensure the completeness of supporting documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before affixing their signatures in the disbursement vouchers and before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit. Strict compliance with the provisions of the law, rules and regulations pertaining to documentation of claims against government funds was also recommended.

We have recommended that claims for Representation and Transportation Allowances and Bids and Awards Committee honoraria be supported with the required documents in order for us to have basis in determining whether the provisions of the afore-cited laws, rules and regulations were observed in granting RATA and BAC honoraria. The ICO Municipal Accountant and ICO Municipal Treasurer see to it that the claims for RATA and BAC honoraria are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment.

We have recommended that management release the share of the barangays from the basic real property tax earnings on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with the provision of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 also known and cited as the Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the barangays to use the additional income for development projects which are beneficial to its constituents.

We have recommended that management ensure that the projects/programs/activities designed under the 20% Development Fund and fund transfers from National Government agencies are implemented within the timeframe. Optimal utilization of the foregoing funds was also recommended in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents and address the needs of the poor constituents and marginalized sectors in the municipality towards the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit exert more efforts in intensifying collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the collection of unpaid local taxes. This will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing rules and regulations concerning the remittance of taxes withheld.
We have recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the GSIS regulations remittance of monthly personal share of employees and the contribution for employer’s share.

We have recommended that the local government unit to continue formulate their annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPBs), ensure gender mainstreaming in their policies, programs and projects and comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations on Gender and Development to guarantee the promotion of public welfare services addressing gender issues.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the unexpended balance of PDAF and DAP totaling P1,930,000.00 be returned to the Bureau of Treasury so that the funds could be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and augment future legitimate funding requirement of the government.

We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book to ensure that there is a stand by fund and it will be used solely for the purpose of carrying disaster risk reduction and management activities.
Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 prescribing accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was further recommended.
The following are the suggested entries for the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:
Under the General Fund Book:
Transfer of Unspent Current Year 		x x x
DRRM Funds to Trust Funds				x x x
Cash
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the       QRF and DRRM-MOOE to special trust fund account.
Under the Trust Fund Book:
Cash					x x x
Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund				x x x
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the       QRF and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund.
We have further recommended that the local government unit require the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit the monthly sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that management require: (a) the Municipal Treasurer to exert best efforts to locate the stale checks and (b) the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustment in the books on stale checks and strictly monitor outstanding checks at year end for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management:
a)  require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to coordinate their efforts in    the conduct of a regular tax mapping to cleanse and update the records of the Assessor’s and Treasurer’s Offices in order to come up with a realistic and reliable set up of RPT/SET Receivables;
b) require the Municipal Treasurer to set timetables/target dates on the completion of the updated RPTARs and request for additional personnel needed in the updating of records to regularly monitor the progress in the encoding of the information needed as inputs in the system; and
c) require  the  Municipal  Assessor  to  facilitate   the  computation  of  adjustments on   restricted   and  tax-exempt   government   properties   for the approval of  the Department of  Finance to pave the way for full enhancement of the system.

We recommended that management cause the immediate return to the Special Education of the borrowed funds amounting to P373,645.82 from the General Fund so that funds may be made available for the educational welfare of school children.

We recommended that management require the:  (a) Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for each commodity/stock which should be reconciled with the stock cards maintained by the designated Supply/Property Officer; and (b) Supply/Property Supply Officer to (i) maintain stock cards for the supplies on hand; (ii) make use of the RIS to document supplies issuances and prepare the monthly summary report thereon; and (iii) conduct physical count of inventory of supplies/materials.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to (a) see to it that acquisition of property and purchases of semi-expendable items are covered by ARE/ICS; and (b) renew the ARE /ICS at least every three years or whenever there is a change in accountability over a property item in order for management to pinpoint responsibility.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) reconstitute an inventory committee to include each Department Head to facilitate inventory taking by Office of all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality; and (b) require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim to increase the coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances.

We recommended that management require: (a) the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of documents before processing of claims; (b) limit representation disbursements to  simple meals only and use funds for projects and activities that will be more beneficial to the Municipality and its constituents; (c) specify in the Purchase Request and canvass sheets, the particular food items to be procured with unit cost;  (d) submit the minutes and approved program design or project proposal for meals claimed during meetings; (e) submit the duly signed list of attendees of the various programs.

We recommended that management:
(a)  require the Municipal  Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documents and that all  data and information needed are supplied including proper approvals before any payment is made;
(b)  submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit Team and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on future disbursements and payments; and
(c)  submit the legal basis for the payment of ₱3,000.00 Bayanihan Fund and henceforth, identify the specific provision of law, rule or regulation covering disbursement of funds.

We recommended that management:
(a)  properly plan and allocate the 20% DF budget according to purpose and prioritization strictly for developmental projects and prepare the development plan in close consultation with the local development council. Priority consideration shall be given to programs and projects which provide long term benefits that partake the nature of capital expenditures or investment;
(b)  consider the inclusion of applicable livelihood opportunities in the development projects intended for a long-term benefit of the majority of the poor marginalized sector of the Municipality; and
(c)     exclude from the 20% Development Fund programs and activities that are mostly in the nature of maintenance and other operating expenses that do not bring about long-term benefits to the community consistent with the guidelines under Joint Memorandum Circular No, 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that management:
(a) require the GAD Focal Person to carefully plan and evaluate the GAD plan, budget, activities/programs/ projects and accomplishments in order to determine the effectiveness of the GAD programs, projects and activities implemented and develop GAD codes based on gender issues to serve as basis for easy monitoring of GAD identified programs and
(b) conduct a comprehensive information, education and communication campaign on gender issues, through direct consultation and dialogues with targeted beneficiaries and to ensure that activities to be undertaken by the LGU are truly gender responsive and effective.

We recommended that management (a) conduct an actual survey on the actual needs of children in a manner which allows the child to participate and to express himself/herself freely; (b) encourage the participation of children, thru their responsible parents, in the program and policy formulation and implementation related to juvenile justice and welfare; and (c) adhere strictly to the required allocation of 1% of IRA for the LCPC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a General Service Office, or designate a GS Officer who would take charge of supply and property management control and management of properties of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents forming part thereof pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.  Considering that the subject deficiency had been repeatedly discussed in prior years’ report, there is a compelling reason that the recommended course of action and corrective measure offered be given serious consideration by Management.

We recommended that the Accountant and the Treasurer continue the practice of remitting amounts due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended that Management continue to withhold taxes and ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and immediately to the BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007.

We  recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the GSO to prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to record all unserviceable properties for  their eventual disposal;
direct the Inventory Committee to properly classify all the equipment in their respective account classification  based on the new accounts prescribed by the Commission on Audit (COA), determine the cause of the variance of P131,585,967.58     and strive for the reconciliation of the PPE between the inventory and accounting records; and
instruct the  Municipal  Accountant to record the donated vehicles based on the fair value of the  vehicles at the time of donation and reclassify the program design for the Municipal Data Network and Disaster Management System to the proper account  Computer Software.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to transfer all road networks carried in the registry to the books of accounts,  keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every  road network and prepare lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component in accordance with the above schedule;
GSO  to render a report  on Local  Road Network of the Municipality and  the Inventory Committee the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the municipality in accordance with the above schedule; and
Municipal Engineer  to provide the Local Accountant and GSO  with the complete description and cost  segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare all the documentary requirements in the  writing-off of the  dormant receivables totaling P312,735.83  and P22,500.00 in the GF and TF, respectively,  as stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005 and submit the same to the Audit Team to rid the books of  unreliable receivable  account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment  crediting the account Textbook and Instructional Materials Inventory for the cost  of books which were already issued based on the Acceptance Report submitted by the GSO;
the GSO to locate the books, NAT and NCAE from the concerned schools, issue the corresponding Acceptance Report and submit the report to the Accounting office to serve as basis in adjusting the inventory account; and
the GSO to determine the status or condition and whereabouts of the other Supplies and Materials Inventory account and prepare the necessary reports such as Acceptance Report, Acknowledgement Receipt, Waste Materials Report, Requisition and Issue Slips or other records to prove issuance  and submit the same to the Accounting Office for proper adjustment of the  inventory account.

We recommended  that the Municipal  Mayor instruct the GSO personnel to make full inventory of all the vehicles which are  not yet insured and cause the processing of their insurance from the GSIS. Likewise, provide adequate insurance coverage for all the government buildings in the GF and SEF to guarantee indemnification in case of occurrence of  catastrophic perils.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council to conduct feasibility of all the projects included in the 20 percent Development Fund to avoid delay in the implementation caused by site location and inaccurate program to maximize the implementation of projects funded out of the 20 percent Development Fund to ensure that the full intention of development would benefit all its constituents and carefully choose each project to be included in the planned program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to establish a GAD Database and Sex-disaggregated data as required under Section 4(b) of JMC No. 2013-01 of the DILG, DBM, NEDA and the PCW in order to create a well defined programs and projects that are best suited to the need of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the activities stated in the Gender and Development Plan in order to address the gender issues.  Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive processes, procedures are properly followed and other factors that would affect the implementation must be considered to avoid problems when it comes to implementation.  Likewise, utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate. Submit justification on why the above programs are considered GAD related activities by the GAD Focal Point Persons.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRO along with the other implementing agencies such as GSO, Engineering Office and BAC to closely monitor the undertaking of the proposed  projects funded out of LDRRMF so that projects, Programs and activities envisioned to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity for risk reduction and management be fully implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the Municipal  Engineer to ensure that relevant information required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 are posted in tarpaulin or similar materials to inform the public of the ongoing projects of the Municipality; and
remind other Department Heads on the posting to the appropriate signboards the programs/activities for implementation by the concerned department as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 and NBC No. 563 in the appropriation and payment of membership fees and annual dues to various leagues and ensure that payments of such kind to private organization shall not be charged against the government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop approving the payment of honoraria to Presidents of Barangay’s Senior Citizens and ERPAT association unless sufficient legal basis for the grant of honorarium has been submitted to the Audit Team for further evaluation, otherwise the payment of said honoraria may be disallowed in audit.

Recommendation:  We recommended that Management submit comments/ justifications relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies.  Likewise, the members of the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA No. 9184.

Recommendation:  We recommended that the Local Chief Executive address the pressing problem on personnel shortage by hiring qualified and working personnel who have the capability to implement the BUB programs on time.

Recommendation:  We recommended that Management submit comments/ justifications relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies in order to complete the conduct of technical review thereof.  Likewise, the members of the Bids and Awards Committee should adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA No. 9184.

Recommendation:   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sanggunian Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects covered by 20% Development Fund.  Further, consider the hiring of working personnel who have the capability to implement the programs, projects and activities incorporated in the yearly 20% Development Fund.

Recommendation:  We recommend that the Local Government create plantilla positions and hire full time personnel to be assigned at the Local DRRM Office pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

Recommendations:  In view hereof, we recommended that LSB submit legal basis justifying the utilization of MOOE in the amount of P371,818.20 for payment of honoraria of LSB teachers and non-formal educators.
Henceforth, evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

Recommendations:  We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should consider the specific standards and general objectives of internal controls in appointing a Municipal Administrator without sacrificing the available manpower at the Municipal Engineering Office.
Moreover, the Sangguniang Bayan may conduct inquiry on the status of implementation of the programs, projects and activities prioritized under the 20% Municipal Development Funds for calendar years 2012 to 2016.

Recommendations:  In view hereof, we recommended that Management consider the following measures:
1.	Submit comments relative to the above-enumerated deficiencies noted in the bidding process.  Likewise, the members of the Bids and Awards Committee should adhere strictly with the standards set forth in RA No. 9184.
2.	Initiate the recommendation to the GPPB the blacklisting of the Contractor without prejudice to the immediate refund of 15% mobilization expenses amounting to P585,000.00 from the Contractor plus liquidated damages pursuant to Section 68 of the Revised IRR, RA No. 9184.
