observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
1. Direct the Accounting Personnel to review and analyze prior years’ records on cash advances inorder to establish the details and status of the accounts
2. Serve a written notice and demand settlement of the outstanding cash advance as required under Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004
3. Direct the Accounting Personnel to make representation with PNB and inquire on the status of the unliquidated cash advance inorder to establish collectibility of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 informed the team that they are exerting their efforts to liquidate the cash advances. They sent letters to former officials and employees of the municipality but no reply was received. The Municipal Accountant claimed that she cannot reconcile the records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the ICO - Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that this was the usual practice for the past years in view of the absence of the required certified list of taxpayers, however, they had assured to implement the given recommendations. They further informed us that they are considering adoption of an application system which will provide automated solution for administration of real property taxes.

observations.recommendation: We have commended the efforts of the local government unit in conducting a complete physical count of its Property, Plant and Equipment and we have also recommended that the local government unit comply with the provisions of Section 379 of Republic Act 7160 on disposal of unserviceable properties in order not to mislead the readers on the actual condition of its properties.
observations.managementAction: Management has expressed willingness to comply with the given recommendations and abide by the existing laws, rules and regulations in the disposal of unserviceable government assets.

observations.recommendation:
To present fairly the amount of receivables in the financial statements, we have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the local government unit requests for write-off of the dormant receivable accounts from the COA through the following procedures:

The Municipal Accountant prepares a recommendation addressed to the Local Chief Executive supported with the documents provided by COA Circular No. 97-001.

Upon approval of the recommendation, the Local Chief Executive forwards the approved recommendation for write-off of dormant receivable accounts to the Sangguniang Bayan for the adoption of a resolution.

The Sangguniang Bayan forwards the duly approved SB Resolution and supporting documents to the Local Chief Executive.

The Local Chief Executive prepares the request for write-off based on the duly signed SB Resolution and supporting documents and forwards the same to the Commission on Audit for review and for approval.
observations.managementAction: Management is in process of locating records and validating the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have commended the local government unit for its utmost efforts in causing the liquidation of long outstanding cash advances and we have also recommended that the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to granting and liquidation of cash advances be strictly observed. We have further recommended that the officials and employees concerned be required to liquidate/settle immediately their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Long outstanding cash advances were liquidated as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accounting Unit record the purchase of all supplies and materials as inventory following the perpetual inventory method. Issuances thereof be recorded as they take place using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) which shall be submitted by the General Service Officer or Local Treasurer as the case maybe.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were amenable to the observations and agreed to comply with the given recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact his collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations to avoid the risk of loss or improper utilization of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Collections were deposited with the authorized depository bank

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC - Municipal Engineer to use five percent of the sum of the Estimated Direct Cost, Overhead, Contingencies and Miscellaneous (OCM) and Contractor’s Profit (CP) as VAT component in the computation of indirect costs of infrastructure project. Although the Engineering and Administrative Overhead are expenses attributed to the project, the same should not be included in the computation of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC).

Since the ABC is the ceiling for acceptable bid prices, we have further recommended that the guidelines provided in Department Order No. 22 dated February 18, 2015 of DPWH in the preparation of ABC be strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: Five percent of the sum of the Estimated Direct Cost, Overhead, Contingencies and Miscellaneous (OCM) and Contractor’s Profit (CP) was used as VAT component in the computation of indirect costs of infrastructure project.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management ensure that the projects/programs designed under the 20% Development Fund are implemented fully within the time frame. Optimal utilization of the 20% of the IRA is also recommended in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents which is essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.
observations.managementAction: Management has explained that some of the projects programmed under the 20% Development Fund were not implemented because some of the National Government Agencies has already constructed classrooms or infrastructures on the same location. Nevertheless, they gave assurance to comply with the given recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the local government unit observe the procedural guidelines under GPPB Resolution No. 09-2009 dated November 23, 2009 in all procurements through shopping and small-value procurement to ensure that prices obtained are advantageous to the government.

We have further recommended that all provisions of laws, rules and regulations pertaining to procurements be strictly adhered to in order to protect the interest of the local government unit at all times.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has instructed the employees concerned to comply with the guidelines on procurement through shopping and small-value procurement.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the General Service Officer or Municipal Treasurer to comply with the requirements in the maintenance of Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) as required by the existing rules and regulations on invoice and receipt of transfer of property to readily identify and establish the accountability and responsibility of officers and employees to whom government properties were issued for official use. Renewal of PAR should also be made every three years or every time there is a change in accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has signified his willingness to comply with the given recommendations. and has provided PAR on some purchases of equipment.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the local government unit continue its efforts in intensifying collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the collection of unpaid local taxes. This will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management has informed us that the Treasury and Assessment personnel had conducted tax campaigns.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the unexpended balance of PDAF and DAP totaling P1,930,000.00 be returned to the Bureau of Treasury so that the funds could be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and augment future legitimate funding requirement of the government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has instructed the Municipal Treasurer and the ICO – Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary documents to facilitate the refund of the unexpended balances to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the given recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book. Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 prescribing accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was further recommended.

The following entries were suggested to record the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:

observations.managementAction: Management agreed and committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  instruct  the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements for the months of October to December 2015, and every month thereafter, to the auditor for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: October to December 2015 BRS were already submitted in March 2016. However, the current year monthly BRS were up to September 2016.
observations.reason: Late receipt of bank statements from the depository bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management maintain deposits only to authorized government depository banks in accordance with Department Order No. 27-05 of the Department of Finance and to those banks with authority from the Monetary Board of BSP and DOF; and seek approval from the DOF if maintaining deposits with the current bank is  a practical option.
observations.reason: To date, the Municipal Treasurer  has already secured a resolution from the SB allowing the closure of the PNB Eastgate, San Fernando Branch to the LBP Apalit Branch, an authorized government depository bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to  deposit all collections regularly and intact to the authorized government depository banks as required in Section 69 of P.D. 1445 and other existing regulations.
observations.reason: There was still delay in the deposit of collections. However, no huge amounts of collections were retained in the Municipal Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the information and publicity on the PPAs of the Government Agencies to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people and to enable the Audit Team to comply with the submission of the Quarterly Report of the PPAs to the Regional Director for monitoring compliance.
observations.reason: Quarterly reports on PPAs were already submitted to COA by the agency. However, it was observed that some municipal vehicles were printed with the name of elective officials which is contrary to rules and regulations on PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Development Council:

 (a) continue to propose high-impact programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund; and 

(b) exclude from the 20% Development Fund programs and activities that are mostly in the nature of maintenance and other operating expenses that do not bring about long-term benefits to the community consistent with the guidelines under Joint Memorandum Circular No, 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.reason:
The LGU has purchased a lot for the  MRF to accommodate waste disposal of seven barangays

The ten-year SWMP is on-going. However, municipal ordinances were issued to apprehend violators on waste disposal

Only a few barangays have adopted this policy

Some barangays have conducted awareness campaigns on solid waste but the residents lacked diligent efforts to comply.

Day care centers, roads and bridges and canals were constructed.

Lack of proper consultation with the Local Development Council prior to the preparation of the Annual Investment  Plan did not permit the LGU to include all capital investment projects under the 20% 
Development 
Fund.  However, most of the projects under the 20% DF are capital in nature while some should be included the MOOE budgetary appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

(a) submit a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF every 15th day after the end of each month to the COA auditor as required in COA Circular No. 2012.002; and 

(b) direct the Accounting and Treasury Departments to comply with Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by transferring the unexpended balance of P987,651.57 to the special trust fund created solely to support risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality for the next five years.
observations.reason:
Lack of familiarity of the LDRRO on the regulations on the submission of the report on utilization of LDRRM funds.

Last year’s unexpended balance of the Calamity fund was transferred.to a special trust fund account. To date, CY 2016 was not yet transferred to the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management set aside at least 1% of its budget for the plans, programs, project and activities for senior citizens and differently-abled persons pursuant to Section 2 of DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 2003-1 dated April 28, 2003.
observations.reason: Management failed to increase the allocated 1% for the OSCA and PWDs because other LGU budgetary appropriations were given more priorities in funding their projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Create the Inventory Committee who should  conduct the complete physical count of all PPE; after the inventory taking,  prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) furnishing one copy to the Auditor; reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting records; prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) duly accomplished and approved by officials concerned to be forwarded to the Auditor concerned for inspection and appraisal of the COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS) and upon receipt of the inspection and appraisal report from COA TAS
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical count of all PPE by Office and submit the same to the Audit Team. However, the Inventory Report was not consolidated and not reconciled with accounting records.
observations.reason: Although they conducted inventory taking, the same was not reconciled with accounting records. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No.  1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible for the year as basis for the recording of actual RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year.  Prepare the schedule of RPT and SET Receivables as of year – end and reconcile the accounts with the general ledger balances to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the updated list of delinquent taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant during the year to adjust the recorded RPT and SET Receivables and reflect the actual collectibles. At the beginning of CY 2017, the List of Taxpayers with corresponding tax due and collectible will now be the basis of recording by the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

General Services Officer-ICO/Supply Officer to conduct immediately the physical count of Textbook and Instructional Materials Inventory, Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory and Other Supplies Inventory with total amount of P23,725,249.62 to determine the existence of the inventory items and reconcile the results with the accounting records to ascertain whether the balance of these inventory accounts per general ledger is correct. If discrepancies were noted, the GSO/Supply Officer prepare the proper documentation. Henceforth, regularly conduct the physical count of inventory items by type and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) which should be reconciled with the accounting records to determine accuracy of both records

Municipal Accountant to draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) dropping off from the books the cost of Inventories issued/consumed but still recorded based on the documents provided by the GSO as a result of the inventory taking and the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued.
observations.managementAction: The GSO has already submitted the list of recipients of Textbooks and Instructional Materials to the Municipal Accountant thus the balance was immensely reduced to P1,572,038.00 as of year-end.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No.  5

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on receivables and their corresponding payables in the books of the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant  organized a series of meeting  with the Provincial Accountant  and Provincial Treasurer to establish and align the process of documentation and recording of  the accounts Due to /Due from LGUs. As of December 31, 2016, the account Due from LGU (Province) was already reconciled but the account  Due to LGU has an unreconciled balance of P359,527.67.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, the balances were fully reconciled
as of March 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: 

Local School Board to stop allocating budget for the above expenses not listed as among the priorities to be charged against the Special Education Fund.

Municipal Accountant and other concerned Municipal Officials to stop payment of similar expenses, which do not fall within the purpose for which the Fund was created. Also, submit the lacking documents and ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with all the required documentations.  Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Local School Board has already stop the allocation of budget for the expenses not listed in the allowed expenditures.

The Municipal Accountant has already submitted the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to implement programs that are disaster responsive and adhere to the goals of the Municipality in terms of disaster preparedness and prevention. See to it that they are aligned to the priority projects and activities stated under the JMC 2013-01 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor  already instructed the MDRRMO to focus the implementation of programs that are responsive and adhere to the goals of the Municipality in terms of  disaster preparedness and prevention. The Municipal Accountant assured that payment of similar expenses will no longer be allowed but only those for disaster preparedness and prevention. The documents that are lacking were already submitted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II Observation No. 10

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant to stop payment of similar expenses, which do not fall within the purpose for which the fund was created. Also, submit the lacking documents and should ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with all the required documentations.  Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular 2012-001 and R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assured that payment of similar expenses will no longer be allowed but only those for disaster preparedness and prevention. The documents that are lacking were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning Officer and the GAD Focal Point Persons to cause the early completion of GAD Database and Sex-disaggregated data as required under the Section 4(b) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the DILG, DBM, NEDA and the PCW in order to create a well-defined programs and projects that are best suited to the needs of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already instructed the MPDC and GAD Focal Point Person to cause the preparation of the GAD Data Base. According to the GAD Focal Person, the Data Base is already for consolidation and will be operational in CY 2017 and be used as basis  in preparing GAD plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 8

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the activities stated in the Gender and Development Plan in order to address the gender issues. Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive processes, procedures are properly followed and other factors that would affect the        implementation  must    be considered to avoid problems when it comes to implementation. Likewise, utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: Included in the instruction of the Municipal Mayor was the proper monitoring and the status of implementation of all the GAD related activities.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer, the Engineer and other officers concerned to submit Purchase Orders, executed contracts and request for inspection report to the auditor within the prescribed period specified in COA Circular 2009-001 to allow review and evaluation of the contract as well as proper inspection of the projects procured.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already submitted the POs/Contracts and Request for Inspection to the Office of the Auditor after the receipt of the AOM.
